1	/*
2	 * grid.odin — 256×256 world grid (M3).
3	 *
4	 * Each cell stores a terrain type, occupant character id (0 = empty),
5	 * and a generic object id (0 = none).
6	 *
7	 * Terrain zones and their walkability:
8	 *   .Water  — impassable
9	 *   .Beach  — walkable, spawnable
10	 *   .Grass  — walkable, spawnable
11	 *   .Town   — walkable, spawnable (preferred spawn)
12	 *   .Forest — walkable, not spawnable
13	 */
14	
15	package dmls
16	
17	import rl "vendor:raylib"
18	import "core:math"
19	
20	GRID_W :: 256
21	GRID_H :: 256
22	
23	// Terrain scale: smaller = more zoomed-out noise features.
24	TERRAIN_SCALE :: 0.035
25	TERRAIN_OCTAVES :: 5
26	
27	Terrain :: enum u8 {
28	    Water,
29	    Beach,
30	    Grass,
31	    Town,
32	    Forest,
33	}
34	
35	Cell :: struct {
36	    terrain   : Terrain,
37	    occupant  : u32,    // character id, 0 = empty
38	    object_id : u32,    // reserved for Phase 8 objects
39	}
40	
41	Grid :: struct {
42	    cells     : [GRID_H][GRID_W]Cell,
43	    world_seed: u64,
44	}
45	
46	// ---- Terrain colour lookup -------------------------------------------------
47	
48	terrain_color :: proc(t: Terrain) -> rl.Color {
49	    switch t {
50	    case .Water:  return rl.Color{30,  80,  160, 255}
51	    case .Beach:  return rl.Color{210, 190, 130, 255}
52	    case .Grass:  return rl.Color{60,  140, 60,  255}
53	    case .Town:   return rl.Color{160, 140, 110, 255}
54	    case .Forest: return rl.Color{30,  90,  30,  255}
55	    }
56	    return rl.Color{0, 0, 0, 255}
57	}
58	
59	terrain_walkable :: proc(t: Terrain) -> bool {
60	    return t != .Water
61	}
62	
63	terrain_spawnable :: proc(t: Terrain) -> bool {
64	    return t == .Grass || t == .Town || t == .Beach
65	}
66	
67	// ---- Grid generation -------------------------------------------------------
68	
69	grid_generate :: proc(g: ^Grid, seed: u64) {
70	    g.world_seed = seed
71	    g^ = {}
72	    g.world_seed = seed
73	
74	    ns: NoiseState
75	    noise_init(&ns, seed)
76	
77	    // Second noise for town/forest layer
78	    ns2: NoiseState
79	    noise_init(&ns2, seed ~ 0xdeadbeefcafe1234)
80	
81	    for row in 0..<GRID_H {
82	        for col in 0..<GRID_W {
83	            x := f64(col)
84	            y := f64(row)
85	
86	            // Island mask: distance from centre falls off to water at edges
87	            cx  := f64(GRID_W) / 2.0
88	            cy  := f64(GRID_H) / 2.0
89	            dx  := (x - cx) / cx
90	            dy  := (y - cy) / cy
91	            dist := math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy)
92	
93	            // Base elevation from fBm noise
94	            elevation := fbm(&ns, x * TERRAIN_SCALE, y * TERRAIN_SCALE, TERRAIN_OCTAVES)
95	            // Clamp to [-1,1] roughly — fBm with 5 octaves goes beyond 1
96	            elevation = elevation * 0.5
97	
98	            // Apply island radial mask — edges become water
99	            elevation -= dist * 1.2
100	
101	            // Secondary noise for town/forest differentiation
102	            detail := perlin2(&ns2, x * TERRAIN_SCALE * 2, y * TERRAIN_SCALE * 2)
103	
104	            terrain: Terrain
105	            switch {
106	            case elevation < -0.3:
107	                terrain = .Water
108	            case elevation < -0.1:
109	                terrain = .Beach
110	            case elevation < 0.25:
111	                // Grass vs town vs forest based on detail noise
112	                if detail > 0.3 {
113	                    terrain = .Town
114	                } else if detail < -0.3 {
115	                    terrain = .Forest
116	                } else {
117	                    terrain = .Grass
118	                }
119	            case:
120	                terrain = .Forest   // high ground = dense forest
121	            }
122	
123	            g.cells[row][col].terrain = terrain
124	        }
125	    }
126	}
127	
128	// ---- Occupant helpers ------------------------------------------------------
129	
130	grid_place_character :: proc(g: ^Grid, id: u32, col, row: i32) -> bool {
131	    if col < 0 || col >= GRID_W || row < 0 || row >= GRID_H do return false
132	    cell := &g.cells[row][col]
133	    if cell.occupant != 0 do return false
134	    if !terrain_walkable(cell.terrain) do return false
135	    cell.occupant = id
136	    return true
137	}
138	
139	grid_move_character :: proc(g: ^Grid, id: u32, old_col, old_row, new_col, new_row: i32) -> bool {
140	    if new_col < 0 || new_col >= GRID_W || new_row < 0 || new_row >= GRID_H do return false
141	    dst := &g.cells[new_row][new_col]
142	    if dst.occupant != 0 do return false
143	    if !terrain_walkable(dst.terrain) do return false
144	    g.cells[old_row][old_col].occupant = 0
145	    dst.occupant = id
146	    return true
147	}
148	
149	grid_remove_character :: proc(g: ^Grid, col, row: i32) {
150	    if col >= 0 && col < GRID_W && row >= 0 && row < GRID_H {
151	        g.cells[row][col].occupant = 0
152	    }
153	}
154	
155	// ---- Spawn: find a random spawnable cell using a simple LCG ----------------
156	
157	grid_find_spawn :: proc(g: ^Grid, rng: ^u64) -> (col, row: i32, ok: bool) {
158	    for _ in 0..<512 {
159	        rng^ = rng^ * 6364136223846793005 + 1442695040888963407
160	        c := i32((rng^ >> 33) % GRID_W)
161	        r := i32((rng^ >> 17) % GRID_H)
162	        cell := &g.cells[r][c]
163	        if terrain_spawnable(cell.terrain) && cell.occupant == 0 {
164	            return c, r, true
165	        }
166	    }
167	    return 0, 0, false
168	}
169	
170	// ---- Rendering -------------------------------------------------------------
171	
172	// Render the grid into the current drawing context.
173	// origin_x/y: top-left pixel of the simulation view.
174	// view_w/h:   pixel size of the simulation panel.
175	// cell_px:    pixel size per grid cell (use render_cell_px() from screen_cfg).
176	grid_render :: proc(g: ^Grid, origin_x, origin_y, view_w, view_h: i32,
177	                    cam_col, cam_row: i32, cell_px: i32) {
178	    cpx := max(cell_px, 1)
179	    visible_cols := view_w  / cpx + 2
180	    visible_rows := view_h / cpx + 2
181	
182	    start_col := cam_col - visible_cols / 2
183	    start_row := cam_row - visible_rows / 2
184	
185	    for r in 0..<visible_rows {
186	        for c in 0..<visible_cols {
187	            gc := start_col + c
188	            gr := start_row + r
189	            if gc < 0 || gc >= GRID_W || gr < 0 || gr >= GRID_H do continue
190	
191	            cell  := &g.cells[gr][gc]
192	            color := terrain_color(cell.terrain)
193	
194	            px := origin_x + c * cpx
195	            py := origin_y + r * cpx
196	            rl.DrawRectangle(px, py, cpx, cpx, color)
197	
198	            // Draw occupant dot
199	            if cell.occupant != 0 {
200	                dot_x := px + cpx / 2
201	                dot_y := py + cpx / 2
202	                rl.DrawPixel(dot_x, dot_y, rl.WHITE)
203	            }
204	        }
205	    }
206	}
207

1	/*
2	 * starter_chars.odin — Pre-programmed starter characters (CHARACTERS.MD).
3	 *
4	 * Four characters are asserted into Epilog and placed on the grid at world
5	 * initialisation, before the player adds anyone via the creation screen.
6	 *
7	 * Initialisation order follows CHARACTERS.MD §Initialisation Order:
8	 *   1. character/2
9	 *   2. gender/2, dating_pref/2, pronouns/2, birthday/3, age/2
10	 *   3. personality_trait/2
11	 *   4. real_world_family/2
12	 *   5. nickname_for/3
13	 *   6. starting relationship facts (crush, friend, acquaintance, stranger, sibling)
14	 *   7. derived rules (see below — replace engine rules loaded at startup)
15	 *   8-10. interaction_count priming
16	 *
17	 * Predicate names used here follow CHARACTERS.MD exactly:
18	 *   crush/2, friend/2, acquaintance/2, sibling/2, stranger/2
19	 * The derived rules in CHARACTERS.MD use crush/2; the sim also queries
20	 * crushing/2 (aliased below: crushing(X,Y) :- crush(X,Y).).
21	 */
22	
23	package dmls
24	
25	import "core:fmt"
26	import "core:strings"
27	
28	// ---- Character IDs (fixed, reserved) ---------------------------------------
29	
30	STARTER_ID_CHARACTER_A :: u32(1)
31	STARTER_ID_URIEL     :: u32(2)
32	STARTER_ID_ANNA      :: u32(3)
33	STARTER_ID_SALONI    :: u32(4)
34	STARTER_COUNT        :: 4
35	
36	// ---- Helper: load a single fact string into Epilog -------------------------
37	
38	_sl :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, fact: string) {
39	    buf: [512]u8
40	    s := fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "%s.", fact)
41	    buf[len(s)] = 0
42	    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf[:])))
43	}
44	
45	// ---- Helper: build a Character struct from literal values ------------------
46	
47	_make_char :: proc(
48	    id         : u32,
49	    name       : string,
50	    nickname   : string,
51	    age        : u8,
52	    gender     : Gender,
53	    pref       : DatingPref,
54	    pronouns   : Pronouns,
55	    personality: Personality,
56	    sprite     : u32,
57	) -> Character {
58	    c: Character
59	    c.id          = id
60	    c.age         = age
61	    c.gender      = gender
62	    c.dating_pref = pref
63	    c.pronouns    = pronouns
64	    c.personality = personality
65	    c.sprite      = sprite
66	    n := min(len(name), MAX_NAME_LEN - 1)
67	    copy(c.name[:], name[:n])
68	    if len(nickname) > 0 {
69	        nn := min(len(nickname), MAX_NAME_LEN - 1)
70	        copy(c.nickname[:], nickname[:nn])
71	    }
72	    return c
73	}
74	
75	// ---- Assert all facts for one starter character ----------------------------
76	
77	_assert_starter_facts :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine,
78	    atom      : string,
79	    personality_a : string,
80	    gender_a  : string,
81	    pref_a    : string,
82	    pronouns_a: string,
83	    bday_m, bday_d: int,
84	    age_v     : int,
85	    traits    : []string,
86	    rwf       : string,
87	) {
88	    buf: [256]u8
89	
90	    // 1. character/2
91	    s := fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "character(%s, %s)", atom, personality_a)
92	    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
93	
94	    // 2. gender, dating_pref, pronouns, birthday, age
95	    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "gender(%s, %s)", atom, gender_a)
96	    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
97	
98	    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "dating_pref(%s, %s)", atom, pref_a)
99	    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
100	
101	    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "pronouns(%s, %s)", atom, pronouns_a)
102	    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
103	
104	    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "birthday(%s, %d, %d)", atom, bday_m, bday_d)
105	    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
106	
107	    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "age(%s, %d)", atom, age_v)
108	    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
109	
110	    // 3. personality_trait/2
111	    for t in traits {
112	        s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "personality_trait(%s, %s)", atom, t)
113	        _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
114	    }
115	
116	    // 4. real_world_family/2
117	    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "real_world_family(%s, %s)", atom, rwf)
118	    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
119	}
120	
121	// ---- Load CHARACTERS.MD derived rules (replaces engine defaults) -----------
122	
123	starter_load_rules :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine) {
124	    // crush/2 alias → crushing/2 so sim tick queries work
125	    epilog_load_string(engine,
126	        "crushing(X, Y) :- crush(X, Y).")
127	
128	    // rivals: two characters crushing on the same target
129	    epilog_load_string(engine,
130	        "rivals(X, Y) :- crush(X, Z), crush(Y, Z), X \\= Y.")
131	
132	    // can_crush: eligible to develop a crush
133	    epilog_load_string(engine,
134	        "can_crush(X, Y) :- dating_pref(X, Pref), gender(Y, Pref), \\+ sibling(X, Y), \\+ partner(X, _), X \\= Y.")
135	    epilog_load_string(engine,
136	        "can_crush(X, Y) :- dating_pref(X, any), \\+ sibling(X, Y), \\+ partner(X, _), X \\= Y.")
137	
138	    // eligible/2 used by sim_tick → delegates to can_crush
139	    epilog_load_string(engine,
140	        "eligible(X, Y) :- can_crush(X, Y).")
141	
142	    // mood derivation
143	    epilog_load_string(engine,
144	        "mood(X, jealous)    :- rivals(X, _).")
145	    epilog_load_string(engine,
146	        "mood(X, lovestruck) :- crush(X, _), \\+ rivals(X, _).")
147	    epilog_load_string(engine,
148	        "mood(X, content)    :- \\+ crush(X, _), \\+ rivals(X, _).")
149	
150	    // great_friends (richer version using friend/2 + interaction_count)
151	    epilog_load_string(engine,
152	        "great_friends(X, Y) :- friend(X, Y), friend(Y, X), interaction_count(X, Y, N), N > 10.")
153	
154	    // best_friends alias
155	    epilog_load_string(engine,
156	        "best_friends(X, Y) :- great_friends(X, Y).")
157	
158	    // unrequited
159	    epilog_load_string(engine,
160	        "unrequited(X, Y) :- crush(X, Y), \\+ crush(Y, X).")
161	
162	    // sibling rule: siblings cannot develop crushes on each other
163	    epilog_load_string(engine,
164	        "\\+ can_crush(X, Y) :- sibling(X, Y).")
165	}
166	
167	// ---- Main entry point: seed all 4 characters --------------------------------
168	
169	// Returns the next available ID after the starters (i.e. STARTER_COUNT + 1).
170	starters_seed :: proc(res: ^Residents, world: ^World, engine: ^EpilogEngine) {
171	    // ---- Build Character structs -------------------------------------------
172	
173	    deb := _make_char(STARTER_ID_CHARACTER_A,
174	        "[NAME]", "",
175	        20, .Male, .Any, .He_Him, .Ambitious_Achiever, 0)
176	
177	    uriel := _make_char(STARTER_ID_URIEL,
178	        "Uriel", "",
179	        21, .Female, .Any, .She_Her, .Outgoing_Merrymaker, 1)
180	
181	    anna := _make_char(STARTER_ID_ANNA,
182	        "Anna Croft", "sunflower",
183	        21, .Female, .Male, .She_Her, .Outgoing_Dynamo, 2)
184	
185	    saloni := _make_char(STARTER_ID_SALONI,
186	        "Saloni", "",
187	        20, .Female, .Male, .She_Her, .Outgoing_Merrymaker, 3)
188	
189	    // ---- Insert into residents (preserving fixed IDs) ----------------------
190	
191	    chars := [STARTER_COUNT]Character{deb, uriel, anna, saloni}
192	    for i in 0..<STARTER_COUNT {
193	        idx := res.count
194	        res.chars[idx] = chars[i]
195	        res.count += 1
196	    }
197	    res.next_id = STARTER_COUNT + 1
198	
199	    // ---- Assert Epilog facts (initialisation order) ------------------------
200	
201	    // [NAME]
202	    _assert_starter_facts(engine, "character_a",
203	        "ambitious_achiever", "male", "any", "he_him",
204	        6, 15, 20,
205	        []string{"confident", "straight_talker", "results_driven"},
206	        "yes")
207	
208	    // Uriel
209	    _assert_starter_facts(engine, "uriel",
210	        "outgoing_merrymaker", "female", "any", "she_her",
211	        6, 20, 21,
212	        []string{"cheerful", "infectiously_positive", "socially_confident"},
213	        "yes")
214	
215	    // Anna Croft
216	    _assert_starter_facts(engine, "anna_croft",
217	        "outgoing_dynamo", "female", "male", "she_her",
218	        7, 12, 21,
219	        []string{"energetic", "driven", "action_oriented"},
220	        "no")
221	
222	    // Saloni
223	    _assert_starter_facts(engine, "saloni",
224	        "outgoing_merrymaker", "female", "male", "she_her",
225	        7, 18, 20,
226	        []string{"cheerful", "infectiously_positive", "socially_confident"},
227	        "yes")
228	
229	    // 5. Nicknames
230	    _sl(engine, "nickname_for(anna_croft, character_a, sunflower)")
231	    _sl(engine, "nickname_for(character_a, anna_croft, my_demon)")
232	
233	    // 6. Starting relationships
234	    //    [NAME]
235	    _sl(engine, "sibling(character_a, saloni)")
236	    _sl(engine, "sibling(saloni, character_a)")
237	    _sl(engine, "crush(character_a, uriel)")
238	    _sl(engine, "friend(character_a, uriel)")
239	    _sl(engine, "friend(character_a, saloni)")
240	    _sl(engine, "acquaintance(character_a, anna_croft)")
241	    //    Also load as relationship/3 for the residents screen queries
242	    _sl(engine, "relationship(character_a, saloni, sibling)")
243	    _sl(engine, "relationship(character_a, uriel, friend)")
244	    _sl(engine, "relationship(character_a, anna_croft, acquaintance)")
245	
246	    //    Uriel
247	    _sl(engine, "crush(uriel, character_a)")
248	    _sl(engine, "friend(uriel, character_a)")
249	    _sl(engine, "acquaintance(uriel, saloni)")
250	    _sl(engine, "stranger(uriel, anna_croft)")
251	    _sl(engine, "relationship(uriel, character_a, friend)")
252	    _sl(engine, "relationship(uriel, saloni, acquaintance)")
253	
254	    //    Anna Croft
255	    _sl(engine, "acquaintance(anna_croft, character_a)")
256	    _sl(engine, "stranger(anna_croft, saloni)")
257	    _sl(engine, "stranger(anna_croft, uriel)")
258	    _sl(engine, "relationship(anna_croft, character_a, acquaintance)")
259	
260	    //    Saloni
261	    _sl(engine, "friend(saloni, character_a)")
262	    _sl(engine, "acquaintance(saloni, uriel)")
263	    _sl(engine, "stranger(saloni, anna_croft)")
264	    _sl(engine, "relationship(saloni, character_a, friend)")
265	    _sl(engine, "relationship(saloni, character_a, sibling)")
266	    _sl(engine, "relationship(saloni, uriel, acquaintance)")
267	
268	    // 8-10. Primed interaction counts
269	    _sl(engine, "interaction_count(character_a, uriel, 5)")
270	    _sl(engine, "interaction_count(character_a, saloni, 20)")
271	    _sl(engine, "interaction_count(character_a, anna_croft, 2)")
272	
273	    // ---- Spawn on grid -----------------------------------------------------
274	
275	    // Starters spawn in their designated zones.
276	    // We try the preferred zone first; grid_find_spawn falls back automatically.
277	    _spawn_in_zone(world, res, STARTER_ID_CHARACTER_A, .Town)
278	    _spawn_in_zone(world, res, STARTER_ID_URIEL,     .Beach)
279	    _spawn_in_zone(world, res, STARTER_ID_ANNA,      .Grass)
280	    _spawn_in_zone(world, res, STARTER_ID_SALONI,    .Town)
281	}
282	
283	// Attempt to spawn a character in a preferred terrain zone.
284	// Falls back to any spawnable cell if the preferred zone is not found quickly.
285	_spawn_in_zone :: proc(world: ^World, res: ^Residents, id: u32, preferred: Terrain) {
286	    c := residents_find(res, id)
287	    if c == nil do return
288	
289	    // Try up to 256 times to find a cell of the preferred terrain.
290	    for _ in 0..<256 {
291	        world.rng = world.rng * 6364136223846793005 + 1442695040888963407
292	        col := i32((world.rng >> 33) % GRID_W)
293	        world.rng = world.rng * 6364136223846793005 + 1442695040888963407
294	        row := i32((world.rng >> 17) % GRID_H)
295	        cell := &world.grid.cells[row][col]
296	        if cell.terrain == preferred && cell.occupant == 0 {
297	            if grid_place_character(&world.grid, id, col, row) {
298	                c.position = {col, row}
299	                idx := int(id - 1)
300	                if idx < MAX_RESIDENTS { world.positions[idx] = c.position }
301	                return
302	            }
303	        }
304	    }
305	
306	    // Fallback: any spawnable cell
307	    col, row, ok := grid_find_spawn(&world.grid, &world.rng)
308	    if ok {
309	        grid_place_character(&world.grid, id, col, row)
310	        c.position = {col, row}
311	        idx := int(id - 1)
312	        if idx < MAX_RESIDENTS { world.positions[idx] = c.position }
313	    }
314	}
315

file.content:
/*
 * starter_chars.odin — Pre-programmed starter characters (CHARACTERS.MD).
 *
 * Four characters are asserted into Epilog and placed on the grid at world
 * initialisation, before the player adds anyone via the creation screen.
 *
 * Initialisation order follows CHARACTERS.MD §Initialisation Order:
 *   1. character/2
 *   2. gender/2, dating_pref/2, pronouns/2, birthday/3, age/2
 *   3. personality_trait/2
 *   4. real_world_family/2
 *   5. nickname_for/3
 *   6. starting relationship facts (crush, friend, acquaintance, stranger, sibling)
 *   7. derived rules (see below — replace engine rules loaded at startup)
 *   8-10. interaction_count priming
 *
 * Predicate names used here follow CHARACTERS.MD exactly:
 *   crush/2, friend/2, acquaintance/2, sibling/2, stranger/2
 * The derived rules in CHARACTERS.MD use crush/2; the sim also queries
 * crushing/2 (aliased below: crushing(X,Y) :- crush(X,Y).).
 */

package dmls

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strings"

// ---- Character IDs (fixed, reserved) ---------------------------------------

STARTER_ID_CHARACTER_A :: u32(1)
STARTER_ID_URIEL     :: u32(2)
STARTER_ID_ANNA      :: u32(3)
STARTER_ID_SALONI    :: u32(4)
STARTER_COUNT        :: 4

// ---- Helper: load a single fact string into Epilog -------------------------

_sl :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, fact: string) {
    buf: [512]u8
    s := fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "%s.", fact)
    buf[len(s)] = 0
    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf[:])))
}

// ---- Helper: build a Character struct from literal values ------------------

_make_char :: proc(
    id         : u32,
    name       : string,
    nickname   : string,
    age        : u8,
    gender     : Gender,
    pref       : DatingPref,
    pronouns   : Pronouns,
    personality: Personality,
    sprite     : u32,
) -> Character {
    c: Character
    c.id          = id
    c.age         = age
    c.gender      = gender
    c.dating_pref = pref
    c.pronouns    = pronouns
    c.personality = personality
    c.sprite      = sprite
    n := min(len(name), MAX_NAME_LEN - 1)
    copy(c.name[:], name[:n])
    if len(nickname) > 0 {
        nn := min(len(nickname), MAX_NAME_LEN - 1)
        copy(c.nickname[:], nickname[:nn])
    }
    return c
}

// ---- Assert all facts for one starter character ----------------------------

_assert_starter_facts :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine,
    atom      : string,
    personality_a : string,
    gender_a  : string,
    pref_a    : string,
    pronouns_a: string,
    bday_m, bday_d: int,
    age_v     : int,
    traits    : []string,
    rwf       : string,
) {
    buf: [256]u8

    // 1. character/2
    s := fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "character(%s, %s)", atom, personality_a)
    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))

    // 2. gender, dating_pref, pronouns, birthday, age
    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "gender(%s, %s)", atom, gender_a)
    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))

    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "dating_pref(%s, %s)", atom, pref_a)
    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))

    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "pronouns(%s, %s)", atom, pronouns_a)
    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))

    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "birthday(%s, %d, %d)", atom, bday_m, bday_d)
    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))

    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "age(%s, %d)", atom, age_v)
    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))

    // 3. personality_trait/2
    for t in traits {
        s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "personality_trait(%s, %s)", atom, t)
        _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
    }

    // 4. real_world_family/2
    s = fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "real_world_family(%s, %s)", atom, rwf)
    _sl(engine, string(buf[:len(s)]))
}

// ---- Load CHARACTERS.MD derived rules (replaces engine defaults) -----------

starter_load_rules :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine) {
    // crush/2 alias → crushing/2 so sim tick queries work
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "crushing(X, Y) :- crush(X, Y).")

    // rivals: two characters crushing on the same target
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "rivals(X, Y) :- crush(X, Z), crush(Y, Z), X \\= Y.")

    // can_crush: eligible to develop a crush
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "can_crush(X, Y) :- dating_pref(X, Pref), gender(Y, Pref), \\+ sibling(X, Y), \\+ partner(X, _), X \\= Y.")
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "can_crush(X, Y) :- dating_pref(X, any), \\+ sibling(X, Y), \\+ partner(X, _), X \\= Y.")

    // eligible/2 used by sim_tick → delegates to can_crush
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "eligible(X, Y) :- can_crush(X, Y).")

    // mood derivation
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "mood(X, jealous)    :- rivals(X, _).")
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "mood(X, lovestruck) :- crush(X, _), \\+ rivals(X, _).")
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "mood(X, content)    :- \\+ crush(X, _), \\+ rivals(X, _).")

    // great_friends (richer version using friend/2 + interaction_count)
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "great_friends(X, Y) :- friend(X, Y), friend(Y, X), interaction_count(X, Y, N), N > 10.")

    // best_friends alias
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "best_friends(X, Y) :- great_friends(X, Y).")

    // unrequited
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "unrequited(X, Y) :- crush(X, Y), \\+ crush(Y, X).")

    // sibling rule: siblings cannot develop crushes on each other
    epilog_load_string(engine,
        "\\+ can_crush(X, Y) :- sibling(X, Y).")
}

// ---- Main entry point: seed all 4 characters --------------------------------

// Returns the next available ID after the starters (i.e. STARTER_COUNT + 1).
starters_seed :: proc(res: ^Residents, world: ^World, engine: ^EpilogEngine) {
    // ---- Build Character structs -------------------------------------------

    deb := _make_char(STARTER_ID_CHARACTER_A,
        "[NAME]", "",
        20, .Male, .Any, .He_Him, .Ambitious_Achiever, 0)

    uriel := _make_char(STARTER_ID_URIEL,
        "Uriel", "",
        21, .Female, .Any, .She_Her, .Outgoing_Merrymaker, 1)

    anna := _make_char(STARTER_ID_ANNA,
        "Anna Croft", "sunflower",
        21, .Female, .Male, .She_Her, .Outgoing_Dynamo, 2)

    saloni := _make_char(STARTER_ID_SALONI,
        "Saloni", "",
        20, .Female, .Male, .She_Her, .Outgoing_Merrymaker, 3)

    // ---- Insert into residents (preserving fixed IDs) ----------------------

    chars := [STARTER_COUNT]Character{deb, uriel, anna, saloni}
    for i in 0..<STARTER_COUNT {
        idx := res.count
        res.chars[idx] = chars[i]
        res.count += 1
    }
    res.next_id = STARTER_COUNT + 1

    // ---- Assert Epilog facts (initialisation order) ------------------------

    // [NAME]
    _assert_starter_facts(engine, "character_a",
        "ambitious_achiever", "male", "any", "he_him",
        6, 15, 20,
        []string{"confident", "straight_talker", "results_driven"},
        "yes")

    // Uriel
    _assert_starter_facts(engine, "uriel",
        "outgoing_merrymaker", "female", "any", "she_her",
        6, 20, 21,
        []string{"cheerful", "infectiously_positive", "socially_confident"},
        "yes")

    // Anna Croft
    _assert_starter_facts(engine, "anna_croft",
        "outgoing_dynamo", "female", "male", "she_her",
        7, 12, 21,
        []string{"energetic", "driven", "action_oriented"},
        "no")

    // Saloni
    _assert_starter_facts(engine, "saloni",
        "outgoing_merrymaker", "female", "male", "she_her",
        7, 18, 20,
        []string{"cheerful", "infectiously_positive", "socially_confident"},
        "yes")

    // 5. Nicknames
    _sl(engine, "nickname_for(anna_croft, character_a, sunflower)")
    _sl(engine, "nickname_for(character_a, anna_croft, my_demon)")

    // 6. Starting relationships
    //    [NAME]
    _sl(engine, "sibling(character_a, saloni)")
    _sl(engine, "sibling(saloni, character_a)")
    _sl(engine, "crush(character_a, uriel)")
    _sl(engine, "friend(character_a, uriel)")
    _sl(engine, "friend(character_a, saloni)")
    _sl(engine, "acquaintance(character_a, anna_croft)")
    //    Also load as relationship/3 for the residents screen queries
    _sl(engine, "relationship(character_a, saloni, sibling)")
    _sl(engine, "relationship(character_a, uriel, friend)")
    _sl(engine, "relationship(character_a, anna_croft, acquaintance)")

    //    Uriel
    _sl(engine, "crush(uriel, character_a)")
    _sl(engine, "friend(uriel, character_a)")
    _sl(engine, "acquaintance(uriel, saloni)")
    _sl(engine, "stranger(uriel, anna_croft)")
    _sl(engine, "relationship(uriel, character_a, friend)")
    _sl(engine, "relationship(uriel, saloni, acquaintance)")

    //    Anna Croft
    _sl(engine, "acquaintance(anna_croft, character_a)")
    _sl(engine, "stranger(anna_croft, saloni)")
    _sl(engine, "stranger(anna_croft, uriel)")
    _sl(engine, "relationship(anna_croft, character_a, acquaintance)")

    //    Saloni
    _sl(engine, "friend(saloni, character_a)")
    _sl(engine, "acquaintance(saloni, uriel)")
    _sl(engine, "stranger(saloni, anna_croft)")
    _sl(engine, "relationship(saloni, character_a, friend)")
    _sl(engine, "relationship(saloni, character_a, sibling)")
    _sl(engine, "relationship(saloni, uriel, acquaintance)")

    // 8-10. Primed interaction counts
    _sl(engine, "interaction_count(character_a, uriel, 5)")
    _sl(engine, "interaction_count(character_a, saloni, 20)")
    _sl(engine, "interaction_count(character_a, anna_croft, 2)")

    // ---- Spawn on grid -----------------------------------------------------

    // Starters spawn in their designated zones.
    // We try the preferred zone first; grid_find_spawn falls back automatically.
    _spawn_in_zone(world, res, STARTER_ID_CHARACTER_A, .Town)
    _spawn_in_zone(world, res, STARTER_ID_URIEL,     .Beach)
    _spawn_in_zone(world, res, STARTER_ID_ANNA,      .Grass)
    _spawn_in_zone(world, res, STARTER_ID_SALONI,    .Town)
}

// Attempt to spawn a character in a preferred terrain zone.
// Falls back to any spawnable cell if the preferred zone is not found quickly.
_spawn_in_zone :: proc(world: ^World, res: ^Residents, id: u32, preferred: Terrain) {
    c := residents_find(res, id)
    if c == nil do return

    // Try up to 256 times to find a cell of the preferred terrain.
    for _ in 0..<256 {
        world.rng = world.rng * 6364136223846793005 + 1442695040888963407
        col := i32((world.rng >> 33) % GRID_W)
        world.rng = world.rng * 6364136223846793005 + 1442695040888963407
        row := i32((world.rng >> 17) % GRID_H)
        cell := &world.grid.cells[row][col]
        if cell.terrain == preferred && cell.occupant == 0 {
            if grid_place_character(&world.grid, id, col, row) {
                c.position = {col, row}
                idx := int(id - 1)
                if idx < MAX_RESIDENTS { world.positions[idx] = c.position }
                return
            }
        }
    }

    // Fallback: any spawnable cell
    col, row, ok := grid_find_spawn(&world.grid, &world.rng)
    if ok {
        grid_place_character(&world.grid, id, col, row)
        c.position = {col, row}
        idx := int(id - 1)
        if idx < MAX_RESIDENTS { world.positions[idx] = c.position }
    }
}

1	/*
2	 * residents.odin — Resident list screen (M2).
3	 */
4	
5	package dmls
6	
7	import rl "vendor:raylib"
8	import "core:fmt"
9	import "core:strings"
10	
11	// ---- Relationship query state ----------------------------------------------
12	
13	MAX_REL_RESULTS :: 64
14	
15	RelResult :: struct {
16	    other_id   : u32,
17	    other_name : string,
18	    tag        : string,
19	}
20	
21	ResidentListScreen :: struct {
22	    selected_id : u32,
23	    rel_results : [MAX_REL_RESULTS]RelResult,
24	    rel_count   : int,
25	}
26	
27	// Raw callback data (no context available in "c" procs)
28	RelQueryAccum :: struct {
29	    results : ^[MAX_REL_RESULTS]RelResult,
30	    count   : ^int,
31	}
32	
33	_rel_query_cb :: proc "c" (soln: ^EpilogSolution, ud: rawptr) -> EpilogSignal {
34	    acc := cast(^RelQueryAccum)ud
35	
36	    other_atom: cstring
37	    tag_atom  : cstring
38	
39	    for i in 0..<epilog_soln_var_count(soln) {
40	        name := epilog_soln_var_name(soln, i)
41	        val  := epilog_soln_var_atom(soln, i)
42	        if name == nil || val == nil do continue
43	        switch string(name) {
44	        case "Other": other_atom = val
45	        case "Tag":   tag_atom   = val
46	        }
47	    }
48	
49	    if other_atom != nil && tag_atom != nil && acc.count^ < MAX_REL_RESULTS {
50	        other_str := string(other_atom)
51	        other_id  := u32(0)
52	        if len(other_str) > 5 && other_str[:5] == "char_" {
53	            for ch in other_str[5:] {
54	                if ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' {
55	                    other_id = other_id * 10 + u32(ch - '0')
56	                }
57	            }
58	        }
59	        idx := acc.count^
60	        acc.results[idx].other_id   = other_id
61	        acc.results[idx].other_name = string(other_atom)
62	        acc.results[idx].tag        = string(tag_atom)
63	        acc.count^ += 1
64	    }
65	    return .EPILOG_NEXT
66	}
67	
68	_query_relationships :: proc(
69	    s      : ^ResidentListScreen,
70	    id     : u32,
71	    res    : ^Residents,
72	    engine : ^EpilogEngine,
73	) {
74	    s.rel_count = 0
75	
76	    buf_id  : [32]u8
77	    atom    := character_atom(id, buf_id[:])
78	    buf_goal: [128]u8
79	    gs := fmt.bprintf(buf_goal[:], "relationship(%s, Other, Tag)", atom)
80	    buf_goal[len(gs)] = 0
81	
82	    acc := RelQueryAccum{&s.rel_results, &s.rel_count}
83	    epilog_query(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf_goal[:])), _rel_query_cb, &acc)
84	
85	    // Resolve atom names to real character names (needs context, so done here)
86	    for i in 0..<s.rel_count {
87	        c := residents_find(res, s.rel_results[i].other_id)
88	        if c != nil {
89	            s.rel_results[i].other_name = character_name(c)
90	        }
91	    }
92	}
93	
94	// Returns true when the player clicks "Add resident".
95	resident_list_update :: proc(
96	    s     : ^ResidentListScreen,
97	    res   : ^Residents,
98	    engine: ^EpilogEngine,
99	) -> bool {
100	    rl.ClearBackground(COL_BG)
101	
102	    rl.DrawText("Island Residents", UI_LPAD, UI_PAD, UI_HSIZE, COL_TEXT)
103	
104	    count_buf: [32]u8
105	    cs := fmt.bprintf(count_buf[:], "%d resident(s)", res.count)
106	    count_buf[len(cs)] = 0
107	    rl.DrawText(cstring(raw_data(count_buf[:])), UI_LPAD, UI_PAD + UI_HSIZE + 6, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
108	
109	    add_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(SW() - 200), f32(UI_PAD), 180, 36}
110	    hov_add  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), add_rect)
111	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(add_rect, hov_add ? COL_ACCENT : COL_SELECT)
112	    rl.DrawText("+ Add resident", SW() - 190, UI_PAD + 8, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)
113	    if hov_add && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
114	        return true
115	    }
116	
117	    list_y := i32(UI_PAD + UI_HSIZE + 40)
118	
119	    for i in 0..<res.count {
120	        c    := &res.chars[i]
121	        row  := rl.Rectangle{f32(UI_LPAD), f32(list_y + i32(i) * 48), 340, 42}
122	        sel  := c.id == s.selected_id
123	        hov  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), row)
124	        bg   := sel ? COL_SELECT : (hov ? COL_HOVER : COL_PANEL)
125	        rl.DrawRectangleRec(row, bg)
126	        rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(row, 1, sel ? COL_ACCENT : COL_MUTED)
127	
128	        hue := f32(c.sprite) / f32(SPRITE_COUNT)
129	        r   := u8(80 + 100 * (1 - hue))
130	        g   := u8(80 + 100 * hue)
131	        b   := u8(180 - 80 * hue)
132	        rl.DrawRectangle(i32(row.x) + 6, i32(row.y) + 9, 24, 24, rl.Color{r, g, b, 200})
133	
134	        name_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(character_name(c), context.temp_allocator)
135	        rl.DrawText(name_cstr, i32(row.x) + 38, i32(row.y) + 6, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
136	
137	        pers_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(personality_label(c.personality), context.temp_allocator)
138	        rl.DrawText(pers_cstr, i32(row.x) + 38, i32(row.y) + 26, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
139	
140	        if hov && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) && c.id != s.selected_id {
141	            s.selected_id = c.id
142	            _query_relationships(s, c.id, res, engine)
143	        }
144	    }
145	
146	    if res.count == 0 {
147	        rl.DrawText("No residents yet. Add the first one!", UI_LPAD + 8, list_y + 14, UI_FSIZE, COL_MUTED)
148	    }
149	
150	    // Right panel — relationship detail
151	    if s.selected_id != 0 {
152	        rx := i32(UI_LPAD + 360)
153	        rl.DrawRectangle(rx - 10, list_y - 8, SW() - rx, SH() - list_y, COL_PANEL)
154	
155	        sel_char := residents_find(res, s.selected_id)
156	        if sel_char != nil {
157	            name_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(character_name(sel_char), context.temp_allocator)
158	            rl.DrawText(name_cstr, rx, list_y, UI_HSIZE, COL_TEXT)
159	
160	            ry := list_y + UI_HSIZE + 10
161	
162	            pron_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(pronouns_label(sel_char.pronouns), context.temp_allocator)
163	            rl.DrawText(pron_cstr, rx, ry, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
164	            ry += 22
165	
166	            pers_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(personality_label(sel_char.personality), context.temp_allocator)
167	            rl.DrawText(pers_cstr, rx, ry, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
168	            ry += 22
169	
170	            age_buf: [32]u8
171	            age_s := fmt.bprintf(age_buf[:], "Age %d", sel_char.age)
172	            age_buf[len(age_s)] = 0
173	            rl.DrawText(cstring(raw_data(age_buf[:])), rx, ry, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
174	            ry += 30
175	
176	            rl.DrawText("Relationships:", rx, ry, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)
177	            ry += 22
178	
179	            if s.rel_count == 0 {
180	                rl.DrawText("none", rx + 8, ry, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
181	            } else {
182	                for ri in 0..<s.rel_count {
183	                    r := &s.rel_results[ri]
184	                    row_buf: [128]u8
185	                    nr := fmt.bprintf(row_buf[:], "%s  --  %s", r.other_name, r.tag)
186	                    row_buf[len(nr)] = 0
187	                    rl.DrawText(cstring(raw_data(row_buf[:])), rx + 8, ry, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)
188	                    ry += 22
189	                }
190	            }
191	        }
192	    }
193	
194	    return false
195	}

1	/*
2	 * creation.odin — Character creation screen (M2).
3	 *
4	 * Screen flow:
5	 *   Creation → Confirm → Relationship seeding → back to Residents / Simulation
6	 *
7	 * All drawing uses Raylib directly — no external UI framework.
8	 */
9	
10	package dmls
11	
12	import rl "vendor:raylib"
13	import "core:strings"
14	import "core:fmt"
15	import "core:mem"
16	
17	// ---- Layout constants ------------------------------------------------------
18	
19	SCREEN_W :: 1920   // max; active dims are g_screen.w / g_screen.h via SW()/SH()
20	SCREEN_H :: 1080
21	
22	UI_PAD   :: 24
23	UI_LPAD  :: 64          // left margin for form fields
24	UI_FSIZE :: 20          // font size for body text
25	UI_HSIZE :: 28          // font size for headings
26	UI_SMALL :: 16
27	
28	COL_BG      :: rl.Color{15,  15,  20,  255}
29	COL_PANEL   :: rl.Color{28,  28,  38,  255}
30	COL_ACCENT  :: rl.Color{120, 80,  200, 255}
31	COL_ACCENT2 :: rl.Color{200, 80,  120, 255}
32	COL_TEXT    :: rl.Color{220, 220, 230, 255}
33	COL_MUTED   :: rl.Color{130, 130, 150, 255}
34	COL_INPUT   :: rl.Color{40,  40,  55,  255}
35	COL_HOVER   :: rl.Color{60,  60,  80,  255}
36	COL_SELECT  :: rl.Color{90,  60,  160, 255}
37	COL_CONFIRM :: rl.Color{60,  160, 100, 255}
38	
39	// ---- Input field -----------------------------------------------------------
40	
41	InputField :: struct {
42	    buf    : [MAX_NAME_LEN]u8,
43	    len    : int,
44	    active : bool,
45	}
46	
47	input_field_init :: proc(f: ^InputField) { f^ = {} }
48	
49	input_field_str :: proc(f: ^InputField) -> string {
50	    return string(f.buf[:f.len])

Starting with **1.1** — island name needs to persist through save/load. The `IslandName` struct already exists and is shown in the stat panel, but it's not serialised. I'll fix that, then proceed through all tiers.

**1.1 — Island naming: wire into save/load**

input.old_string:
/*
 * saveload.odin — Binary save/load (M7).
 *
 * Format:
 *   [4]  magic   "DMLS"
 *   [4]  version u32le = 1
 *   [8]  seed    u64le
 *   [8]  tick    u64le
 *   [4]  char_count u32le
 *   [N]  Character records (fixed struct, little-endian)
 *   [4]  rng_state_hi u32le  (upper 32 bits of DependentRNG.state)
 *   [4]  rng_state_lo u32le
 *   [4]  rng_seq_hi   u32le
 *   [4]  rng_seq_lo   u32le
 *   [4]  fact_count   u32le
 *   [N]  fact strings — each: [2] u16le length, then UTF-8 bytes (no null)
 *   [4]  event_count  u32le
 *   [N]  event entries — each: [8] tick u64le, [2] u16le length, bytes
 *   [4]  crc32   (over all bytes before this field)
 *
 * All integers little-endian. Structs written field-by-field to avoid
 * alignment padding surprises.
 */
input.new_string:
/*
 * saveload.odin — Binary save/load (M7).
 *
 * Format:
 *   [4]  magic   "DMLS"
 *   [4]  version u32le = 2
 *   [8]  seed    u64le
 *   [8]  tick    u64le
 *   [2+N] island name (u16le length + UTF-8 bytes)
 *   [4]  char_count u32le
 *   [N]  Character records (fixed struct, little-endian)
 *   [4]  rng_state_hi u32le  (upper 32 bits of DependentRNG.state)
 *   [4]  rng_state_lo u32le
 *   [4]  rng_seq_hi   u32le
 *   [4]  rng_seq_lo   u32le
 *   [4]  fact_count   u32le

 *   [N]  event entries — each: [8] tick u64le, [2] u16le length, bytes
 *   [4]  crc32   (over all bytes before this field)
 *
 * All integers little-endian. Structs written field-by-field to avoid
 * alignment padding surprises.
 */

content.content: The file d:\DMLS\src\anim.odin has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +// Each sprite is 2 frames × 8 rows × u8 bitmask (bit7=leftmost pixel).
+// Frame 0 = idle/stand, Frame 1 = step (right foot forward).
+_px :: proc(bits: u8, col: int) -> bool { return (bits >> u8(7 - col)) & 1 == 1 }
+// Generic humanoid sprite (used for player-created chars 4-7)
+// [NAME]: dark hair, glasses, pink cardigan  (sprite slot 0)
+// Uriel: blonde fringe, black top, teal trousers  (sprite slot 1)
+// Anna Croft: blonde, red eyes, orange top  (sprite slot 2)
+// Saloni: brown hair, glasses, peach top  (sprite slot 3)
+// Draw a character sprite at (px, py), scaled to cell_px.
+// Hair/accent colour is derived from base_col; skin is fixed warm tone.
+_draw_sprite :: proc(c: ^Character, px, py: i32, base_col: rl.Color, frame: int, cell_px: i32) {
+    // Scale: sprite is 8×8; cell_px might be 1–8+ px per grid cell.
+    // We draw each sprite pixel as max(1, cell_px/4) screen pixels, centred.
+            // Top 2 rows = hair/head → use base_col (character colour)
// Draw a character dot with idle animation applied (for use in sim view).
draw_char_dot_animated :: proc(c: ^Character, px, py: i32, base_col: rl.Color, t: f32, cell_px: i32) {
-    rl.DrawRectangle(px, apy, cell_px, cell_px, rl.Color{r, g, b, 255})
+    // Determine walk frame: flip every 0.5 s while the character recently moved.
+    // Apply brightness to base_col for the sprite
+        // Too small for sprites — fall back to coloured dot

content.text: **1.4 — Mood icons above characters.** I'll add a `draw_mood_icon` helper in `anim.odin` and call it from the sim view loop in `main.odin`.

content.content: The file d:\DMLS\src\anim.odin has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +// Small pixel icons that pulse gently (scale 1.0→1.15→1.0 over 1 second).
+// Drawn above the character sprite in the sim view.
+// Icon types: heart(pink), flame(orange), star(yellow), drop(blue), swords(red)
+_mood_icon_heart :: proc(cx, cy, r: i32, col: rl.Color) {
+    // Two overlapping circles + triangle = simple heart
+_mood_icon_flame :: proc(cx, cy, r: i32, col: rl.Color) {
+_mood_icon_star :: proc(cx, cy, r: i32, col: rl.Color) {
+    // Approximate star as overlapping rotated squares
+    rl.DrawRectangle(cx - r/4, cy - r, r/2, r*2, col)
+    rl.DrawRectangle(cx - r,   cy - r/4, r*2, r/2, col)

content.text:
Wait — looking at the original main loop, the World_Setup screen just called `_draw_world_setup(app)` and relied on the Residents screen being reached separately (there must be a navigation button). Let me re-read the original flow to not break it:

Looking at the original code: `_draw_world_setup` returns `bool` (true = generated). The original just called it without checking the return. The transition to `Residents` must happen elsewhere — but looking more carefully at the original, `_draw_world_setup` only returns `true` when Generate is clicked, but the original code ignored the return. So the world was generated but the user stayed on World_Setup until... they used a separate nav button. Let me check what actually transitions to Residents in the original:

Looking at the original main loop — there was just `_draw_world_setup(app)` with no transition! The user must have navigated to Residents via a button drawn inside `_draw_world_setup` or... actually on closer look, the original draw_world_setup doesn't have a "next" button to Residents — it just generates the world. The actual transition to Residents is likely via the Back button going to Title and then a different path. Wait, or maybe there's a "Simulate" / "Continue" button I missed.

Let me re-read `_draw_world_setup` — it only has "Generate island ->" (returns true) and "Back" (→ Title). The generate button returning true was supposed to advance the screen. The original code was just `_draw_world_setup(app)` which ignores the bool. This means the original code had a bug where generating the world didn't navigate anywhere. The DLC plan assumes this flow works though. Let me check if starters_seed is called anywhere else:

content.text:
All Tiers 1–3 are implemented. Here's a summary of what was written:

---

**Tier 1 — Critical Feel** ✓
- **1.1** Island name persists in save file (format version bumped to v2)
- **1.2** 8×8 pixel-art sprites per character (Deb/Uriel/Anna/Saloni + generic), 2-frame walk cycle, falls back to coloured dot when cell < 4px
- **1.3** Hover arrows colour-coded by relationship type (pink=crush, green=friend, teal=acquaintance, blue=sibling, lavender=partner, dashed red=rivals)
- **1.4** Mood icons pulse above sprites (heart/flame/star/raindrop/crossed swords per mood)
- **1.5** Event log draws character names in their palette colour using per-token measurement
- **1.6** Speed buttons in stat panel (Pause / 1× / 4× / 16×), Space toggles pause, +/- cycles speed

**Tier 2 — World Quality** ✓
- **2.1** Zone labels computed from terrain centroids, appear on load (3s) and on hover
- **2.2** Day/night overlay (Dawn/Day/Dusk/Night per 100-tick cycle) + stars over ocean at night
- **2.3** Weather system (Clear/Rain/Fog/Wind) with visual effects + stat panel display
- **2.4** Landmarks placed at generation (bench/food stall/lookout/grove) rendered as overlays on terrain
- **2.5** 64×64 minimap in stat panel showing terrain + character dots

**Tier 3 — Social Depth** ✓
- **3.1** Dialogue bubbles (5% chance per proximity tick, lines vary by relationship, fade after 3s)
- **3.2** Crush confession fires at crush≥20 ticks + interactions≥15, Accept/Reject prompt with 10s auto-resolve by mood
- **3.3** Rival confrontation fires when rivals within 5 cells for 30+ ticks, Let it happen / Intervene prompt
- **3.4** Friendship decay: friend→acquaintance at 30 idle ticks, acquaintance→stranger at 50
- **3.5** Backstory panel opens on clicking a resident in the sidebar (ESC or click outside closes)

New files created: [world_fx.odin](src/world_fx.odin), [landmarks.odin](src/landmarks.odin), [social_events.odin](src/social_events.odin)

914	    defer rl.CloseWindow()
915

content.text:
The structure looks correct. The problem is the `.Resolution` screen — `SW()` and `SH()` return 0 at startup because `g_screen` hasn't been set yet when `resolution_picker_update` first calls `rl.ClearBackground` and draws text. But that's pre-existing and was working before.

The real issue: I changed `.World_Setup` from a plain call to an `if` block that swallows the case — **the original code had `_draw_world_setup(app)` on its own line with no `if`**, meaning the return value was ignored and nothing happened after generate. The original flow must have been different: the world was generated at a different point. Let me look at what `_draw_world_setup` originally returned and what happened:

Looking at the original main loop it was just:
```odin
case .World_Setup:
    _draw_world_setup(app)
```

The `_draw_world_setup` returns `true` when Generate is clicked — but the original never used that. So the transition to Residents was **never through World_Setup returning true**. The original flow must be: generate the world inline when Back is pressed from Residents, or Residents is reached differently. 

But wait — actually the problem is simpler. The original `case .World_Setup` just called `_draw_world_setup(app)` and ignored the bool. The screen never advanced from World_Setup to Residents automatically — it relied on the user pressing some other button. But there's no such button in `_draw_world_setup`. 

This means the original had a bug — or more likely, the island was pre-seeded somewhere else. Looking at `main()`: there's no `starters_seed` call at init. So the original flow **was** broken, and the intent was that `_draw_world_setup` returning `true` should advance to Residents. My `if` fix is correct — but it's now calling `starters_seed` every time Generate is clicked, which is right.

The "no screen/output" issue is something else entirely. Let me check if the process is actually launching visibly:

file.content:
@echo off
setlocal enabledelayedexpansion

:: ============================================================================
:: DMLS build script (Windows)
::
:: Usage:
::   build.bat            -- build engine DLL + Odin binary (release)
::   build.bat debug      -- build engine DLL + Odin binary (debug)
::   build.bat engine     -- build Epilog DLL only
::   build.bat run        -- build then run
::   build.bat clean      -- remove build artifacts
:: ============================================================================

set MODE=release
set ACTION=all

for %%a in (%*) do (
    if /i "%%a"=="debug"  set MODE=debug
    if /i "%%a"=="engine" set ACTION=engine
    if /i "%%a"=="run"    set ACTION=run
    if /i "%%a"=="clean"  set ACTION=clean
)

:: ---- Clean -----------------------------------------------------------------

if /i "!ACTION!"=="clean" (
    echo [clean] Removing build artifacts...
    if exist build rmdir /s /q build
    if exist engine\epilog\epilog.dll del /q engine\epilog\epilog.dll
    echo [clean] Done.
    goto :eof
)

:: ---- Build Epilog DLL ------------------------------------------------------

if not exist build mkdir build

set EPILOG_DIR=engine\epilog
set EPILOG_DLL=engine\epilog\epilog.dll

if not exist "!EPILOG_DLL!" (
    set ACTION_ENGINE=1
) else (
    set ACTION_ENGINE=0
)

if /i "!ACTION!"=="engine" set ACTION_ENGINE=1
if /i "!ACTION!"=="all"    set ACTION_ENGINE=1
if /i "!ACTION!"=="run"    set ACTION_ENGINE=1

if "!ACTION_ENGINE!"=="1" (
    echo [engine] Building Epilog DLL...
    set CXX=clang++
    set CXXFLAGS=-std=c++23 -O2 -DNDEBUG -I!EPILOG_DIR!\include -target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

    if "!MODE!"=="debug" (
        set CXXFLAGS=-std=c++23 -g -O0 -DDEBUG -I!EPILOG_DIR!\include -target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
    )

    set EPILOG_SRCS=
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\internals\*.cpp) do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\parser\*.cpp)    do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\engine\*.cpp)    do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"

    :: -Wl,/DEF generates epilog.lib (import library) alongside the DLL
    !CXX! !CXXFLAGS! -shared -Wl,/DEF:!EPILOG_DIR!\epilog.def !EPILOG_SRCS! -o "!EPILOG_DLL!"
    if !errorlevel! neq 0 (
        echo [engine] ERROR: Epilog DLL build failed.
        exit /b 1
    )
    echo [engine] Built !EPILOG_DLL! + epilog.lib
)

if /i "!ACTION!"=="engine" goto :eof

:: ---- Build Odin binary -----------------------------------------------------

echo [odin] Building DMLS...

set ODIN_FLAGS=-collection:dmls=src
set ODIN_OUT=build\dmls.exe

if "!MODE!"=="debug" (
    set ODIN_FLAGS=!ODIN_FLAGS! -debug
)

:: Copy DLLs next to the binary so Windows finds them at runtime
copy /y "!EPILOG_DLL!" build\ >nul
copy /y "%ODIN_ROOT%\vendor\raylib\windows\raylib.dll" build\ >nul 2>&1 || copy /y "%ODIN_PATH%\vendor\raylib\windows\raylib.dll" build\ >nul

odin build src -out:!ODIN_OUT! !ODIN_FLAGS! -define:RAYLIB_SHARED=true
if !errorlevel! neq 0 (
    echo [odin] ERROR: Odin build failed.
    exit /b 1
)

echo [odin] Built !ODIN_OUT!

:: ---- Run -------------------------------------------------------------------

if /i "!ACTION!"=="run" (
    echo [run] Starting DMLS...
    !ODIN_OUT!
)

endlocal

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user asked to "read DLC.MD and implement" — implementing all features described in D:\DMLS\DLC.MD for the DMLS (Deb's Minimal Life Simulator) project. Mid-session the user said "till Tier 3", scoping the work to Tiers 1, 2, and 3 only (Tiers 4 and 5 deferred). The user then reported a build error (landmarks.odin syntax), then "no screen/output on running" after the build succeeded.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Odin language** — primary implementation language; XOR is `~` not `^`, pointer dereference is `^`
   - **Raylib** — rendering via `vendor:raylib`; DrawCircle radius is `f32`; RenderTexture2D for 80/20 split
   - **Epilog C++ DLL** — Prolog-based relational engine; C FFI via `epilog_ffi.h`; always source of truth for social state; queries use callbacks with "c" calling convention
   - **DMLS screen state machine** — Resolution → Title → World_Setup → Residents → Creation → Rel_Seeding → Simulation
   - **80/20 layout** — sim_tex (80% width, 60 FPS) + stat_tex (20% width, 10 FPS)
   - **Epilog facts** — base facts serialised on save, derived rules re-loaded at startup; never cache, always query
   - **Binary save format** — DMLS magic, version u32, seed u64, tick u64, island name (new v2), characters, RNG state, facts, events, CRC32
   - **Package scope** — all .odin files share `package dmls`; `_`-prefixed procs are still package-visible
   - **Global pointers** — `g_decay ^FriendshipDecay` and `g_bubbles ^BubbleSystem` in sim.odin allow sim_tick to notify Tier 3 systems without App in scope
   - **DLL distribution** — raylib.dll must be beside dmls.exe in build/; build.bat copies it via %ODIN_ROOT% which was unset

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **d:\DMLS\src\saveload.odin** — Bumped SAVE_VERSION to 2, added island_name optional parameter to save_game and load_game, inserted island name as length-prefixed string between tick and char_count:
     ```odin
     save_game :: proc(path: string, world: ^World, residents: ^Residents,
                       sim: ^SimState, engine: ^EpilogEngine,
                       island_name: ^IslandName = nil) -> bool
     load_game :: proc(path: string, world: ^World, residents: ^Residents,
                       sim: ^SimState, engine: ^EpilogEngine,
                       island_name: ^IslandName = nil) -> bool
     ```

   - **d:\DMLS\src\anim.odin** — Added 8×8 pixel-art sprite bitmaps, _draw_sprite, mood icon procs, draw_mood_icon:
     ```odin
     _SPRITE_DEB := [2][8]u8{
         {0b00111100, 0b01111110, 0b01011010, 0b00111100, 0b01111110, 0b00111100, 0b01100110, 0b01100110},
         {0b00111100, 0b01111110, 0b01011010, 0b00111100, 0b01111110, 0b00111100, 0b01001010, 0b01010110},
     }
     // Similar for _SPRITE_URIEL, _SPRITE_ANNA, _SPRITE_SALONI, _SPRITE_GENERIC

     _mood_icon_heart :: proc(cx, cy, r: i32, col: rl.Color) {
         rl.DrawCircle(cx - r/2, cy, f32(r/2), col)  // f32 cast required
         rl.DrawCircle(cx + r/2, cy, f32(r/2), col)
         rl.DrawTriangle({f32(cx-r),f32(cy)},{f32(cx+r),f32(cy)},{f32(cx),f32(cy+r)}, col)
     }
     draw_mood_icon :: proc(m: Mood, px, py: i32, t: f32, cell_px: i32)
     draw_char_dot_animated :: proc(c: ^Character, px, py: i32, base_col: rl.Color, t: f32, cell_px: i32)
     // Uses sprite when cell_px >= 4, plain rectangle otherwise
     ```

   - **d:\DMLS\src\main.odin** — Major additions:
     - App struct gained: `sim_speed: int`, `weather: WeatherState`, `zone_labels: ZoneLabels`, `bubbles: BubbleSystem`, `confession: ConfessionEvent`, `confrontation: ConfrontationEvent`, `decay: FriendshipDecay`, `backstory: BackstoryPanel`
     - `_HoverRelAccum` redesigned to hold tag per entry; `_draw_hover_arrows` now queries relationship/3, crush/2, rivals/2 with colour coding
     - `_draw_event_coloured` proc: scans event text for character names, draws in palette colour
     - Speed buttons drawn in stat panel; `_SPEED_HZ := [4]f32{0, 1.0, 4.0, 16.0}`
     - Tick loop: `for app.sim_speed > 0 && app.sim_timer >= tick_interval`
     - World_Setup case now: `if _draw_world_setup(app) { zone_labels_seed(...); starters_seed(...); sim_init(...); app.screen = .Residents }`
     - Weather/day-night/zone-labels/bubbles drawn in `_draw_sim_view`
     - Minimap + weather/time stat lines in `_draw_stat_panel`
     - Confession + confrontation + backstory drawn in `_update_simulation` after composite
     - `g_decay = &app.decay; g_bubbles = &app.bubbles` set in main()
     - `_do_save`/`_do_load` pass `&app.island_name`

     if g_decay != nil { friendship_decay_record(g_decay, a.id, b.id, tick) }
     if g_bubbles != nil && dependent_next(&s.rng) < 0.05 {

         bubble_on_interact(g_bubbles, a, b, rel_tag, &s.rng)

   - **d:\DMLS\src\world.odin** — `world_init` now calls `landmarks_place(&w.grid, seed)` after `grid_generate`

   - **d:\DMLS\src\grid.odin** — `grid_render` now calls `landmark_render_cell(cell.object_id, px, py, cpx)` after drawing terrain colour; removed occupant dot draw (characters are drawn separately with sprites)

   - **d:\DMLS\src\world_fx.odin** (NEW) — Zone labels, day/night cycle, weather system, minimap:

     Imports used: `rl "vendor:raylib"`, `"core:fmt"`, `"core:strings"` (math removed — was unused)

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **landmarks.odin XOR operator**: Linter/user changed `~` (Odin XOR) to `^` (pointer deref). Error: "Syntax Error: Expected ';', got identifier". Fix: reverted to `lrng := seed ~ u64(0xDEAD_BEEF_1234)`
   - **world_fx.odin f64/f32 mismatch**: `dependent_next` returns `f64`, `span` is `f32`. Fix: `f32(dependent_next(rng)) * span`
   - **anim.odin DrawCircle radius**: `rl.DrawCircle(cx, cy, radius, col)` — radius must be `f32`. Fixed in `_mood_icon_heart`, `_mood_icon_flame`, `_mood_icon_drop` with `f32(r/2)` and `f32(r*2/3)` casts
   - **No screen/output**: Exit code 0xC0000135 (DLL not found). `raylib.dll` was missing from `build/`. `build.bat` line 90 uses `%ODIN_ROOT%` env var which was not set; odin is at `D:\Odin\dist`. Fix: manually ran `Copy-Item "D:\Odin\dist\vendor\raylib\windows\raylib.dll" "D:\DMLS\build\raylib.dll"`

   - **Unused import `"core:math"`** in social_events.odin: removed

5. Problem Solving:

   - raylib.dll distribution: permanently fixed by placing dll in build/; build.bat already has the copy command but `%ODIN_ROOT%` env var needs to be set or the copy silently fails

6. All User Messages:

8. Current Work:
   The last thing worked on was diagnosing and fixing the "no screen/output on running" issue. The build succeeded (`build\dmls.exe` was produced), but running the exe crashed silently with exit code `0xC0000135` (STATUS_DLL_NOT_FOUND). Investigation revealed `raylib.dll` was absent from `D:\DMLS\build\` — only `epilog.dll` was present. `build.bat` line 90 attempts to copy raylib.dll using `%ODIN_ROOT%` which was not set in the environment. The fix was:

   ```
   After this, `build\` contained both `epilog.dll` and `raylib.dll`. The build.bat `run` action then reported `[run] Starting DMLS...` successfully on the prior build, but we were running directly from `build\dmls.exe` which was the crash. No confirmation the window actually appeared was received from the user before the summary request.

file.content:
@echo off
setlocal enabledelayedexpansion

:: ============================================================================
:: DMLS build script (Windows)
::
:: Usage:
::   build.bat            -- build engine DLL + Odin binary (release)
::   build.bat debug      -- build engine DLL + Odin binary (debug)
::   build.bat engine     -- build Epilog DLL only
::   build.bat run        -- build then run
::   build.bat clean      -- remove build artifacts
:: ============================================================================

set MODE=release
set ACTION=all

for %%a in (%*) do (
    if /i "%%a"=="debug"  set MODE=debug
    if /i "%%a"=="engine" set ACTION=engine
    if /i "%%a"=="run"    set ACTION=run
    if /i "%%a"=="clean"  set ACTION=clean
)

:: ---- Clean -----------------------------------------------------------------

if /i "!ACTION!"=="clean" (
    echo [clean] Removing build artifacts...
    if exist build rmdir /s /q build
    if exist engine\epilog\epilog.dll del /q engine\epilog\epilog.dll
    echo [clean] Done.
    goto :eof
)

:: ---- Build Epilog DLL ------------------------------------------------------

if not exist build mkdir build

set EPILOG_DIR=engine\epilog
set EPILOG_DLL=engine\epilog\epilog.dll

if not exist "!EPILOG_DLL!" (
    set ACTION_ENGINE=1
) else (
    set ACTION_ENGINE=0
)

if /i "!ACTION!"=="engine" set ACTION_ENGINE=1
if /i "!ACTION!"=="all"    set ACTION_ENGINE=1
if /i "!ACTION!"=="run"    set ACTION_ENGINE=1

if "!ACTION_ENGINE!"=="1" (
    echo [engine] Building Epilog DLL...
    set CXX=clang++
    set CXXFLAGS=-std=c++23 -O2 -DNDEBUG -I!EPILOG_DIR!\include -target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

    if "!MODE!"=="debug" (
        set CXXFLAGS=-std=c++23 -g -O0 -DDEBUG -I!EPILOG_DIR!\include -target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
    )

    set EPILOG_SRCS=
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\internals\*.cpp) do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\parser\*.cpp)    do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\engine\*.cpp)    do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"

    :: -Wl,/DEF generates epilog.lib (import library) alongside the DLL
    !CXX! !CXXFLAGS! -shared -Wl,/DEF:!EPILOG_DIR!\epilog.def !EPILOG_SRCS! -o "!EPILOG_DLL!"
    if !errorlevel! neq 0 (
        echo [engine] ERROR: Epilog DLL build failed.
        exit /b 1
    )
    echo [engine] Built !EPILOG_DLL! + epilog.lib
)

if /i "!ACTION!"=="engine" goto :eof

:: ---- Build Odin binary -----------------------------------------------------

echo [odin] Building DMLS...

set ODIN_FLAGS=-collection:dmls=src
set ODIN_OUT=build\dmls.exe

if "!MODE!"=="debug" (
    set ODIN_FLAGS=!ODIN_FLAGS! -debug
)

:: Copy DLLs next to the binary so Windows finds them at runtime
copy /y "!EPILOG_DLL!" build\ >nul

:: Locate raylib.dll from the odin compiler's own directory
for /f "tokens=*" %%i in ('where odin 2^>nul') do set ODIN_BIN=%%i
for %%i in ("!ODIN_BIN!") do set ODIN_DIR=%%~dpi
set RAYLIB_SRC=!ODIN_DIR!vendor\raylib\windows\raylib.dll
if exist "!RAYLIB_SRC!" (
    copy /y "!RAYLIB_SRC!" build\ >nul
) else (
    echo [warn] raylib.dll not found at !RAYLIB_SRC! -- ensure it is in build\
)

odin build src -out:!ODIN_OUT! !ODIN_FLAGS! -define:RAYLIB_SHARED=true
if !errorlevel! neq 0 (
    echo [odin] ERROR: Odin build failed.
    exit /b 1
)

echo [odin] Built !ODIN_OUT!

:: ---- Run -------------------------------------------------------------------

if /i "!ACTION!"=="run" (
    echo [run] Starting DMLS...
    !ODIN_OUT!
)

endlocal
