content.content:
1	# DMLS — Web API & Dashboard Specification
2	
3	> *The island runs. The browser watches.*
4	
5	DMLS exposes a lightweight HTTP REST API and a WebSocket event stream from a parallel thread alongside the Raylib simulation. When Raylib is not initiated (headless mode), the API thread becomes the only interface. A bundled web dashboard connects to both and provides a live browser view of the simulation state.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Architecture
10	
11	```
12	┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
13	│                    DMLS Process                     │
14	│                                                     │
15	│  ┌──────────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────┐ │
16	│  │  Simulation Core │    │     API Thread         │ │
17	│  │  (Odin + Epilog) │◄──►│  HTTP REST + WebSocket │ │
18	│  │  [main thread]   │    │  [parallel thread]     │ │
19	│  └──────────────────┘    └────────────┬───────────┘ │
20	│                                       │             │
21	└───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┘
22	                                        │
23	                          ┌─────────────▼────────────┐
24	                          │    Web Dashboard          │
25	                          │    localhost:7771         │
26	                          │    HTML + JS + WS client  │
27	                          └──────────────────────────┘
28	```
29	
30	### Thread Model
31	
32	- **Main thread** — simulation loop (Raylib if headed, pure tick loop if headless)
33	- **API thread** — HTTP server + WebSocket broadcaster, spawned at startup
34	- **Shared state** — a mutex-protected snapshot struct that the simulation writes every tick and the API thread reads
35	
36	The API thread never touches Epilog directly. It reads only from the snapshot. The simulation thread owns Epilog exclusively.
37	
38	### Headless Mode
39	
40	Launch with `--headless` flag:
41	
42	```sh
43	./dmls --headless
44	./dmls --headless --load save.dmls
45	./dmls --headless --seed 5961641267136C
46	```
47	
48	In headless mode:
49	- Raylib is never initialised
50	- No window is created
51	- Simulation ticks at configured speed (default 1×, configurable via API)
52	- API thread is the only interface
53	- Process runs until `POST /api/control/quit` or SIGINT
54	
55	---
56	
57	## Base URL
58	
59	```
60	http://localhost:7771
61	```
62	
63	Port configurable via `--port` flag or `dmls.cfg`.
64	
65	---
66	
67	## Shared Snapshot Struct
68	
69	Written by simulation thread every tick. Read by API thread under mutex.
70	
71	```odin
72	SimSnapshot :: struct {
73	    tick            : u64,
74	    island_name     : string,
75	    seed            : u64,
76	    speed           : u8,          // 0=paused, 1=1x, 4=4x, 16=16x
77	    weather         : string,
78	    time_of_day     : string,      // dawn / day / dusk / night
79	    residents       : [dynamic]ResidentSnapshot,
80	    crushes         : [dynamic]RelationSnapshot,
81	    rivals          : [dynamic]RivalSnapshot,
82	    events          : [dynamic]EventEntry,
83	    is_headless     : bool,
84	}
85	
86	ResidentSnapshot :: struct {
87	    id              : u32,
88	    name            : string,
89	    mood            : string,
90	    position        : [2]i32,
91	    personality     : string,
92	    relationships   : [dynamic]RelationSnapshot,
93	    interaction_counts : map[string]u32,
94	}
95	
96	RelationSnapshot :: struct {
97	    from            : string,
98	    to              : string,
99	    kind            : string,      // crush, friend, acquaintance, sibling, partner, rival
100	}
101	
102	RivalSnapshot :: struct {
103	    a               : string,
104	    b               : string,
105	    duration_ticks  : u32,
106	}
107	
108	EventEntry :: struct {
109	    tick            : u64,
110	    description     : string,
111	    characters      : [dynamic]string,
112	    kind            : string,      // crush_formed, rival_detected, confession, confrontation, decay
113	}
114	```
115	
116	---
117	
118	## REST API
119	
120	All responses are `application/json`. All write endpoints require `Content-Type: application/json`.
121	
122	---
123	
124	### GET /api/status
125	
126	Returns current simulation snapshot.
127	
128	**Response:**
129	```json
130	{
131	  "tick": 134,
132	  "island_name": "Yggdrasilz",
133	  "seed": "5961641267136C",
134	  "speed": 1,
135	  "weather": "clear",
136	  "time_of_day": "day",
137	  "is_headless": false,
138	  "residents": 4,
139	  "crushes": 1,
140	  "rivals": 0
141	}
142	```
143	
144	---
145	
146	### GET /api/residents
147	
148	Returns all residents with full relationship and mood data.
149	
150	**Response:**
151	```json
152	{
153	  "residents": [
154	    {
155	      "id": 1,
156	      "name": "[NAME]",
157	      "mood": "content",
158	      "personality": "ambitious_achiever",
159	      "position": [128, 140],
160	      "relationships": [
161	        { "to": "Uriel",      "kind": "friend"      },
162	        { "to": "Saloni",     "kind": "sibling"     },
163	        { "to": "Anna Croft", "kind": "acquaintance"}
164	      ],
165	      "interaction_counts": {
166	        "Uriel":      12,
167	        "Saloni":     24,
168	        "Anna Croft":  4
169	      }
170	    }
171	  ]
172	}
173	```
174	
175	---
176	
177	### GET /api/residents/:name
178	
179	Returns a single resident by name (case-insensitive).
180	
181	**Example:** `GET /api/residents/uriel`
182	
183	**Response:**
184	```json
185	{
186	  "id": 2,
187	  "name": "Uriel",
188	  "mood": "lovestruck",
189	  "personality": "outgoing_merrymaker",
190	  "position": [110, 155],
191	  "relationships": [
192	    { "to": "[NAME]", "kind": "friend", "extra": "crushing" },
193	    { "to": "Saloni",    "kind": "acquaintance" },
194	    { "to": "Anna Croft","kind": "stranger" }
195	  ],
196	  "interaction_counts": {
197	    "[NAME]": 12,
198	    "Saloni":     3
199	  }
200	}
201	```
202	
203	**Error (404):**
204	```json
205	{ "error": "resident not found", "name": "uriel" }
206	```
207	
208	---
209	
210	### GET /api/relationships
211	
212	Returns all active relationships across the island.
213	
214	**Response:**
215	```json
216	{
217	  "relationships": [
218	    { "from": "Uriel",      "to": "[NAME]", "kind": "crush"       },
219	    { "from": "[NAME]",  "to": "Uriel",     "kind": "friend"      },
220	    { "from": "[NAME]",  "to": "Saloni",    "kind": "sibling"     },
221	    { "from": "Anna Croft", "to": "[NAME]", "kind": "acquaintance"}
222	  ]
223	}
224	```
225	
226	---
227	
228	### GET /api/rivals
229	
230	Returns all active rival pairs.
231	
232	**Response:**
233	```json
234	{
235	  "rivals": [
236	    {
237	      "a": "Uriel",
238	      "b": "Anna Croft",
239	      "target": "[NAME]",
240	      "duration_ticks": 12
241	    }
242	  ]
243	}
244	```
245	
246	---
247	
248	### GET /api/events
249	
250	Returns the event log. Optional query params: `?limit=20&kind=crush_formed`
251	
252	**Response:**
253	```json
254	{
255	  "events": [
256	    {
257	      "tick": 89,
258	      "kind": "rival_detected",
259	      "description": "Uriel and Anna Croft became rivals over [NAME]",
260	      "characters": ["Uriel", "Anna Croft", "[NAME]"]
261	    },
262	    {
263	      "tick": 52,
264	      "kind": "interaction",
265	      "description": "Uriel approached [NAME]",
266	      "characters": ["Uriel", "[NAME]"]
267	    }
268	  ]
269	}
270	```
271	
272	---
273	
274	### GET /api/query
275	
276	Run a raw Epilog query against the simulation state. Read-only — does not allow `assert` or `retract`.
277	
278	**Request:**
279	```json
280	{ "goal": "rivals(X, Y)" }
281	```
282	
283	**Response (success):**
284	```json
285	{
286	  "goal": "rivals(X, Y)",
287	  "results": [
288	    { "X": "uriel", "Y": "anna_croft" }
289	  ],
290	  "count": 1
291	}
292	```
293	
294	**Response (failure):**
295	```json
296	{
297	  "goal": "rivals(X, Y)",
298	  "results": [],
299	  "count": 0
300	}
301	```
302	
303	**Response (forbidden):**
304	```json
305	{
306	  "error": "assert and retract are not permitted via the query API"
307	}
308	```
309	
310	---
311	
312	### POST /api/control/speed
313	
314	Set simulation tick speed.
315	
316	**Request:**
317	```json
318	{ "speed": 4 }
319	```
320	
321	Valid values: `0` (pause), `1`, `4`, `16`.
322	
323	**Response:**
324	```json
325	{ "speed": 4, "previous": 1 }
326	```
327	
328	---
329	
330	### POST /api/control/pause
331	
332	Toggle pause state.
333	
334	**Response:**
335	```json
336	{ "paused": true, "tick": 134 }
337	```
338	
339	---
340	
341	### POST /api/control/save
342	
343	Trigger a save to the default save path.
344	
345	**Response:**
346	```json
347	{ "saved": true, "path": "yggdrasilz.dmls", "tick": 134 }
348	```
349	
350	---
351	
352	### POST /api/control/quit
353	
354	Gracefully shut down the simulation. Saves before exit.
355	
356	**Response:**
357	```json
358	{ "quitting": true, "final_tick": 134 }
359	```
360	
361	---
362	
363	### POST /api/residents
364	
365	Add a new resident to the island at runtime (equivalent to character creation screen).
366	
367	**Request:**
368	```json
369	{
370	  "name": "Kim Dokja",
371	  "age": 20,
372	  "birthday": "2/14",
373	  "gender": "male",
374	  "dating_pref": "male",
375	  "pronouns": "he/him",
376	  "personality": "ambitious_achiever",
377	  "sprite": 5,
378	  "starting_relationships": [
379	    { "with": "[NAME]", "kind": "stranger" }
380	  ]
381	}
382	```
383	
384	**Response:**
385	```json
386	{
387	  "id": 5,
388	  "name": "Kim Dokja",
389	  "spawned_at_tick": 134,
390	  "position": [132, 148]
391	}
392	```
393	
394	---
395	
396	### DELETE /api/residents/:name
397	
398	Remove a resident from the island. Retracts all their Epilog facts. Cannot remove the last resident.
399	
400	**Response:**
401	```json
402	{ "removed": "Kim Dokja", "at_tick": 200 }
403	```
404	
405	---
406	
407	## WebSocket API
408	
409	Connect to:
410	```
411	ws://localhost:7771/ws
412	```
413	
414	The server pushes events to all connected clients. Clients may also send commands.
415	
416	---
417	
418	### Server → Client Messages
419	
420	All messages are JSON with a `type` field.
421	
422	---
423	
424	#### tick
425	
426	Sent every simulation tick.
427	
428	```json
429	{
430	  "type": "tick",
431	  "tick": 135,
432	  "speed": 1,
433	  "weather": "clear",
434	  "time_of_day": "day"
435	}
436	```
437	
438	---
439	
440	#### snapshot
441	
442	Full state snapshot. Sent on client connect, and every 10 ticks.
443	
444	```json
445	{
446	  "type": "snapshot",
447	  "tick": 135,
448	  "island_name": "Yggdrasilz",
449	  "seed": "5961641267136C",
450	  "residents": [ /* ResidentSnapshot array */ ],
451	  "crushes": 1,
452	  "rivals": 0,
453	  "events": [ /* last 20 EventEntry */ ]
454	}
455	```
456	
457	---
458	
459	#### event
460	
461	Fired when a social event occurs.
462	
463	```json
464	{
465	  "type": "event",
466	  "tick": 135,
467	  "kind": "crush_formed",
468	  "description": "Anna Croft developed a crush on [NAME]",
469	  "characters": ["Anna Croft", "[NAME]"]
470	}
471	```
472	
473	Event kinds:
474	- `crush_formed`
475	- `crush_resolved` (accepted/rejected)
476	- `rival_detected`
477	- `rival_confrontation`
478	- `friendship_formed`
479	- `friendship_decayed`
480	- `partner_formed`
481	- `mood_changed`
482	- `resident_arrived`
483	- `resident_departed`
484	
485	---
486	
487	#### mood_update
488	
489	Fired when any character's mood changes.
490	
491	```json
492	{
493	  "type": "mood_update",
494	  "character": "Uriel",
495	  "mood": "jealous",
496	  "previous_mood": "lovestruck",
497	  "tick": 136
498	}
499	```
500	
501	---
502	
503	#### position_update
504	
505	Fired every tick for each moving character. Clients use this to animate sprites on the dashboard map.
506	
507	```json
508	{
509	  "type": "position_update",
510	  "updates": [
511	    { "name": "[NAME]",  "position": [129, 140] },
512	    { "name": "Uriel",      "position": [111, 154] },
513	    { "name": "Anna Croft", "position": [122, 147] },
514	    { "name": "Saloni",     "position": [130, 141] }
515	  ],
516	  "tick": 135
517	}
518	```
519	
520	---
521	
522	#### prompt
523	
524	Fired when the simulation is waiting for player input (confession, confrontation). If headless, a prompt sent via WS is the only way to resolve it.
525	
526	```json
527	{
528	  "type": "prompt",
529	  "prompt_id": "conf_uriel_deb_136",
530	  "kind": "confession",
531	  "from": "Uriel",
532	  "to": "[NAME]",
533	  "tick": 136,
534	  "auto_resolve_in_ticks": 10,
535	  "options": ["accept", "reject"]
536	}
537	```
538	
539	Simulation pauses tick progression until prompt is resolved or auto-resolved.
540	
541	---
542	
543	#### error
544	
545	Sent when a client command fails.
546	
547	```json
548	{
549	  "type": "error",
550	  "message": "unknown speed value: 8",
551	  "original_command": { "type": "set_speed", "speed": 8 }
552	}
553	```
554	
555	---
556	
557	### Client → Server Messages
558	
559	Clients send JSON commands over the WebSocket connection.
560	
561	---
562	
563	#### set_speed
564	
565	```json
566	{ "type": "set_speed", "speed": 4 }
567	```
568	
569	---
570	
571	#### pause / unpause
572	
573	```json
574	{ "type": "pause" }
575	{ "type": "unpause" }
576	```
577	
578	---
579	
580	#### resolve_prompt
581	
582	Resolve a pending confession or confrontation prompt.
583	
584	```json
585	{
586	  "type": "resolve_prompt",
587	  "prompt_id": "conf_uriel_deb_136",
588	  "choice": "accept"
589	}
590	```
591	
592	---
593	
594	#### query
595	
596	Run an Epilog query (read-only).
597	
598	```json
599	{ "type": "query", "goal": "mood(X, jealous)" }
600	```
601	
602	Response arrives as a `query_result` message:
603	
604	```json
605	{
606	  "type": "query_result",
607	  "goal": "mood(X, jealous)",
608	  "results": [ { "X": "uriel" } ],
609	  "count": 1
610	}
611	```
612	
613	---
614	
615	#### add_resident
616	
617	```json
618	{
619	  "type": "add_resident",
620	  "name": "Kim Dokja",
621	  "age": 20,
622	  "gender": "male",
623	  "dating_pref": "male",
624	  "personality": "ambitious_achiever"
625	}
626	```
627	
628	---
629	
630	## Web Dashboard
631	
632	Served at `http://localhost:7771` as a single HTML file with embedded JS. No framework, no build step — pure HTML5 + vanilla JS + WebSocket client.
633	
634	---
635	
636	### Layout
637	
638	```
639	┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
640	│  DMLS Dashboard — Yggdrasilz          Tick: 135   Speed: [1×▼]  │
641	├──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┤
642	│                          │  RESIDENTS                            │
643	│                          │  ● [NAME]    content               │
644	│    Island Map            │  ● Uriel        lovestruck ♥          │
645	│    (canvas, live)        │  ● Anna Croft   content               │
646	│                          │  ● Saloni       content               │
647	│    Characters as dots    ├───────────────────────────────────────┤
648	│    with mood icons       │  RELATIONSHIPS                        │
649	│    Arrows on hover       │  Uriel → [NAME]     [crush]        │
650	│                          │  [NAME] ↔ Saloni    [sibling]      │
651	│                          │  Anna Croft → [NAME] [acquaintance]│
652	│                          ├───────────────────────────────────────┤
653	│                          │  EVENTS                               │
654	│                          │  [135] Uriel approached [NAME]     │
655	│                          │  [128] Anna & Deb traded stories      │
656	│                          │  [089] Rivals detected: Uriel ↔ Anna  │
657	├──────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────┤
658	│  QUERY CONSOLE                                                   │
659	│  ?- rivals(X, Y).    [Run]                                       │
660	│  > X = uriel, Y = anna_croft                                     │
661	└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
662	```
663	
664	---
665	
666	### Dashboard Features
667	
668	- **Live island map** — HTML5 Canvas renders the 256×256 grid. Characters are coloured dots with mood icons. Hover a dot to see relationship arrows drawn to all connected characters.
669	- **Resident list** — live mood and name, coloured per character. Click to expand relationship detail.
670	- **Relationship panel** — all current relationships with kind labels. Rival pairs highlighted in red.
671	- **Event log** — scrollable, last 50 events. Character names colour-coded. New events flash briefly.
672	- **Query console** — type any Epilog query, send via WebSocket, see results inline.
673	- **Speed control** — dropdown: Paused / 1× / 4× / 16×. Updates via WebSocket command.
674	- **Prompt overlay** — when a confession or confrontation fires, a modal appears over the dashboard with Accept/Reject buttons. Sends `resolve_prompt` via WebSocket.
675	- **Connection indicator** — green dot (connected) / red dot (disconnected, auto-reconnect every 3 seconds).
676	
677	---
678	
679	### Dashboard Delivery
680	
681	The dashboard HTML is embedded as a string literal in the Odin API server and served at `GET /`. No external files required. The simulation binary is fully self-contained.
682	
683	```odin
684	DASHBOARD_HTML :: #load("dashboard.html", string)
685	
686	handle_root :: proc(conn: ^HttpConn) {
687	    http_respond(conn, 200, "text/html", DASHBOARD_HTML)
688	}
689	```
690	
691	`dashboard.html` is compiled into the binary at build time via Odin's `#load` directive.
692	
693	---
694	
695	## Implementation Notes
696	
697	### HTTP Server
698	
699	Use a minimal Odin HTTP server — either a thin wrapper over POSIX sockets or a small library. The server needs to handle:
700	- GET and POST only
701	- JSON request/response bodies
702	- WebSocket upgrade handshake (RFC 6455)
703	- One persistent listener thread with a connection pool
704	
705	Do not use a full web framework. The API surface is small enough that raw socket handling is tractable.
706	
707	### Mutex Strategy
708	
709	```odin
710	SimState :: struct {
711	    mu       : sync.Mutex,
712	    snapshot : SimSnapshot,
713	}
714	```
715	
716	Simulation thread:
717	1. Runs tick logic
718	2. Locks mutex
719	3. Writes updated snapshot
720	4. Unlocks mutex
721	
722	API thread:
723	1. Locks mutex
724	2. Reads snapshot (copy to local)
725	3. Unlocks mutex
726	4. Serves response from local copy
727	
728	Mutex hold time is always short — just a struct copy. No Epilog calls inside the lock.
729	
730	### WebSocket Broadcast
731	
732	Maintain a slice of active WebSocket connections. After each tick, the simulation thread signals the API thread via a channel. The API thread reads the snapshot and broadcasts `tick`, `position_update`, and any pending `event` messages to all connected clients.
733	
734	```odin
735	ws_broadcast :: proc(server: ^WsServer, msg: string) {
736	    sync.lock(&server.conn_mu)
737	    defer sync.unlock(&server.conn_mu)
738	    for conn in server.connections {
739	        ws_send(conn, msg)
740	    }
741	}
742	```
743	
744	### Headless Tick Loop
745	
746	```odin
747	headless_loop :: proc(sim: ^Simulation, api: ^ApiServer) {
748	    for !sim.quit {
749	        simulation_tick(sim)
750	        snapshot := build_snapshot(sim)
751	        api_push_snapshot(api, snapshot)
752	        time.sleep(tick_duration(sim.speed))
753	    }
754	}
755	```
756	
757	---
758	
759	## Configuration File
760	
761	`dmls.cfg` — read at startup, all fields optional.
762	
763	```ini
764	port     = 7771
765	headless = false
766	speed    = 1
767	save     = autosave.dmls
768	seed     = 0          ; 0 = random
769	```
770	
771	CLI flags override config file values.
772	
773	---
774	
775	## Summary
776	
777	| Feature | Method |
778	|---------|--------|
779	| Full state poll | `GET /api/status` |
780	| Resident data | `GET /api/residents` |
781	| Live event stream | WebSocket `/ws` |
782	| Run Epilog query | `GET /api/query` or WS `query` |
783	| Control speed | `POST /api/control/speed` or WS `set_speed` |
784	| Resolve prompts headlessly | WS `resolve_prompt` |
785	| Add residents at runtime | `POST /api/residents` or WS `add_resident` |
786	| Web dashboard | `GET /` — self-contained HTML |
787	
788	---
789	
790	*The island runs whether or not you're watching.*
791	*The API makes sure you can always check in.*

file.content:
# DMLS — Web API & Dashboard Specification

> *The island runs. The browser watches.*

DMLS exposes a lightweight HTTP REST API and a WebSocket event stream from a parallel thread alongside the Raylib simulation. When Raylib is not initiated (headless mode), the API thread becomes the only interface. A bundled web dashboard connects to both and provides a live browser view of the simulation state.

---

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    DMLS Process                     │
│                                                     │
│  ┌──────────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Simulation Core │    │     API Thread         │ │
│  │  (Odin + Epilog) │◄──►│  HTTP REST + WebSocket │ │
│  │  [main thread]   │    │  [parallel thread]     │ │
│  └──────────────────┘    └────────────┬───────────┘ │
│                                       │             │
└───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┘
                                        │
                          ┌─────────────▼────────────┐
                          │    Web Dashboard          │
                          │    localhost:7771         │
                          │    HTML + JS + WS client  │
                          └──────────────────────────┘
```

### Thread Model

- **Main thread** — simulation loop (Raylib if headed, pure tick loop if headless)
- **API thread** — HTTP server + WebSocket broadcaster, spawned at startup
- **Shared state** — a mutex-protected snapshot struct that the simulation writes every tick and the API thread reads

The API thread never touches Epilog directly. It reads only from the snapshot. The simulation thread owns Epilog exclusively.

### Headless Mode

Launch with `--headless` flag:

```sh
./dmls --headless
./dmls --headless --load save.dmls
./dmls --headless --seed 5961641267136C
```

In headless mode:
- Raylib is never initialised
- No window is created
- Simulation ticks at configured speed (default 1×, configurable via API)
- API thread is the only interface
- Process runs until `POST /api/control/quit` or SIGINT

---

## Base URL

```
http://localhost:7771
```

Port configurable via `--port` flag or `dmls.cfg`.

---

## Shared Snapshot Struct

Written by simulation thread every tick. Read by API thread under mutex.

```odin
SimSnapshot :: struct {
    tick            : u64,
    island_name     : string,
    seed            : u64,
    speed           : u8,          // 0=paused, 1=1x, 4=4x, 16=16x
    weather         : string,
    time_of_day     : string,      // dawn / day / dusk / night
    residents       : [dynamic]ResidentSnapshot,
    crushes         : [dynamic]RelationSnapshot,
    rivals          : [dynamic]RivalSnapshot,
    events          : [dynamic]EventEntry,
    is_headless     : bool,
}

ResidentSnapshot :: struct {
    id              : u32,
    name            : string,
    mood            : string,
    position        : [2]i32,
    personality     : string,
    relationships   : [dynamic]RelationSnapshot,
    interaction_counts : map[string]u32,
}

RelationSnapshot :: struct {
    from            : string,
    to              : string,
    kind            : string,      // crush, friend, acquaintance, sibling, partner, rival
}

RivalSnapshot :: struct {
    a               : string,
    b               : string,
    duration_ticks  : u32,
}

EventEntry :: struct {
    tick            : u64,
    description     : string,
    characters      : [dynamic]string,
    kind            : string,      // crush_formed, rival_detected, confession, confrontation, decay
}
```

---

## REST API

All responses are `application/json`. All write endpoints require `Content-Type: application/json`.

---

### GET /api/status

Returns current simulation snapshot.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "tick": 134,
  "island_name": "Yggdrasilz",
  "seed": "5961641267136C",
  "speed": 1,
  "weather": "clear",
  "time_of_day": "day",
  "is_headless": false,
  "residents": 4,
  "crushes": 1,
  "rivals": 0
}
```

---

### GET /api/residents

Returns all residents with full relationship and mood data.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "residents": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "[NAME]",
      "mood": "content",
      "personality": "ambitious_achiever",
      "position": [128, 140],
      "relationships": [
        { "to": "Uriel",      "kind": "friend"      },
        { "to": "Saloni",     "kind": "sibling"     },
        { "to": "Anna Croft", "kind": "acquaintance"}
      ],
      "interaction_counts": {
        "Uriel":      12,
        "Saloni":     24,
        "Anna Croft":  4
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

---

### GET /api/residents/:name

Returns a single resident by name (case-insensitive).

**Example:** `GET /api/residents/uriel`

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 2,
  "name": "Uriel",
  "mood": "lovestruck",
  "personality": "outgoing_merrymaker",
  "position": [110, 155],
  "relationships": [
    { "to": "[NAME]", "kind": "friend", "extra": "crushing" },
    { "to": "Saloni",    "kind": "acquaintance" },
    { "to": "Anna Croft","kind": "stranger" }
  ],
  "interaction_counts": {
    "[NAME]": 12,
    "Saloni":     3
  }
}
```

**Error (404):**
```json
{ "error": "resident not found", "name": "uriel" }
```

---

### GET /api/relationships

Returns all active relationships across the island.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "relationships": [
    { "from": "Uriel",      "to": "[NAME]", "kind": "crush"       },
    { "from": "[NAME]",  "to": "Uriel",     "kind": "friend"      },
    { "from": "[NAME]",  "to": "Saloni",    "kind": "sibling"     },
    { "from": "Anna Croft", "to": "[NAME]", "kind": "acquaintance"}
  ]
}
```

---

### GET /api/rivals

Returns all active rival pairs.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "rivals": [
    {
      "a": "Uriel",
      "b": "Anna Croft",
      "target": "[NAME]",
      "duration_ticks": 12
    }
  ]
}
```

---

### GET /api/events

Returns the event log. Optional query params: `?limit=20&kind=crush_formed`

**Response:**
```json
{
  "events": [
    {
      "tick": 89,
      "kind": "rival_detected",
      "description": "Uriel and Anna Croft became rivals over [NAME]",
      "characters": ["Uriel", "Anna Croft", "[NAME]"]
    },
    {
      "tick": 52,
      "kind": "interaction",
      "description": "Uriel approached [NAME]",
      "characters": ["Uriel", "[NAME]"]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

### GET /api/query

Run a raw Epilog query against the simulation state. Read-only — does not allow `assert` or `retract`.

**Request:**
```json
{ "goal": "rivals(X, Y)" }
```

**Response (success):**
```json
{
  "goal": "rivals(X, Y)",
  "results": [
    { "X": "uriel", "Y": "anna_croft" }
  ],
  "count": 1
}
```

**Response (failure):**
```json
{
  "goal": "rivals(X, Y)",
  "results": [],
  "count": 0
}
```

**Response (forbidden):**
```json
{
  "error": "assert and retract are not permitted via the query API"
}
```

---

### POST /api/control/speed

Set simulation tick speed.

**Request:**
```json
{ "speed": 4 }
```

Valid values: `0` (pause), `1`, `4`, `16`.

**Response:**
```json
{ "speed": 4, "previous": 1 }
```

---

### POST /api/control/pause

Toggle pause state.

**Response:**
```json
{ "paused": true, "tick": 134 }
```

---

### POST /api/control/save

Trigger a save to the default save path.

**Response:**
```json
{ "saved": true, "path": "yggdrasilz.dmls", "tick": 134 }
```

---

### POST /api/control/quit

Gracefully shut down the simulation. Saves before exit.

**Response:**
```json
{ "quitting": true, "final_tick": 134 }
```

---

### POST /api/residents

Add a new resident to the island at runtime (equivalent to character creation screen).

**Request:**
```json
{
  "name": "Kim Dokja",
  "age": 20,
  "birthday": "2/14",
  "gender": "male",
  "dating_pref": "male",
  "pronouns": "he/him",
  "personality": "ambitious_achiever",
  "sprite": 5,
  "starting_relationships": [
    { "with": "[NAME]", "kind": "stranger" }
  ]
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": 5,
  "name": "Kim Dokja",
  "spawned_at_tick": 134,
  "position": [132, 148]
}
```

---

### DELETE /api/residents/:name

Remove a resident from the island. Retracts all their Epilog facts. Cannot remove the last resident.

**Response:**
```json
{ "removed": "Kim Dokja", "at_tick": 200 }
```

---

## WebSocket API

Connect to:
```
ws://localhost:7771/ws
```

The server pushes events to all connected clients. Clients may also send commands.

---

### Server → Client Messages

All messages are JSON with a `type` field.

---

#### tick

Sent every simulation tick.

```json
{
  "type": "tick",
  "tick": 135,
  "speed": 1,
  "weather": "clear",
  "time_of_day": "day"
}
```

---

#### snapshot

Full state snapshot. Sent on client connect, and every 10 ticks.

```json
{
  "type": "snapshot",
  "tick": 135,
  "island_name": "Yggdrasilz",
  "seed": "5961641267136C",
  "residents": [ /* ResidentSnapshot array */ ],
  "crushes": 1,
  "rivals": 0,
  "events": [ /* last 20 EventEntry */ ]
}
```

---

#### event

Fired when a social event occurs.

```json
{
  "type": "event",
  "tick": 135,
  "kind": "crush_formed",
  "description": "Anna Croft developed a crush on [NAME]",
  "characters": ["Anna Croft", "[NAME]"]
}
```

Event kinds:
- `crush_formed`
- `crush_resolved` (accepted/rejected)
- `rival_detected`
- `rival_confrontation`
- `friendship_formed`
- `friendship_decayed`
- `partner_formed`
- `mood_changed`
- `resident_arrived`
- `resident_departed`

---

#### mood_update

Fired when any character's mood changes.

```json
{
  "type": "mood_update",
  "character": "Uriel",
  "mood": "jealous",
  "previous_mood": "lovestruck",
  "tick": 136
}
```

---

#### position_update

Fired every tick for each moving character. Clients use this to animate sprites on the dashboard map.

```json
{
  "type": "position_update",
  "updates": [
    { "name": "[NAME]",  "position": [129, 140] },
    { "name": "Uriel",      "position": [111, 154] },
    { "name": "Anna Croft", "position": [122, 147] },
    { "name": "Saloni",     "position": [130, 141] }
  ],
  "tick": 135
}
```

---

#### prompt

Fired when the simulation is waiting for player input (confession, confrontation). If headless, a prompt sent via WS is the only way to resolve it.

```json
{
  "type": "prompt",
  "prompt_id": "conf_uriel_deb_136",
  "kind": "confession",
  "from": "Uriel",
  "to": "[NAME]",
  "tick": 136,
  "auto_resolve_in_ticks": 10,
  "options": ["accept", "reject"]
}
```

Simulation pauses tick progression until prompt is resolved or auto-resolved.

---

#### error

Sent when a client command fails.

```json
{
  "type": "error",
  "message": "unknown speed value: 8",
  "original_command": { "type": "set_speed", "speed": 8 }
}
```

---

### Client → Server Messages

Clients send JSON commands over the WebSocket connection.

---

#### set_speed

```json
{ "type": "set_speed", "speed": 4 }
```

---

#### pause / unpause

```json
{ "type": "pause" }
{ "type": "unpause" }
```

---

#### resolve_prompt

Resolve a pending confession or confrontation prompt.

```json
{
  "type": "resolve_prompt",
  "prompt_id": "conf_uriel_deb_136",
  "choice": "accept"
}
```

---

#### query

Run an Epilog query (read-only).

```json
{ "type": "query", "goal": "mood(X, jealous)" }
```

Response arrives as a `query_result` message:

```json
{
  "type": "query_result",
  "goal": "mood(X, jealous)",
  "results": [ { "X": "uriel" } ],
  "count": 1
}
```

---

#### add_resident

```json
{
  "type": "add_resident",
  "name": "Kim Dokja",
  "age": 20,
  "gender": "male",
  "dating_pref": "male",
  "personality": "ambitious_achiever"
}
```

---

## Web Dashboard

Served at `http://localhost:7771` as a single HTML file with embedded JS. No framework, no build step — pure HTML5 + vanilla JS + WebSocket client.

---

### Layout

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  DMLS Dashboard — Yggdrasilz          Tick: 135   Speed: [1×▼]  │
├──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┤
│                          │  RESIDENTS                            │
│                          │  ● [NAME]    content               │
│    Island Map            │  ● Uriel        lovestruck ♥          │
│    (canvas, live)        │  ● Anna Croft   content               │
│                          │  ● Saloni       content               │
│    Characters as dots    ├───────────────────────────────────────┤
│    with mood icons       │  RELATIONSHIPS                        │
│    Arrows on hover       │  Uriel → [NAME]     [crush]        │
│                          │  [NAME] ↔ Saloni    [sibling]      │
│                          │  Anna Croft → [NAME] [acquaintance]│
│                          ├───────────────────────────────────────┤
│                          │  EVENTS                               │
│                          │  [135] Uriel approached [NAME]     │
│                          │  [128] Anna & Deb traded stories      │
│                          │  [089] Rivals detected: Uriel ↔ Anna  │
├──────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────┤
│  QUERY CONSOLE                                                   │
│  ?- rivals(X, Y).    [Run]                                       │
│  > X = uriel, Y = anna_croft                                     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

### Dashboard Features

- **Live island map** — HTML5 Canvas renders the 256×256 grid. Characters are coloured dots with mood icons. Hover a dot to see relationship arrows drawn to all connected characters.
- **Resident list** — live mood and name, coloured per character. Click to expand relationship detail.
- **Relationship panel** — all current relationships with kind labels. Rival pairs highlighted in red.
- **Event log** — scrollable, last 50 events. Character names colour-coded. New events flash briefly.
- **Query console** — type any Epilog query, send via WebSocket, see results inline.
- **Speed control** — dropdown: Paused / 1× / 4× / 16×. Updates via WebSocket command.
- **Prompt overlay** — when a confession or confrontation fires, a modal appears over the dashboard with Accept/Reject buttons. Sends `resolve_prompt` via WebSocket.
- **Connection indicator** — green dot (connected) / red dot (disconnected, auto-reconnect every 3 seconds).

---

### Dashboard Delivery

The dashboard HTML is embedded as a string literal in the Odin API server and served at `GET /`. No external files required. The simulation binary is fully self-contained.

```odin
DASHBOARD_HTML :: #load("dashboard.html", string)

handle_root :: proc(conn: ^HttpConn) {
    http_respond(conn, 200, "text/html", DASHBOARD_HTML)
}
```

`dashboard.html` is compiled into the binary at build time via Odin's `#load` directive.

---

## Implementation Notes

### HTTP Server

Use a minimal Odin HTTP server — either a thin wrapper over POSIX sockets or a small library. The server needs to handle:
- GET and POST only
- JSON request/response bodies
- WebSocket upgrade handshake (RFC 6455)
- One persistent listener thread with a connection pool

Do not use a full web framework. The API surface is small enough that raw socket handling is tractable.

### Mutex Strategy

```odin
SimState :: struct {
    mu       : sync.Mutex,
    snapshot : SimSnapshot,
}
```

Simulation thread:
1. Runs tick logic
2. Locks mutex
3. Writes updated snapshot
4. Unlocks mutex

API thread:
1. Locks mutex
2. Reads snapshot (copy to local)
3. Unlocks mutex
4. Serves response from local copy

Mutex hold time is always short — just a struct copy. No Epilog calls inside the lock.

### WebSocket Broadcast

Maintain a slice of active WebSocket connections. After each tick, the simulation thread signals the API thread via a channel. The API thread reads the snapshot and broadcasts `tick`, `position_update`, and any pending `event` messages to all connected clients.

```odin
ws_broadcast :: proc(server: ^WsServer, msg: string) {
    sync.lock(&server.conn_mu)
    defer sync.unlock(&server.conn_mu)
    for conn in server.connections {
        ws_send(conn, msg)
    }
}
```

### Headless Tick Loop

```odin
headless_loop :: proc(sim: ^Simulation, api: ^ApiServer) {
    for !sim.quit {
        simulation_tick(sim)
        snapshot := build_snapshot(sim)
        api_push_snapshot(api, snapshot)
        time.sleep(tick_duration(sim.speed))
    }
}
```

---

## Configuration File

`dmls.cfg` — read at startup, all fields optional.

```ini
port     = 7771
headless = false
speed    = 1
save     = autosave.dmls
seed     = 0          ; 0 = random
```

CLI flags override config file values.

---

## Summary

| Feature | Method |
|---------|--------|
| Full state poll | `GET /api/status` |
| Resident data | `GET /api/residents` |
| Live event stream | WebSocket `/ws` |
| Run Epilog query | `GET /api/query` or WS `query` |
| Control speed | `POST /api/control/speed` or WS `set_speed` |
| Resolve prompts headlessly | WS `resolve_prompt` |
| Add residents at runtime | `POST /api/residents` or WS `add_resident` |
| Web dashboard | `GET /` — self-contained HTML |

---

*The island runs whether or not you're watching.*
*The API makes sure you can always check in.*

src\bridge.odin
src\noise.odin
src\character.odin
src\rand.odin
src\starter_chars.odin
src\creation.odin
src\title.odin
src\residents.odin
src\relationships.odin
src\screen_cfg.odin
src\saveload.odin
src\grid.odin
src\world.odin
src\sim.odin
src\main.odin
src\social_events.odin
src\landmarks.odin
src\world_fx.odin
src\anim.odin

1	/*
2	 * sim.odin — Relational simulation tick system (M5).
3	 *
4	 * Every simulation tick:
5	 *   1. Characters near each other accumulate interaction_count in Epilog.
6	 *   2. Dependent RNG decides if a crush develops.
7	 *   3. Eligible check (Epilog query) gates crush formation.
8	 *   4. Mood is re-derived each tick from relationship state.
9	 *   5. Events are appended to the rolling event log.
10	 *
11	 * The Epilog engine is always the source of truth for social state.
12	 * Odin never caches relationship facts — it queries every tick.
13	 */
14	
15	package dmls
16	
17	import "core:fmt"
18	import "core:strings"
19	
20	// ---- Event log -------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	MAX_EVENTS   :: 20
23	EVENT_MSG_LEN :: 96
24	
25	EventEntry :: struct {
26	    tick : u64,
27	    msg  : [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8,
28	}
29	
30	EventLog :: struct {
31	    entries : [MAX_EVENTS]EventEntry,
32	    head    : int,   // ring-buffer write index
33	    count   : int,
34	}
35	
36	event_log_push :: proc(log: ^EventLog, tick: u64, msg: string) {
37	    e := &log.entries[log.head]
38	    e.tick = tick
39	    n := min(len(msg), EVENT_MSG_LEN - 1)
40	    copy(e.msg[:], msg[:n])
41	    e.msg[n] = 0
42	    log.head  = (log.head + 1) % MAX_EVENTS
43	    if log.count < MAX_EVENTS { log.count += 1 }
44	}
45	
46	// Iterate from newest to oldest (newest first).
47	event_log_get :: proc(log: ^EventLog, i: int) -> ^EventEntry {
48	    if i >= log.count do return nil
49	    idx := (log.head - 1 - i + MAX_EVENTS * 2) % MAX_EVENTS
50	    return &log.entries[idx]
51	}
52	
53	event_entry_str :: proc(e: ^EventEntry) -> string {
54	    return strings.truncate_to_byte(string(e.msg[:]), 0)
55	}
56	
57	// ---- Mood ------------------------------------------------------------------
58	
59	Mood :: enum u8 {
60	    Content,
61	    Happy,
62	    Sad,
63	    Lovestruck,
64	    Jealous,
65	}
66	
67	mood_label :: proc(m: Mood) -> string {
68	    switch m {
69	    case .Content:    return "content"
70	    case .Happy:      return "happy"
71	    case .Sad:        return "sad"
72	    case .Lovestruck: return "lovestruck"
73	    case .Jealous:    return "jealous"
74	    }
75	    return "content"
76	}
77	
78	// Per-character social state (derived each tick — NOT authoritative, just cache).
79	CharSocial :: struct {
80	    mood             : Mood,
81	    interaction_count: u32,   // total lifetime interactions (also in Epilog)
82	    has_crush        : bool,
83	}
84	
85	// ---- Simulation state ------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	SimState :: struct {
88	    rng      : DependentRNG,
89	    social   : [MAX_RESIDENTS]CharSocial,
90	    log      : EventLog,
91	    // Counters displayed in stats panel
92	    crush_count : int,
93	    rival_count : int,
94	}
95	
96	sim_init :: proc(s: ^SimState, seed: u64) {
97	    s^ = {}
98	    dependent_rng_init(&s.rng, seed, 0xC0FFEE_CAFE_1234)
99	}
100	
101	// ---- Epilog interaction count helpers --------------------------------------
102	
103	_assert_interaction :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, id_a, id_b: u32, count: u32) {
104	    buf_a, buf_b, buf_src: [128]u8
105	    atom_a := character_atom(id_a, buf_a[:])
106	    atom_b := character_atom(id_b, buf_b[:])
107	
108	    // Retract old count first (ignore failure — might not exist)
109	    rs := fmt.bprintf(buf_src[:], "interaction_count(%s, %s, _)", atom_a, atom_b)
110	    buf_src[len(rs)] = 0
111	    // Epilog doesn't have retract in the FFI, so we just re-assert.
112	    // Because Epilog indexes by first arg, the newer fact shadows the older one
113	    // for queries. In practice we'll accumulate duplicates but queries still work.
114	    as := fmt.bprintf(buf_src[:], "interaction_count(%s, %s, %d).", atom_a, atom_b, count)
115	    buf_src[len(as)] = 0
116	    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf_src[:])))
117	}
118	
119	_assert_crush :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, from_id, on_id: u32) {
120	    buf_a, buf_b, buf_src: [128]u8
121	    atom_a := character_atom(from_id, buf_a[:])
122	    atom_b := character_atom(on_id, buf_b[:])
123	    s := fmt.bprintf(buf_src[:], "crushing(%s, %s).", atom_a, atom_b)
124	    buf_src[len(s)] = 0
125	    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf_src[:])))
126	}
127	
128	_assert_mood :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, id: u32, m: Mood) {
129	    buf_id, buf_src: [128]u8
130	    atom := character_atom(id, buf_id[:])
131	    s := fmt.bprintf(buf_src[:], "mood(%s, %s).", atom, mood_label(m))
132	    buf_src[len(s)] = 0
133	    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf_src[:])))
134	}
135	
136	// ---- Epilog query helpers (callbacks use "c" ABI) --------------------------
137	
138	_BoolResult :: struct { found: bool }
139	
140	_bool_cb :: proc "c" (soln: ^EpilogSolution, ud: rawptr) -> EpilogSignal {
141	    r := cast(^_BoolResult)ud
142	    r.found = true
143	    return .EPILOG_STOP
144	}
145	
146	_epilog_bool :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, goal: string) -> bool {
147	    buf: [256]u8
148	    gs := fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "%s", goal)
149	    buf[len(gs)] = 0
150	    r := _BoolResult{false}
151	    epilog_query(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf[:])), _bool_cb, &r)
152	    return r.found
153	}
154	
155	// Count solutions (up to cap)
156	_CountResult :: struct { n: int, cap: int }
157	
158	_count_cb :: proc "c" (soln: ^EpilogSolution, ud: rawptr) -> EpilogSignal {
159	    r := cast(^_CountResult)ud
160	    r.n += 1
161	    if r.n >= r.cap do return .EPILOG_STOP
162	    return .EPILOG_NEXT
163	}
164	
165	_epilog_count :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, goal: string, cap: int) -> int {
166	    buf: [256]u8
167	    gs := fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "%s", goal)
168	    buf[len(gs)] = 0
169	    r := _CountResult{0, cap}
170	    epilog_query(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf[:])), _count_cb, &r)
171	    return r.n
172	}
173	
174	// ---- Load M5 Epilog rules --------------------------------------------------
175	
176	sim_load_rules :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine) {
177	    // Mood derivation rules
178	    epilog_load_string(engine,
179	        "mood(X, jealous) :- rivals(X, _).")
180	    epilog_load_string(engine,
181	        "mood(X, lovestruck) :- crushing(X, _).")
182	    epilog_load_string(engine,
183	        "mood(X, happy) :- best_friends(X, _).")
184	    epilog_load_string(engine,
185	        "mood(X, sad) :- relationship(X, _, partner), crushing(_, X).")
186	    // unrequited: X crushes on Y but Y doesn't crush on X
187	    epilog_load_string(engine,
188	        "unrequited(X, Y) :- crushing(X, Y), \\+ crushing(Y, X).")
189	}
190	
191	// ---- Proximity check -------------------------------------------------------
192	// Two characters are "near" if they are within NEAR_DIST grid cells.
193	
194	NEAR_DIST :: 8
195	
196	_chars_near :: proc(a, b: ^Character) -> bool {
197	    dx := a.position[0] - b.position[0]
198	    dy := a.position[1] - b.position[1]
199	    if dx < 0 { dx = -dx }
200	    if dy < 0 { dy = -dy }
201	    return dx + dy <= NEAR_DIST
202	}
203	
204	// ---- Main simulation tick --------------------------------------------------
205	
206	// Global pointers set by main — nil-safe throughout.
207	g_decay   : ^FriendshipDecay
208	g_bubbles : ^BubbleSystem
209	
210	sim_tick :: proc(s: ^SimState, res: ^Residents, engine: ^EpilogEngine, tick: u64) {
211	    // 1. Proximity — increment interaction counts
212	    for i in 0..<res.count {
213	        for j in i+1..<res.count {
214	            a := &res.chars[i]
215	            b := &res.chars[j]
216	            if _chars_near(a, b) {
217	                ia := int(a.id - 1)
218	                ib := int(b.id - 1)
219	                if ia < MAX_RESIDENTS { s.social[ia].interaction_count += 1 }
220	                if ib < MAX_RESIDENTS { s.social[ib].interaction_count += 1 }
221	                cnt_a := s.social[ia].interaction_count
222	                _assert_interaction(engine, a.id, b.id, cnt_a)
223	                if g_decay != nil {
224	                    friendship_decay_record(g_decay, a.id, b.id, tick)
225	                }
226	                // Dialogue bubble (low probability to avoid spam)
227	                if g_bubbles != nil && dependent_next(&s.rng) < 0.05 {
228	                    rel_tag := "acquaintance"
229	                    if ia < MAX_RESIDENTS && s.social[ia].has_crush { rel_tag = "crush" }
230	                    bubble_on_interact(g_bubbles, a, b, rel_tag, &s.rng)
231	                }
232	
233	                // 2. Crush development (gate: eligible query + RNG threshold)
234	                if !s.social[ia].has_crush {
235	                    chance := 0.003  // ~0.3% per proximity tick per character
236	                    roll   := dependent_next(&s.rng)
237	                    if roll < chance {
238	                        buf_a, buf_b, goal_buf: [128]u8
239	                        atom_a := character_atom(a.id, buf_a[:])
240	                        atom_b := character_atom(b.id, buf_b[:])
241	                        gs := fmt.bprintf(goal_buf[:], "eligible(%s, %s)", atom_a, atom_b)
242	                        goal_buf[len(gs)] = 0
243	                        if _epilog_bool(engine, string(goal_buf[:len(gs)])) {
244	                            _assert_crush(engine, a.id, b.id)
245	                            s.social[ia].has_crush = true
246	                            // Event log
247	                            msg_buf: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
248	                            name_a := character_name(a)
249	                            name_b := character_name(b)
250	                            ms := fmt.bprintf(msg_buf[:], "%s develops a crush on %s", name_a, name_b)
251	                            event_log_push(&s.log, tick, string(msg_buf[:len(ms)]))
252	                        }
253	                    }
254	                }
255	            }
256	        }
257	    }
258	
259	    // 3. Re-derive moods and assert them
260	    for i in 0..<res.count {
261	        c  := &res.chars[i]
262	        ia := int(c.id - 1)
263	        if ia >= MAX_RESIDENTS do continue
264	
265	        buf_id, goal_buf: [64]u8
266	        atom := character_atom(c.id, buf_id[:])
267	
268	        // Check rivals
269	        gs := fmt.bprintf(goal_buf[:], "rivals(%s, _)", atom)
270	        goal_buf[len(gs)] = 0
271	        if _epilog_bool(engine, string(goal_buf[:len(gs)])) {
272	            s.social[ia].mood = .Jealous
273	            _assert_mood(engine, c.id, .Jealous)
274	            continue
275	        }
276	
277	        // Check crushing
278	        gs = fmt.bprintf(goal_buf[:], "crushing(%s, _)", atom)
279	        goal_buf[len(gs)] = 0
280	        if _epilog_bool(engine, string(goal_buf[:len(gs)])) {
281	            s.social[ia].mood = .Lovestruck
282	            _assert_mood(engine, c.id, .Lovestruck)
283	            continue
284	        }
285	
286	        // Check best_friends
287	        gs = fmt.bprintf(goal_buf[:], "best_friends(%s, _)", atom)
288	        goal_buf[len(gs)] = 0
289	        if _epilog_bool(engine, string(goal_buf[:len(gs)])) {
290	            s.social[ia].mood = .Happy
291	            _assert_mood(engine, c.id, .Happy)
292	            continue
293	        }
294	
295	        s.social[ia].mood = .Content
296	        _assert_mood(engine, c.id, .Content)
297	    }
298	
299	    // 4. Update summary counters for stats panel display
300	    crush_buf, rival_buf: [64]u8
301	    cs := fmt.bprintf(crush_buf[:], "crushing(_, _)")
302	    crush_buf[len(cs)] = 0
303	    rs := fmt.bprintf(rival_buf[:], "rivals(_, _)")
304	    rival_buf[len(rs)] = 0
305	    s.crush_count = _epilog_count(engine, string(crush_buf[:len(cs)]), 64)
306	    s.rival_count = _epilog_count(engine, string(rival_buf[:len(rs)]), 64) / 2  // pairs
307	}
308

1	/*
2	 * main.odin — DMLS entry point (M0–M8, complete).
3	 *
4	 * Screen state machine:
5	 *   .Resolution   — resolution picker (first screen)
6	 *   .Title        — animated title card + New/Load buttons
7	 *   .World_Setup  — hex seed entry, generate island
8	 *   .Residents    — resident list + "Add resident"
9	 *   .Creation     — character creation form
10	 *   .Rel_Seeding  — relationship tags for new character
11	 *   .Simulation   — 80/20 split: grid left, sidebar right
12	 *
13	 * Layout in Simulation:
14	 *   Left  sim_tex  (80% of SW): grid, animated char dots, hover arrows
15	 *   Right stat_tex (20% of SW): island stats, residents, events, navigate
16	 *   Dividing line drawn at stats_x().
17	 *
18	 * Save / Load (M7): F5 = save, F9 = load; auto-save every 60 ticks.
19	 * Cutscene overlay (M8): triggered by new social events from SimState.
20	 *
21	 * Navigation:
22	 *   ESC on any screen = quit.
23	 *   Back buttons on World_Setup, Residents, Rel_Seeding, Simulation.
24	 */
25

102	// ---- Helpers ---------------------------------------------------------------
103	
104	_parse_seed :: proc(s: string) -> (u64, bool) {
105	    val := u64(0)
106	    if len(s) == 0 do return 0, false
107	    for ch in s {
108	        switch ch {
109	        case '0'..='9': val = val * 16 + u64(ch - '0')
110	        case 'a'..='f': val = val * 16 + u64(ch - 'a' + 10)
111	        case 'A'..='F': val = val * 16 + u64(ch - 'A' + 10)
112	        case: return 0, false
113	        }
114	    }
115	    return val, true
116	}
117	
118	_default_seed :: proc() -> u64 {
119	    return 0x596767647261736C
120	}

128	_do_load :: proc(app: ^App) -> bool {
129	    epilog_destroy(app.engine)
130	    app.engine = epilog_create()
131	    _load_all_rules(app)
132	
133	    ok := load_game(SAVE_PATH, &app.world, &app.residents, &app.sim, app.engine, &app.island_name)
134	    if ok {
135	        zone_labels_seed(&app.zone_labels, &app.world.grid)
136	        sim_tick(&app.sim, &app.residents, app.engine, app.world.tick)
137	        app.cam_col = GRID_W / 2
138	        app.cam_row = GRID_H / 2
139	        _draw_stat_panel(app)
140	    }
141	    return ok
142	}

148	// ---- Shared exit/back button helpers ---------------------------------------
149	
150	// Draw an exit (X) button in the top-right corner.  Returns true if clicked.
151	_draw_exit_btn :: proc() -> bool {
152	    sz   := i32(36)
153	    x    := SW() - sz - 8
154	    y    := i32(8)
155	    rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(x), f32(y), f32(sz), f32(sz)}
156	    hov  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), rect)
157	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(rect, hov ? rl.Color{180, 40, 40, 255} : rl.Color{80, 30, 30, 200})
158	    rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(rect, 1, rl.Color{200, 60, 60, 255})
159	    rl.DrawText("X", x + 11, y + 8, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
160	    return hov && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT)
161	}
162	
163	// Draw a back button at the bottom-left.  Returns true if clicked.
164	_draw_back_btn :: proc() -> bool {
165	    rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(UI_LPAD), f32(SH() - 52), 120, 36}
166	    hov  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), rect)
167	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(rect, hov ? COL_HOVER : COL_INPUT)
168	    rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(rect, 1, COL_MUTED)
169	    rl.DrawText("<- Back", UI_LPAD + 10, SH() - 42, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
170	    return hov && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT)
171	}
172	
173	// ---- World setup screen ----------------------------------------------------
174	
175	_draw_world_setup :: proc(app: ^App) -> bool {
176	    rl.ClearBackground(COL_BG)
177	    rl.DrawText("DMLS", UI_LPAD, UI_PAD, 48, COL_TEXT)
178	    rl.DrawText("Deb's Minimal Life Simulator", UI_LPAD, UI_PAD + 54, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
179	
180	    y := i32(UI_PAD + 110)
181	    rl.DrawText("World seed (hex)", UI_LPAD, y, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
182	    y += 22
183	
184	    input_field_draw(&app.seed_field, UI_LPAD, y, 340, 38, "")
185	    if rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(),
186	            rl.Rectangle{f32(UI_LPAD), f32(y), 340, 38}) &&
187	            rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
188	        app.seed_field.active = true
189	    }
190	    input_field_update(&app.seed_field)
191	
192	    if app.seed_err {
193	        rl.DrawText("Invalid hex seed", UI_LPAD, y + 44, UI_SMALL, rl.RED)
194	    }
195	
196	    y += 54
197	    rnd_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(UI_LPAD + 350), f32(y - 54), 160, 38}
198	    hov_rnd  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), rnd_rect)
199	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(rnd_rect, hov_rnd ? COL_HOVER : COL_INPUT)
200	    rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(rnd_rect, 1, COL_MUTED)
201	    rl.DrawText("Random seed", UI_LPAD + 360, y - 44, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
202	    if hov_rnd && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
203	        rng := u64(rl.GetTime() * 1e9)
204	        rng ~= rng << 13; rng ~= rng >> 7; rng ~= rng << 17
205	        buf: [20]u8
206	        ns := fmt.bprintf(buf[:], "%016X", rng)
207	        app.seed_field.len = 0
208	        for i in 0..<len(ns) { app.seed_field.buf[i] = buf[i] }
209	        app.seed_field.len = len(ns)
210	    }
211	
212	    go_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(UI_LPAD), f32(y), 200, 42}
213	    hov_go  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), go_rect)
214	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(go_rect, hov_go ? COL_ACCENT : COL_SELECT)
215	    rl.DrawText("Generate island ->", UI_LPAD + 12, y + 11, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
216	
217	    if (hov_go && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT)) || rl.IsKeyPressed(.ENTER) {
218	        seed_str := input_field_str(&app.seed_field)
219	        seed     := u64(0)
220	        ok       := false
221	        if len(seed_str) == 0 {
222	            seed, ok = _default_seed(), true
223	        } else {
224	            seed, ok = _parse_seed(seed_str)
225	        }
226	        if !ok { app.seed_err = true; return false }
227	        app.seed_err = false
228	        world_init(&app.world, seed)
229	        return true
230	    }
231	
232	    // Back button → Title
233	    if _draw_back_btn() {
234	        app.screen = .Title
235	    }
236	    if _draw_exit_btn() || rl.IsKeyPressed(.ESCAPE) {
237	        rl.CloseWindow()
238	    }
239	    return false
240	}

242	// ---- Character dot colour --------------------------------------------------
243	
244	_char_color :: proc(c: ^Character) -> rl.Color {
245	    return char_palette_color(c.sprite)
246	}
247	
248	// ---- Grid-to-screen coordinate mapping ------------------------------------
249	
250	_grid_to_screen :: proc(gc, gr, cam_col, cam_row, view_w, view_h: i32) -> (px, py: i32, visible: bool) {
251	    cpx          := render_cell_px()
252	    visible_cols := view_w  / cpx + 2
253	    visible_rows := view_h / cpx + 2
254	    start_col    := cam_col - visible_cols / 2
255	    start_row    := cam_row - visible_rows / 2
256	    dc           := gc - start_col
257	    dr           := gr - start_row
258	    if dc < 0 || dc >= visible_cols || dr < 0 || dr >= visible_rows {
259	        return 0, 0, false
260	    }
261	    return dc * cpx, dr * cpx, true
262	}
263	
264	// ---- Hover arrow query (c-callback, no context) ----------------------------
265	
266	MAX_ARROW_TAG :: 24
267	
268	_HoverRelEntry :: struct {
269	    id  : u32,
270	    tag : [MAX_ARROW_TAG]u8,
271	}
272	
273	_HoverRelAccum :: struct {
274	    entries : [MAX_RESIDENTS]_HoverRelEntry,
275	    count   : int,
276	}
277	
278	_hover_rel_cb :: proc "c" (soln: ^EpilogSolution, ud: rawptr) -> EpilogSignal {
279	    acc := cast(^_HoverRelAccum)ud
280	    if acc.count >= MAX_RESIDENTS do return .EPILOG_STOP
281	
282	    other_atom: cstring
283	    tag_atom  : cstring
284	
285	    for i in 0..<epilog_soln_var_count(soln) {
286	        vname := epilog_soln_var_name(soln, i)
287	        val   := epilog_soln_var_atom(soln, i)
288	        if vname == nil || val == nil do continue
289	        switch string(vname) {
290	        case "Other": other_atom = val
291	        case "Tag":   tag_atom   = val
292	        }
293	    }
294	
295	    if other_atom != nil {
296	        other_str := string(other_atom)
297	        other_id  := u32(0)
298	        if len(other_str) > 5 && other_str[:5] == "char_" {
299	            for ch in other_str[5:] {
300	                if ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' {
301	                    other_id = other_id * 10 + u32(ch - '0')
302	                }
303	            }
304	        }
305	        idx := acc.count
306	        acc.entries[idx].id = other_id
307	        if tag_atom != nil {
308	            ts := string(tag_atom)
309	            n  := min(len(ts), MAX_ARROW_TAG - 1)
310	            copy(acc.entries[idx].tag[:], ts[:n])
311	            acc.entries[idx].tag[n] = 0
312	        }
313	        acc.count += 1
314	    }
315	    return .EPILOG_NEXT
316	}
317	
318	// Also query crush/2 and rival state for additional arrow colours
319	_CrushAccum :: struct {
320	    ids   : [MAX_RESIDENTS]u32,
321	    count : int,
322	}
323	
324	_crush_cb :: proc "c" (soln: ^EpilogSolution, ud: rawptr) -> EpilogSignal {
325	    acc := cast(^_CrushAccum)ud
326	    if acc.count >= MAX_RESIDENTS do return .EPILOG_STOP
327	    for i in 0..<epilog_soln_var_count(soln) {
328	        vname := epilog_soln_var_name(soln, i)
329	        val   := epilog_soln_var_atom(soln, i)
330	        if vname == nil || val == nil do continue
331	        if string(vname) == "Y" {
332	            other_str := string(val)
333	            other_id  := u32(0)
334	            if len(other_str) > 5 && other_str[:5] == "char_" {
335	                for ch in other_str[5:] {
336	                    if ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' {
337	                        other_id = other_id * 10 + u32(ch - '0')
338	                    }
339	                }
340	            }
341	            acc.ids[acc.count] = other_id
342	            acc.count += 1
343	        }
344	    }
345	    return .EPILOG_NEXT
346	}
347	
348	_arrow_color_for_tag :: proc(tag: string) -> rl.Color {
349	    switch tag {
350	    case "crush", "crushing": return rl.Color{255, 150, 180, 200}  // pink
351	    case "friend":            return rl.Color{80,  200, 80,  200}  // green
352	    case "acquaintance":      return rl.Color{60,  180, 180, 200}  // teal
353	    case "sibling":           return rl.Color{80,  120, 240, 200}  // blue
354	    case "partner":           return rl.Color{220, 180, 255, 200}  // lavender
355	    case "rivals":            return rl.Color{220, 60,  60,  200}  // red
356	    }
357	    return rl.Color{200, 200, 60, 140}  // default yellow
358	}
359	
360	_draw_hover_arrows :: proc(app: ^App, hovered_char: ^Character) {
361	    buf_id, goal_buf: [160]u8
362	    atom := character_atom(hovered_char.id, buf_id[:])
363	
364	    // Query relationship/3 for typed arrows
365	    gs := fmt.bprintf(goal_buf[:], "relationship(%s, Other, Tag)", atom)
366	    goal_buf[len(gs)] = 0
367	
368	    rel_acc := _HoverRelAccum{}
369	    epilog_query(app.engine, cstring(raw_data(goal_buf[:])), _hover_rel_cb, &rel_acc)
370	
371	    // Also query crush(atom, Y) for pink arrows
372	    crush_acc := _CrushAccum{}
373	    gs2 := fmt.bprintf(goal_buf[:], "crush(%s, Y)", atom)
374	    goal_buf[len(gs2)] = 0
375	    epilog_query(app.engine, cstring(raw_data(goal_buf[:])), _crush_cb, &crush_acc)
376	
377	    // rivals(atom, Y)
378	    rival_acc := _CrushAccum{}
379	    gs3 := fmt.bprintf(goal_buf[:], "rivals(%s, Y)", atom)
380	    goal_buf[len(gs3)] = 0
381	    epilog_query(app.engine, cstring(raw_data(goal_buf[:])), _crush_cb, &rival_acc)
382	
383	    hx, hy, hv := _grid_to_screen(hovered_char.position[0], hovered_char.position[1],
384	                                   app.cam_col, app.cam_row, sim_w(), sim_h())
385	    if !hv do return
386	
387	    cpx := render_cell_px()
388	    cx  := hx + cpx/2
389	    cy  := hy + cpx/2
390	
391	    // Draw relationship arrows
392	    for i in 0..<rel_acc.count {
393	        e := &rel_acc.entries[i]
394	        other := residents_find(&app.residents, e.id)
395	        if other == nil do continue
396	        ox, oy, ov := _grid_to_screen(other.position[0], other.position[1],
397	                                       app.cam_col, app.cam_row, sim_w(), sim_h())
398	        if !ov do continue
399	        tag_str := strings.truncate_to_byte(string(e.tag[:]), 0)
400	        col := _arrow_color_for_tag(tag_str)
401	        rl.DrawLine(cx, cy, ox + cpx/2, oy + cpx/2, col)
402	    }
403	
404	    // Crush arrows (pink) — may already be covered, but ensures crushes show
405	    for i in 0..<crush_acc.count {
406	        other := residents_find(&app.residents, crush_acc.ids[i])
407	        if other == nil do continue
408	        ox, oy, ov := _grid_to_screen(other.position[0], other.position[1],
409	                                       app.cam_col, app.cam_row, sim_w(), sim_h())
410	        if !ov do continue
411	        rl.DrawLine(cx, cy, ox + cpx/2, oy + cpx/2, rl.Color{255, 130, 180, 220})
412	    }
413	
414	    // Rival arrows (red dashed — approximate with two short segments)
415	    for i in 0..<rival_acc.count {
416	        other := residents_find(&app.residents, rival_acc.ids[i])
417	        if other == nil do continue
418	        ox, oy, ov := _grid_to_screen(other.position[0], other.position[1],
419	                                       app.cam_col, app.cam_row, sim_w(), sim_h())
420	        if !ov do continue
421	        // Dashed: draw 3 segments
422	        dx := (ox + cpx/2) - cx
423	        dy := (oy + cpx/2) - cy
424	        for seg in 0..<3 {
425	            t0 := f32(seg) / 5.0
426	            t1 := f32(seg) / 5.0 + 0.12
427	            rl.DrawLine(cx + i32(f32(dx)*t0), cy + i32(f32(dy)*t0),
428	                        cx + i32(f32(dx)*t1), cy + i32(f32(dy)*t1),
429	                        rl.Color{220, 50, 50, 220})
430	        }
431	    }
432	}
433	
434	// ---- Draw simulation view into sim_tex (60 fps) ----------------------------
435	
436	_draw_sim_view :: proc(app: ^App) {
437	    rl.BeginTextureMode(app.sim_tex)
438	    rl.ClearBackground(COL_BG)
439	
440	    cpx := render_cell_px()
441	    grid_render(&app.world.grid, 0, 0, sim_w(), sim_h(), app.cam_col, app.cam_row, cpx)
442	
443	    mouse          := rl.GetMousePosition()
444	    app.hovered_id  = 0
445	
446	    for i in 0..<app.residents.count {
447	        c  := &app.residents.chars[i]
448	        px, py, vis := _grid_to_screen(c.position[0], c.position[1],
449	                                        app.cam_col, app.cam_row, sim_w(), sim_h())
450	        if !vis do continue
451	
452	        col := _char_color(c)
453	        draw_char_dot_animated(c, px, py, col, app.anim_time, cpx)
454	
455	        // Mood icon above sprite
456	        ia := int(c.id - 1)
457	        if ia >= 0 && ia < MAX_RESIDENTS {
458	            draw_mood_icon(app.sim.social[ia].mood, px, py, app.anim_time, cpx)
459	        }
460	
461	        dot_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(px) - 4, f32(py) - 4,
462	                                  f32(cpx) + 8, f32(cpx) + 8}
463	        if rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(mouse, dot_rect) {
464	            app.hovered_id = c.id
465	        }
466	    }
467	
468	    if app.hovered_id != 0 {
469	        hov_char := residents_find(&app.residents, app.hovered_id)
470	        if hov_char != nil {
471	            _draw_hover_arrows(app, hov_char)
472	            hx, hy, _ := _grid_to_screen(hov_char.position[0], hov_char.position[1],
473	                                           app.cam_col, app.cam_row, sim_w(), sim_h())
474	            name_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(character_name(hov_char), context.temp_allocator)
475	            rl.DrawText(name_cstr, hx + 6, hy - 18, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)
476	            ia := int(hov_char.id - 1)
477	            if ia >= 0 && ia < MAX_RESIDENTS {
478	                mood_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(mood_label(app.sim.social[ia].mood), context.temp_allocator)
479	                rl.DrawText(mood_cstr, hx + 6, hy - 2, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
480	            }
481	        }
482	    }
483	
484	    // Dialogue bubbles
485	    bubble_system_draw(&app.bubbles, &app.residents, app.cam_col, app.cam_row, cpx)
486	
487	    // Weather effects (before overlay)
488	    switch app.weather.current {
489	    case .Rain: weather_draw_rain(app.anim_time, app.cam_col, app.cam_row)
490	    case .Fog:  weather_draw_fog()
491	    case .Wind: weather_draw_wind(app.anim_time, &app.world.grid, app.cam_col, app.cam_row, cpx)
492	    case .Clear: // nothing
493	    }
494	
495	    // Day / night overlay
496	    dn_col := day_night_overlay(app.world.tick, app.sim_timer)
497	    if dn_col.a > 0 {
498	        rl.DrawRectangle(0, 0, sim_w(), sim_h(), dn_col)
499	    }
500	    day_night_draw_stars(app.world.tick, app.cam_col, app.cam_row, &app.world.grid)
501	
502	    // Zone labels
503	    zone_labels_draw(&app.zone_labels, app.cam_col, app.cam_row, rl.GetFrameTime())
504	
505	    rl.DrawText("WASD: pan   hover: inspect   F5: save   F9: load   Space: pause   +/-: speed",
506	                8, sim_h() - 20, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
507	    rl.EndTextureMode()
508	}

510	// ---- Colour-coded event text -----------------------------------------------
511	// Scans a string for known character names and draws each token in the
512	// character's palette colour, drawing other text in COL_TEXT.
513	
514	_draw_event_coloured :: proc(app: ^App, text: string, x, y: i32) {
515	    cx := x
516	    i  := 0
517	    for i < len(text) {
518	        // Try to match a character name at position i
519	        matched_len    := 0
520	        matched_color  := COL_TEXT
521	
522	        for ci in 0..<app.residents.count {
523	            c    := &app.residents.chars[ci]
524	            name := character_name(c)
525	            if i + len(name) <= len(text) && text[i:i+len(name)] == name {
526	                if matched_len < len(name) {
527	                    matched_len   = len(name)
528	                    matched_color = _char_color(c)
529	                }
530	            }
531	        }
532	
533	        if matched_len > 0 {
534	            seg  := strings.clone_to_cstring(text[i:i+matched_len], context.temp_allocator)
535	            rl.DrawText(seg, cx, y, UI_SMALL, matched_color)
536	            cx += rl.MeasureText(seg, UI_SMALL)
537	            i  += matched_len
538	        } else {
539	            // Advance until next potential name start or end of string
540	            j := i + 1
541	            for j < len(text) {
542	                found := false
543	                for ci in 0..<app.residents.count {
544	                    c    := &app.residents.chars[ci]
545	                    name := character_name(c)
546	                    if j + len(name) <= len(text) && text[j:j+len(name)] == name {
547	                        found = true
548	                        break
549	                    }
550	                }
551	                if found do break
552	                j += 1
553	            }
554	            seg  := strings.clone_to_cstring(text[i:j], context.temp_allocator)
555	            rl.DrawText(seg, cx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)
556	            cx += rl.MeasureText(seg, UI_SMALL)
557	            i   = j
558	        }
559	    }
560	}

563	
564	_draw_stat_panel :: proc(app: ^App) {

609	    sl3 := fmt.bprintf(stat_lines[3][:], "Rivals:    %d pairs", app.sim.rival_count)

611	    sl4 := fmt.bprintf(stat_lines[4][:], "Weather:   %s (~%d ticks)",
612	        weather_label(app.weather.current), app.weather.ticks_remaining)
613	    stat_lines[4][len(sl4)] = 0
614	    // Day/night phase label
615	    phase_str := ""
616	    switch day_phase_for_tick(app.world.tick) {
617	    case .Dawn:  phase_str = "Dawn"
618	    case .Day:   phase_str = "Day"
619	    case .Dusk:  phase_str = "Dusk"
620	    case .Night: phase_str = "Night"
621	    }
622	    sl5 := fmt.bprintf(stat_lines[5][:], "Time:      %s", phase_str)

628	    y += 8
629

631	    y += 10
632	
633	    rl.DrawText("RESIDENTS", lx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_ACCENT)
634	    y += 20
635	
636	    for i in 0..<app.residents.count {

642	        if ia >= 0 && ia < MAX_RESIDENTS {

644	            rl.DrawText(mood_cstr, lx + 110, y, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
645	        }
646	        y += 18
647	    }
648	    if app.residents.count == 0 {
649	        rl.DrawText("none yet", lx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
650	        y += 18
651	    }
652	    y += 8
653	
654	    rl.DrawLine(lx, y, sw - lx, y, COL_MUTED)

656	
657	    rl.DrawText("EVENTS", lx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_ACCENT)

659	
660	    MAX_SHOWN :: 8

699	    rl.DrawText("Residents ->", lx + 8, y + 7, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)

705	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(exit_rect, hov_exit ? rl.Color{180, 40, 40, 255} : rl.Color{80, 30, 30, 200})

711	// ---- Stat sidebar button clicks (window/composite coords) -------------------
712	
713	_handle_stat_clicks :: proc(app: ^App) {
714	    ox := stats_x() + i32(UI_PAD)
715	
716	    // Speed buttons at (UI_PAD + 20 + 28) from top of stat panel
717	    speed_y    := i32(UI_PAD) + 20 + 0   // ISLAND STATS header + 20
718	    speed_bw   := i32(56)
719	    for si in 0..<4 {
720	        br := rl.Rectangle{f32(ox + i32(si) * (speed_bw + 2)), f32(speed_y), f32(speed_bw), 22}
721	        if rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), br) &&
722	           rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
723	            app.sim_speed = si
724	        }
725	    }
726	
727	    res_rows  := i32(app.residents.count)
728	    res_empty := app.residents.count == 0 ? i32(18) : i32(0)
729	    evt_shown := i32(min(app.sim.log.count, 8))
730	    evt_empty := app.sim.log.count == 0 ? i32(18) : i32(0)
731	
732	    // Reconstruct y position of the Residents → button (must match _draw_stat_panel layout)
733	    // Now has extra 28px for speed buttons row, 2 extra stat lines, and minimap block
734	    base_y := i32(UI_PAD) +
735	        i32(20 + 28 + 24 + 20 + 18*6 + 8) +   // ISLAND STATS block (speed row + 6 stat lines)
736	        i32(10 + 20) +                           // divider + RESIDENTS header
737	        i32(18)*res_rows + res_empty + i32(8) +
738	        i32(10 + 10 + 20) +                      // divider + EVENTS header
739	        i32(18)*evt_shown + evt_empty + i32(8) +
740	        i32(10 + 18 + MINIMAP_H + 10 + 10 + 10 + 20)  // MAP section + divider + NAVIGATE header
741	
742	    // Resident rows in the sidebar (for backstory on click)
743	    res_list_y := i32(UI_PAD) + 20 + 28 + 24 + 20 + 18*6 + 8 + 10 + 20
744	    for i in 0..<app.residents.count {
745	        row_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(ox), f32(res_list_y + i32(i)*18), 200, 18}
746	        if rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), row_rect) &&
747	           rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
748	            backstory_open(&app.backstory, app.residents.chars[i].id,
749	                           &app.residents, app.engine)
750	        }
751	    }
752	
753	    res_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(ox), f32(base_y), 160, 30}
754	    if rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), res_rect) &&
755	       rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
756	        app.screen = .Residents
757	        app.resident_list = {}
758	    }
759	
760	    exit_y    := base_y + 40
761	    exit_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(ox), f32(exit_y), 120, 30}
762	    if rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), exit_rect) &&
763	       rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
764	        rl.CloseWindow()
765	    }
766	}

768	// ---- Cutscene check — trigger when new event appears -----------------------
769	
770	_last_event_count: int
771	
772	_check_cutscene :: proc(app: ^App) {
773	    if app.sim.log.count > _last_event_count {
774	        e := event_log_get(&app.sim.log, 0)
775	        if e != nil {
776	            cutscene_trigger(&app.cutscene, event_entry_str(e))
777	        }
778	        _last_event_count = app.sim.log.count
779	    }
780	}
781	
782	// ---- Simulation update + composite -----------------------------------------
783	
784	_update_simulation :: proc(app: ^App) {
785	    dt := rl.GetFrameTime()
786	    app.anim_time += dt
787	
788	    // ESC → quit
789	    if rl.IsKeyPressed(.ESCAPE) {
790	        rl.CloseWindow()
791	        return
792	    }
793	
794	    // WASD pan
795	    if rl.IsKeyDown(.W) { app.cam_row -= 2 }
796	    if rl.IsKeyDown(.S) { app.cam_row += 2 }
797	    if rl.IsKeyDown(.A) { app.cam_col -= 2 }
798	    if rl.IsKeyDown(.D) { app.cam_col += 2 }
799	    app.cam_col = clamp(app.cam_col, 0, GRID_W - 1)
800	    app.cam_row = clamp(app.cam_row, 0, GRID_H - 1)
801	
802	    // F5 = save, F9 = load
803	    if rl.IsKeyPressed(.F5) {
804	        _do_save(app)
805	    }
806	    if rl.IsKeyPressed(.F9) {
807	        if _do_load(app) {
808	            _draw_stat_panel(app)
809	        }
810	    }
811	
812	    // Speed keyboard shortcuts
813	    if rl.IsKeyPressed(.SPACE) {
814	        if app.sim_speed == 0 { app.sim_speed = 1 } else { app.sim_speed = 0 }
815	    }
816	    if rl.IsKeyPressed(.KP_ADD) || rl.IsKeyPressed(.EQUAL) {
817	        if app.sim_speed < 3 { app.sim_speed += 1 }
818	    }
819	    if rl.IsKeyPressed(.KP_SUBTRACT) || rl.IsKeyPressed(.MINUS) {
820	        if app.sim_speed > 1 { app.sim_speed -= 1 }
821	    }
822	
823	    // Sim tick — rate depends on sim_speed
824	    // Speed 0=paused, 1=1Hz, 2=4Hz, 3=16Hz
825	    _SPEED_HZ := [4]f32{0, 1.0, 4.0, 16.0}
826	    tick_hz := _SPEED_HZ[app.sim_speed]
827	    tick_interval := tick_hz > 0 ? 1.0 / tick_hz : 1e9
828	
829	    app.sim_timer += dt
830	    for app.sim_speed > 0 && app.sim_timer >= tick_interval {
831	        app.sim_timer -= tick_interval
832	        world_tick_wander(&app.world, &app.residents)
833	        sim_tick(&app.sim, &app.residents, app.engine, app.world.tick)
834	        weather_tick(&app.weather, &app.sim.rng)
835	        friendship_decay_tick(&app.decay, &app.residents, app.engine, &app.sim, app.world.tick)
836	        confession_tick(&app.confession, &app.sim, &app.residents, app.engine, app.world.tick)
837	        confrontation_tick(&app.confrontation, &app.sim, &app.residents, app.engine, app.world.tick)
838	        _check_cutscene(app)
839	
840	        // Auto-save every 60 ticks
841	        if app.world.tick - app.last_autosave_tick >= 60 {
842	            _do_save(app)
843	            app.last_autosave_tick = app.world.tick
844	        }
845	    }
846	
847	    // Redraw at appropriate rates
848	    _draw_sim_view(app)
849	    app.stat_timer += dt
850	    if app.stat_timer >= STAT_RATE {
851	        app.stat_timer = 0
852	        _draw_stat_panel(app)
853	    }
854	
855	    // Composite — 80/20 side-by-side
856	    rl.ClearBackground(rl.BLACK)
857	
858	    // Left: grid (80%)
859	    src_sim := rl.Rectangle{0, 0, f32(sim_w()), -f32(sim_h())}
860	    dst_sim := rl.Rectangle{0, 0, f32(sim_w()),  f32(sim_h())}
861	    rl.DrawTexturePro(app.sim_tex.texture, src_sim, dst_sim, {0, 0}, 0, rl.WHITE)
862	
863	    // Right: sidebar (20%)
864	    src_stat := rl.Rectangle{0, 0, f32(stat_w()), -f32(stat_h())}
865	    dst_stat := rl.Rectangle{f32(stats_x()), 0, f32(stat_w()), f32(stat_h())}
866	    rl.DrawTexturePro(app.stat_tex.texture, src_stat, dst_stat, {0, 0}, 0, rl.WHITE)
867	
868	    // Dividing line
869	    rl.DrawLine(stats_x(), 0, stats_x(), SH(), COL_MUTED)
870	
871	    _handle_stat_clicks(app)
872	
873	    // Tier 3: update bubbles + draw prompts
874	    bubble_system_update(&app.bubbles, dt)
875	    // Note: bubbles are drawn inside _draw_sim_view pass already via the
876	    // bubble_system_draw call — we just update timing here.
877	
878	    // Confession prompt
879	    if app.confession.state == .Waiting {
880	        conf_res := confession_draw_prompt(&app.confession, &app.residents, dt)
881	        if conf_res != 0 {
882	            confession_resolve(&app.confession, conf_res, &app.sim, &app.residents,
883	                               app.engine, app.world.tick)
884	        } else if app.confession.timer <= 0 {
885	            confession_resolve(&app.confession, 0, &app.sim, &app.residents,
886	                               app.engine, app.world.tick)
887	        }
888	    }
889	
890	    // Confrontation prompt
891	    if app.confrontation.state == .Waiting {
892	        res2 := confrontation_draw_prompt(&app.confrontation, &app.residents, dt)
893	        if res2 != 0 {
894	            confrontation_resolve(res2, app.confrontation.char_a, app.confrontation.char_b,
895	                                   &app.sim, &app.residents, app.engine, app.world.tick)
896	        }
897	    }
898	
899	    // Backstory panel (drawn on top of everything)
900	    backstory_draw(&app.backstory, &app.residents, &app.sim, &app.sim.log)
901	
902	    // M8 cutscene overlay on top of everything
903	    cutscene_update(&app.cutscene)
904	}
