906	// ---- Main ------------------------------------------------------------------
907	
908	main :: proc() {
909	    rl.SetConfigFlags({.WINDOW_RESIZABLE})
910	    g_screen.w = SCREEN_W
911	    g_screen.h = SCREEN_H
912	    rl.InitWindow(SCREEN_W, SCREEN_H, "DMLS — Deb's Minimal Life Simulator")
913	    rl.SetTargetFPS(60)
914	    defer rl.CloseWindow()
915	
916	    app := new(App)
917	    defer free(app)
918	
919	    app.engine = epilog_create()
920	    defer epilog_destroy(app.engine)
921	
922	    residents_init(&app.residents)
923	    app.screen    = .Resolution
924	    app.cam_col   = GRID_W / 2
925	    app.cam_row   = GRID_H / 2
926	    app.title_rng = 0xFEEDBEEF_CAFEBABE
927	    app.sim_speed = 1
928	    weather_init(&app.weather)
929	    g_decay   = &app.decay
930	    g_bubbles = &app.bubbles
931	
932	    {
933	        def := _default_seed()
934	        ds  := fmt.bprintf(app.seed_field.buf[:], "%016X", def)
935	        app.seed_field.len = len(ds)
936	        app.seed_field.active = true
937	    }
938	
939	    _load_all_rules(app)
940	    resolution_picker_init(&app.res_picker)
941	
942	    // Allocate RenderTextures at maximum possible sizes.
943	    // sim_tex: widest sim viewport = 80% of 1920 = 1536, tallest = 1080.
944	    // stat_tex: widest sidebar = 20% of 1920 = 384, tallest = 1080.
945	    app.sim_tex  = rl.LoadRenderTexture(SCREEN_W * 80 / 100, SCREEN_H)
946	    app.stat_tex = rl.LoadRenderTexture(SCREEN_W - SCREEN_W * 80 / 100, SCREEN_H)
947	    app.rt_ready = true
948	    defer rl.UnloadRenderTexture(app.sim_tex)
949	    defer rl.UnloadRenderTexture(app.stat_tex)
950	
951	    title_screen_init(&app.title)
952	
953	    for !rl.WindowShouldClose() {
954	        rl.BeginDrawing()
955	        free_all(context.temp_allocator)
956	
957	        switch app.screen {
958	
959	        case .Resolution:
960	            if resolution_picker_update(&app.res_picker) {
961	                app.screen = .Title
962	            }
963	            // Exit button on resolution picker
964	            if _draw_exit_btn() || rl.IsKeyPressed(.ESCAPE) {
965	                rl.CloseWindow()
966	            }
967	
968	        case .Title:
969	            result := title_screen_update(&app.title, &app.island_name, &app.title_rng)
970	            switch result {
971	            case 1:
972	                app.screen = .World_Setup
973	            case 2:
974	                if _do_load(app) {
975	                    app.screen = .Simulation
976	                } else {
977	                    app.title.load_err   = true
978	                    app.title.load_err_t = 3.0
979	                }
980	            }
981	            // Exit button on title screen
982	            if _draw_exit_btn() || rl.IsKeyPressed(.ESCAPE) {
983	                rl.CloseWindow()
984	            }
985	
986	        case .World_Setup:
987	            // Back + exit drawn inside _draw_world_setup
988	            if _draw_world_setup(app) {
989	                zone_labels_seed(&app.zone_labels, &app.world.grid)
990	                starters_seed(&app.residents, &app.world, app.engine)
991	                sim_init(&app.sim, app.world.seed)
992	                app.screen = .Residents
993	            }
994	
995	        case .Residents:
996	            if resident_list_update(&app.resident_list, &app.residents, app.engine) {
997	                creation_screen_init(&app.creation)
998	                app.screen = .Creation
999	            }
1000	            // "Simulate ->" button
1001	            sim_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(SW() - 200), f32(SH() - 60), 180, 42}
1002	            hov_sim  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), sim_rect)
1003	            if app.residents.count > 0 {
1004	                rl.DrawRectangleRec(sim_rect, hov_sim ? COL_ACCENT2 : COL_ACCENT)
1005	                rl.DrawText("Simulate ->", SW() - 188, SH() - 46, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
1006	                if hov_sim && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
1007	                    app.screen = .Simulation
1008	                    _draw_stat_panel(app)
1009	                }
1010	            }
1011	            // Back → World_Setup
1012	            if _draw_back_btn() {
1013	                app.screen = .World_Setup
1014	            }
1015	            if _draw_exit_btn() || rl.IsKeyPressed(.ESCAPE) {
1016	                rl.CloseWindow()
1017	            }
1018	
1019	        case .Creation:
1020	            new_char: Character
1021	            if creation_screen_update(&app.creation, &new_char) {
1022	                app.pending_char = new_char
1023	                app.pending_id   = residents_add(&app.residents, new_char)
1024	                epilog_assert_character(app.engine, residents_find(&app.residents, app.pending_id))
1025	                world_spawn_character(&app.world, &app.residents, app.pending_id)
1026	                rel_seeding_init(&app.rel_seeding, app.pending_id, &app.residents)
1027	                app.screen = .Rel_Seeding
1028	            }
1029	            // Back → Residents
1030	            if _draw_back_btn() {
1031	                app.screen = .Residents
1032	            }
1033	            if _draw_exit_btn() || rl.IsKeyPressed(.ESCAPE) {
1034	                rl.CloseWindow()
1035	            }
1036	
1037	        case .Rel_Seeding:
1038	            if rel_seeding_update(&app.rel_seeding, &app.residents) {
1039	                rel_seeding_commit(&app.rel_seeding, &app.residents, app.engine)
1040	                app.screen = .Residents
1041	                app.resident_list = {}
1042	            }
1043	            // Back → Residents (discards pending character)
1044	            if _draw_back_btn() {
1045	                app.screen = .Residents
1046	            }
1047	            if _draw_exit_btn() || rl.IsKeyPressed(.ESCAPE) {
1048	                rl.CloseWindow()
1049	            }
1050	
1051	        case .Simulation:
1052	            _update_simulation(app)
1053	        }
1054	
1055	        rl.EndDrawing()
1056	    }
1057	}
1058

1	package dmls
2	
3	import "core:fmt"
4	import "core:strings"
5	import "core:c"
6	
7	// ---- Enumerations ----------------------------------------------------------
8	
9	Gender :: enum u8 {
10	    Male,
11	    Female,
12	    Nonbinary,
13	}
14	
15	DatingPref :: enum u8 {
16	    Male,
17	    Female,
18	    Any,
19	    None,
20	}
21	
22	Pronouns :: enum u8 {
23	    He_Him,
24	    She_Her,
25	    They_Them,
26	}
27	
28	Personality :: enum u8 {
29	    Ambitious_Achiever,
30	    Outgoing_Merrymaker,
31	    Outgoing_Dynamo,
32	    Laid_Back_Guy,
33	    Sweetie,
34	    Cranky,
35	}
36	
37	RelationshipTag :: enum u8 {
38	    Stranger,
39	    Acquaintance,
40	    Friend,
41	    Sibling,
42	    Partner,
43	}
44	
45	// ---- Character struct ------------------------------------------------------
46	
47	MAX_NAME_LEN  :: 32
48	MAX_RESIDENTS :: 64
49	
50	Character :: struct {
51	    id          : u32,
52	    name        : [MAX_NAME_LEN]u8,
53	    nickname    : [MAX_NAME_LEN]u8,
54	    age         : u8,
55	    gender      : Gender,
56	    dating_pref : DatingPref,
57	    personality : Personality,
58	    pronouns    : Pronouns,
59	    position    : [2]i32,
60	    sprite      : u32,
61	}
62	
63	character_name :: proc(c: ^Character) -> string {
64	    return strings.truncate_to_byte(string(c.name[:]), 0)
65	}
66	
67	character_nickname :: proc(c: ^Character) -> string {
68	    return strings.truncate_to_byte(string(c.nickname[:]), 0)
69	}
70	
71	character_atom :: proc(id: u32, buf: []u8) -> cstring {
72	    s := fmt.bprintf(buf, "char_%d", id)
73	    buf[len(s)] = 0
74	    return cstring(raw_data(buf))
75	}
76	
77	// ---- Resident registry -----------------------------------------------------
78	
79	Residents :: struct {
80	    chars  : [MAX_RESIDENTS]Character,
81	    count  : u32,
82	    next_id: u32,
83	    rel    : [MAX_RESIDENTS][MAX_RESIDENTS]RelationshipTag,
84	}
85	
86	residents_init :: proc(r: ^Residents) {
87	    r^ = {}
88	    r.next_id = 1
89	}
90	
91	residents_add :: proc(r: ^Residents, c: Character) -> u32 {
92	    assert(r.count < MAX_RESIDENTS)
93	    idx := r.count
94	    r.chars[idx] = c
95	    r.chars[idx].id = r.next_id
96	    r.next_id += 1
97	    r.count += 1
98	    return r.chars[idx].id
99	}
100	
101	residents_find :: proc(r: ^Residents, id: u32) -> ^Character {
102	    for i in 0..<r.count {
103	        if r.chars[i].id == id do return &r.chars[i]
104	    }
105	    return nil
106	}
107	
108	// ---- Display helpers -------------------------------------------------------
109	
110	gender_label :: proc(g: Gender) -> string {
111	    switch g {
112	    case .Male:      return "Male"
113	    case .Female:    return "Female"
114	    case .Nonbinary: return "Nonbinary"
115	    }
116	    return ""
117	}
118	
119	dating_pref_label :: proc(d: DatingPref) -> string {
120	    switch d {
121	    case .Male:   return "Men"
122	    case .Female: return "Women"
123	    case .Any:    return "Anyone"
124	    case .None:   return "Nobody"
125	    }
126	    return ""
127	}
128	
129	pronouns_label :: proc(p: Pronouns) -> string {
130	    switch p {
131	    case .He_Him:    return "he/him"
132	    case .She_Her:   return "she/her"
133	    case .They_Them: return "they/them"
134	    }
135	    return ""
136	}
137	
138	personality_label :: proc(p: Personality) -> string {
139	    switch p {
140	    case .Ambitious_Achiever:  return "Ambitious Achiever"
141	    case .Outgoing_Merrymaker: return "Outgoing Merrymaker"
142	    case .Outgoing_Dynamo:     return "Outgoing Dynamo"
143	    case .Laid_Back_Guy:       return "Laid-Back Guy"
144	    case .Sweetie:             return "Sweetie"
145	    case .Cranky:              return "Cranky"
146	    }
147	    return ""
148	}
149	
150	personality_atom :: proc(p: Personality) -> cstring {
151	    switch p {
152	    case .Ambitious_Achiever:  return "ambitious_achiever"
153	    case .Outgoing_Merrymaker: return "outgoing_merrymaker"
154	    case .Outgoing_Dynamo:     return "outgoing_dynamo"
155	    case .Laid_Back_Guy:       return "laid_back_guy"
156	    case .Sweetie:             return "sweetie"
157	    case .Cranky:              return "cranky"
158	    }
159	    return "unknown"
160	}
161	
162	dating_pref_atom :: proc(d: DatingPref) -> cstring {
163	    switch d {
164	    case .Male:   return "male"
165	    case .Female: return "female"
166	    case .Any:    return "any"
167	    case .None:   return "none"
168	    }
169	    return "none"
170	}
171	
172	relationship_atom :: proc(t: RelationshipTag) -> cstring {
173	    switch t {
174	    case .Stranger:     return "stranger"
175	    case .Acquaintance: return "acquaintance"
176	    case .Friend:       return "friend"
177	    case .Sibling:      return "sibling"
178	    case .Partner:      return "partner"
179	    }
180	    return "stranger"
181	}
182	
183	// ---- Assert character into Epilog ------------------------------------------
184	
185	epilog_assert_character :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, c: ^Character) {
186	    buf_id : [32]u8
187	    buf_src: [256]u8
188	
189	    atom := character_atom(c.id, buf_id[:])
190	
191	    gender_str: cstring
192	    switch c.gender {
193	    case .Male:      gender_str = "male"
194	    case .Female:    gender_str = "female"
195	    case .Nonbinary: gender_str = "nonbinary"
196	    }
197	
198	    s := fmt.bprintf(buf_src[:], "character(%s, %s).", atom, personality_atom(c.personality))
199	    buf_src[len(s)] = 0
200	    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf_src[:])))
201	
202	    s = fmt.bprintf(buf_src[:], "dating_pref(%s, %s).", atom, dating_pref_atom(c.dating_pref))
203	    buf_src[len(s)] = 0
204	    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf_src[:])))
205	
206	    s = fmt.bprintf(buf_src[:], "gender(%s, %s).", atom, gender_str)
207	    buf_src[len(s)] = 0
208	    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf_src[:])))
209	}
210	
211	epilog_assert_relationship :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, id_a, id_b: u32, tag: RelationshipTag) {
212	    buf_a  : [32]u8
213	    buf_b  : [32]u8
214	    buf_src: [128]u8
215	
216	    atom_a := character_atom(id_a, buf_a[:])
217	    atom_b := character_atom(id_b, buf_b[:])
218	
219	    s := fmt.bprintf(buf_src[:], "relationship(%s, %s, %s).", atom_a, atom_b, relationship_atom(tag))
220	    buf_src[len(s)] = 0
221	    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf_src[:])))
222	}
223

1	/*
2	 * social_events.odin — Tier 3 social depth features:
3	 *
4	 *   3.1 Dialogue bubbles — short speech bubbles on character interaction
5	 *   3.2 Crush confession — Accept/Reject prompt + auto-resolve
6	 *   3.3 Rival confrontation — Let it happen / Intervene prompt
7	 *   3.4 Friendship decay — friend→acquaintance→stranger over idle ticks
8	 *   3.5 Character backstory panel — full detail view on click
9	 */
10	
11	package dmls
12	
13	import rl      "vendor:raylib"
14	import "core:fmt"
15	import "core:strings"
16	
17	// ============================================================
18	// 3.1  Dialogue Bubbles
19	// ============================================================
20	
21	MAX_BUBBLES :: 8
22	
23	DialogueBubble :: struct {
24	    char_id : u32,
25	    msg     : [48]u8,
26	    msg_len : int,
27	    timer   : f32,       // seconds remaining
28	}
29	
30	BubbleSystem :: struct {
31	    bubbles : [MAX_BUBBLES]DialogueBubble,
32	    count   : int,
33	}
34	
35	_FRIEND_LINES := []string{
36	    "Did you eat today?",
37	    "I found something interesting.",
38	    "Walk with me?",
39	    "Good to see you.",
40	}
41	
42	_CRUSH_LINES := []string{
43	    "Oh— hi.",
44	    "...",
45	    "Hey.",
46	    "You look nice today.",
47	}
48	
49	_RIVAL_LINES := []string{""}  // rivals don't speak — just a pause
50	
51	_NICK_LINES := []string{
52	    "Sunflower.",
53	    "Hey, sunflower.",
54	    "There you are.",
55	}
56	
57	_ACQUAINTANCE_LINES := []string{
58	    "Hey.",
59	    "Oh, it's you.",
60	    "Hi.",
61	}
62	
63	_bubble_push :: proc(bs: ^BubbleSystem, id: u32, msg: string) {
64	    // Replace existing bubble for same char if any
65	    for i in 0..<bs.count {
66	        if bs.bubbles[i].char_id == id {
67	            b := &bs.bubbles[i]
68	            b.timer = 3.0
69	            n := min(len(msg), 47)
70	            copy(b.msg[:], msg[:n])
71	            b.msg[n] = 0
72	            b.msg_len = n
73	            return
74	        }
75	    }
76	    if bs.count >= MAX_BUBBLES do return
77	    b := &bs.bubbles[bs.count]
78	    b.char_id = id
79	    b.timer   = 3.0
80	    n := min(len(msg), 47)
81	    copy(b.msg[:], msg[:n])
82	    b.msg[n] = 0
83	    b.msg_len = n
84	    bs.count += 1
85	}
86	
87	bubble_system_update :: proc(bs: ^BubbleSystem, dt: f32) {
88	    i := 0
89	    for i < bs.count {
90	        bs.bubbles[i].timer -= dt
91	        if bs.bubbles[i].timer <= 0 {
92	            bs.bubbles[i] = bs.bubbles[bs.count-1]
93	            bs.count -= 1
94	        } else {
95	            i += 1
96	        }
97	    }
98	}
99	
100	// Call when two characters interact (proximity tick).
101	// relationship: "friend", "acquaintance", "crush", "stranger", etc.
102	bubble_on_interact :: proc(bs: ^BubbleSystem, a, b: ^Character,
103	                           relationship: string, rng: ^DependentRNG) {
104	    lines: []string
105	    switch relationship {
106	    case "friend":       lines = _FRIEND_LINES
107	    case "crush":        lines = _CRUSH_LINES
108	    case "acquaintance": lines = _ACQUAINTANCE_LINES
109	    case:                return  // strangers don't speak
110	    }
111	    if len(lines) == 0 do return
112	    idx  := int(dependent_next(rng) * f64(len(lines))) % len(lines)
113	    _bubble_push(bs, a.id, lines[idx])
114	}
115	
116	// Draw all active bubbles.
117	bubble_system_draw :: proc(bs: ^BubbleSystem, res: ^Residents,
118	                           cam_col, cam_row, cpx: i32) {
119	    for i in 0..<bs.count {
120	        b := &bs.bubbles[i]
121	        c := residents_find(res, b.char_id)
122	        if c == nil do continue
123	
124	        px, py, vis := _grid_to_screen(c.position[0], c.position[1],
125	                                        cam_col, cam_row, sim_w(), sim_h())
126	        if !vis do continue
127	
128	        msg_str  := string(b.msg[:b.msg_len])
129	        msg_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(msg_str, context.temp_allocator)
130	        tw       := rl.MeasureText(msg_cstr, UI_SMALL - 2)
131	        bx       := px - tw/2
132	        by       := py - cpx - 22
133	        bw       := tw + 8
134	        bh       := i32(18)
135	
136	        // Fade with timer
137	        alpha := u8(clamp(int(b.timer * 80), 0, 200))
138	
139	        rl.DrawRectangle(bx - 4, by, bw, bh, rl.Color{30, 30, 50, alpha})
140	        rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(rl.Rectangle{f32(bx-4), f32(by), f32(bw), f32(bh)},
141	                                 1, rl.Color{150, 130, 200, alpha})
142	        rl.DrawText(msg_cstr, bx, by + 2, UI_SMALL - 2,
143	                    rl.Color{220, 220, 230, alpha})
144	    }
145	}
146	
147	// ============================================================
148	// 3.2  Crush Confession Event
149	// ============================================================
150	
151	ConfessionState :: enum { None, Waiting, Resolved }
152	
153	ConfessionEvent :: struct {
154	    state       : ConfessionState,
155	    from_id     : u32,
156	    on_id       : u32,
157	    timer       : f32,      // seconds before auto-resolve
158	    crush_ticks : [MAX_RESIDENTS][MAX_RESIDENTS]u64,  // tick when crush started
159	}
160	
161	confession_tick :: proc(ce: ^ConfessionEvent, sim: ^SimState, res: ^Residents,
162	                        engine: ^EpilogEngine, tick: u64) {
163	    if ce.state == .Waiting do return  // already pending player input
164	
165	    // Check all crush pairs: crush for 20+ ticks AND interaction_count > 15
166	    for i in 0..<res.count {
167	        a := &res.chars[i]
168	        ia := int(a.id - 1)
169	        if ia >= MAX_RESIDENTS || !sim.social[ia].has_crush do continue
170	
171	        // Find who they're crushing on
172	        crush_on := _find_crush_target(engine, a.id)
173	        if crush_on == 0 do continue
174	
175	        // Check ticks
176	        start_tick := ce.crush_ticks[ia][int(crush_on-1)]
177	        if start_tick == 0 {
178	            ce.crush_ticks[ia][int(crush_on-1)] = tick
179	            continue
180	        }
181	        if tick - start_tick < 20 do continue
182	        if sim.social[ia].interaction_count < 15 do continue
183	
184	        // Fire confession
185	        ce.state   = .Waiting
186	        ce.from_id = a.id
187	        ce.on_id   = crush_on
188	        ce.timer   = 10.0
189	
190	        // Log the approach
191	        b := residents_find(res, crush_on)
192	        if b != nil {
193	            msg_buf: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
194	            ms := fmt.bprintf(msg_buf[:], "%s: \"I have something to tell you.\"",
195	                character_name(a))
196	            event_log_push(&sim.log, tick, string(msg_buf[:len(ms)]))
197	        }
198	        return
199	    }
200	}
201	
202	_find_crush_target :: proc(engine: ^EpilogEngine, from_id: u32) -> u32 {
203	    buf_a, goal: [128]u8
204	    atom_a := character_atom(from_id, buf_a[:])
205	
206	    result := u32(0)
207	
208	    CrushTargetCB :: struct { target: u32 }
209	    cb_data := CrushTargetCB{}
210	
211	    crush_target_cb :: proc "c" (soln: ^EpilogSolution, ud: rawptr) -> EpilogSignal {
212	        data := cast(^CrushTargetCB)ud
213	        for i in 0..<epilog_soln_var_count(soln) {
214	            vname := epilog_soln_var_name(soln, i)
215	            val   := epilog_soln_var_atom(soln, i)
216	            if vname == nil || val == nil do continue
217	            if string(vname) == "Y" {
218	                s := string(val)
219	                if len(s) > 5 && s[:5] == "char_" {
220	                    id := u32(0)
221	                    for ch in s[5:] {
222	                        if ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' { id = id*10 + u32(ch-'0') }
223	                    }
224	                    data.target = id
225	                }
226	            }
227	        }
228	        return .EPILOG_STOP
229	    }
230	
231	    gs := fmt.bprintf(goal[:], "crush(%s, Y)", atom_a)
232	    goal[len(gs)] = 0
233	    epilog_query(engine, cstring(raw_data(goal[:])), crush_target_cb, &cb_data)
234	    result = cb_data.target
235	    return result
236	}
237	
238	// Call every frame when state == .Waiting — draws the prompt.
239	// Returns: 0=still waiting, 1=accepted, 2=rejected
240	confession_draw_prompt :: proc(ce: ^ConfessionEvent, res: ^Residents, dt: f32) -> int {
241	    if ce.state != .Waiting do return 0
242	
243	    ce.timer -= dt
244	    from := residents_find(res, ce.from_id)
245	    on   := residents_find(res, ce.on_id)
246	    if from == nil || on == nil { ce.state = .None; return 0 }
247	
248	    // Overlay
249	    rl.DrawRectangle(0, 0, SW(), SH(), rl.Color{10, 10, 20, 160})
250	
251	    cw := i32(480)
252	    ch := i32(140)
253	    cx := (SW() - cw) / 2
254	    cy := (SH() - ch) / 2
255	    rl.DrawRectangle(cx, cy, cw, ch, rl.Color{30, 28, 50, 240})
256	    rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(rl.Rectangle{f32(cx), f32(cy), f32(cw), f32(ch)},
257	                             2, rl.Color{255, 130, 180, 200})
258	
259	    // Names in their colours
260	    from_name := strings.clone_to_cstring(character_name(from), context.temp_allocator)
261	    on_name   := strings.clone_to_cstring(character_name(on),   context.temp_allocator)
262	    rl.DrawText(from_name, cx + 20, cy + 16, UI_FSIZE, char_palette_color(from.sprite))
263	    rl.DrawText(" confesses to ", cx + 20 + rl.MeasureText(from_name, UI_FSIZE), cy + 16, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
264	    rl.DrawText(on_name, cx + 20 + rl.MeasureText(from_name, UI_FSIZE) +
265	                rl.MeasureText(" confesses to ", UI_FSIZE), cy + 16, UI_FSIZE, char_palette_color(on.sprite))
266	
267	    // Timer bar
268	    bar_w := i32(f32(cw - 40) * (ce.timer / 10.0))
269	    rl.DrawRectangle(cx + 20, cy + 46, cw - 40, 4, COL_MUTED)
270	    rl.DrawRectangle(cx + 20, cy + 46, bar_w, 4, rl.Color{255, 130, 180, 255})
271	
272	    // Buttons
273	    acc_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(cx + 60), f32(cy + 70), 140, 44}
274	    rej_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(cx + 280), f32(cy + 70), 140, 44}
275	    hov_acc  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), acc_rect)
276	    hov_rej  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), rej_rect)
277	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(acc_rect, hov_acc ? COL_CONFIRM : rl.Color{40, 120, 70, 255})
278	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(rej_rect, hov_rej ? COL_ACCENT2 : rl.Color{120, 40, 60, 255})
279	    rl.DrawText("Accept", cx + 84, cy + 82, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
280	    rl.DrawText("Reject", cx + 304, cy + 82, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
281	
282	    if (hov_acc && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT)) {
283	        ce.state = .Resolved; return 1
284	    }
285	    if (hov_rej && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT)) {
286	        ce.state = .Resolved; return 2
287	    }
288	
289	    // Auto-resolve after 10 seconds based on target's mood
290	    if ce.timer <= 0 {
291	        ce.state = .Resolved
292	        on_idx := int(on.id - 1)
293	        // (caller will resolve based on mood — return 0 means timer expired)
294	        return 0
295	    }
296	    return 0
297	}
298	
299	confession_resolve :: proc(ce: ^ConfessionEvent, result: int,
300	                            sim: ^SimState, res: ^Residents,
301	                            engine: ^EpilogEngine, tick: u64) {
302	    from := residents_find(res, ce.from_id)
303	    on   := residents_find(res, ce.on_id)
304	    if from == nil || on == nil do return
305	
306	    buf_a, buf_b, src: [256]u8
307	    atom_a := character_atom(ce.from_id, buf_a[:])
308	    atom_b := character_atom(ce.on_id,   buf_b[:])
309	
310	    accepted := result == 1
311	    if result == 0 {
312	        // Auto-resolve by mood
313	        on_idx := int(on.id - 1)
314	        m := on_idx >= 0 && on_idx < MAX_RESIDENTS ? sim.social[on_idx].mood : Mood.Content
315	        accepted = m == .Lovestruck || (m == .Content && sim.rng.state & 1 == 0)
316	    }
317	
318	    if accepted {
319	        // Assert partner relationship
320	        s := fmt.bprintf(src[:], "partner(%s, %s).", atom_a, atom_b)
321	        src[len(s)] = 0
322	        epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(src[:])))
323	        s = fmt.bprintf(src[:], "partner(%s, %s).", atom_b, atom_a)
324	        src[len(s)] = 0
325	        epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(src[:])))
326	
327	        ia := int(from.id - 1)
328	        ib := int(on.id - 1)
329	        if ia >= 0 && ia < MAX_RESIDENTS { sim.social[ia].mood = .Happy }
330	        if ib >= 0 && ib < MAX_RESIDENTS { sim.social[ib].mood = .Happy }
331	
332	        msg: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
333	        ms := fmt.bprintf(msg[:], "%s and %s are now partners",
334	            character_name(from), character_name(on))
335	        event_log_push(&sim.log, tick, string(msg[:len(ms)]))
336	    } else {
337	        // Retract crush (mark as no crush)
338	        ia := int(from.id - 1)
339	        ib := int(on.id - 1)
340	        if ia >= 0 && ia < MAX_RESIDENTS {
341	            sim.social[ia].has_crush = false
342	            sim.social[ia].mood = .Sad
343	        }
344	        if ib >= 0 && ib < MAX_RESIDENTS { sim.social[ib].mood = .Content }
345	
346	        msg: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
347	        ms := fmt.bprintf(msg[:], "%s was rejected by %s",
348	            character_name(from), character_name(on))
349	        event_log_push(&sim.log, tick, string(msg[:len(ms)]))
350	    }
351	    ce.state = .None
352	}
353	
354	// ============================================================
355	// 3.3  Rival Confrontation Event
356	// ============================================================
357	
358	ConfrontationState :: enum { None, Waiting }
359	
360	ConfrontationEvent :: struct {
361	    state    : ConfrontationState,
362	    char_a   : u32,
363	    char_b   : u32,
364	    timer    : f32,
365	    cooldown : [MAX_RESIDENTS][MAX_RESIDENTS]u64,
366	}
367	
368	CONFRONT_DIST     :: 5
369	CONFRONT_MIN_TICK :: 30
370	
371	confrontation_tick :: proc(ce: ^ConfrontationEvent, sim: ^SimState,
372	                            res: ^Residents, engine: ^EpilogEngine, tick: u64) {
373	    if ce.state == .Waiting do return
374	
375	    for i in 0..<res.count {
376	        for j in i+1..<res.count {
377	            a := &res.chars[i]
378	            b := &res.chars[j]
379	            ia := int(a.id - 1)
380	            ib := int(b.id - 1)
381	            if ia >= MAX_RESIDENTS || ib >= MAX_RESIDENTS do continue
382	
383	            // Check cooldown
384	            if ce.cooldown[ia][ib] + CONFRONT_MIN_TICK > tick do continue
385	
386	            // Check rivals
387	            buf_a, buf_b2, goal: [128]u8
388	            atom_a := character_atom(a.id, buf_a[:])
389	            atom_b := character_atom(b.id, buf_b2[:])
390	            gs := fmt.bprintf(goal[:], "rivals(%s, %s)", atom_a, atom_b)
391	            goal[len(gs)] = 0
392	            if !_epilog_bool(engine, string(goal[:len(gs)])) do continue
393	
394	            // Check proximity
395	            dx := a.position[0] - b.position[0]
396	            dy := a.position[1] - b.position[1]
397	            if dx < 0 { dx = -dx }
398	            if dy < 0 { dy = -dy }
399	            if dx + dy > CONFRONT_DIST do continue
400	
401	            // Fire confrontation
402	            ce.state  = .Waiting
403	            ce.char_a = a.id
404	            ce.char_b = b.id
405	            ce.timer  = 15.0
406	            ce.cooldown[ia][ib] = tick
407	            ce.cooldown[ib][ia] = tick
408	
409	            msg: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
410	            ms := fmt.bprintf(msg[:], "%s and %s face each other",
411	                character_name(a), character_name(b))
412	            event_log_push(&sim.log, tick, string(msg[:len(ms)]))
413	            return
414	        }
415	    }
416	}
417	
418	// Returns 0=waiting, 1=let it happen, 2=intervene
419	confrontation_draw_prompt :: proc(ce: ^ConfrontationEvent,
420	                                   res: ^Residents, dt: f32) -> int {
421	    if ce.state != .Waiting do return 0
422	
423	    ce.timer -= dt
424	    a := residents_find(res, ce.char_a)
425	    b := residents_find(res, ce.char_b)
426	    if a == nil || b == nil { ce.state = .None; return 0 }
427	
428	    rl.DrawRectangle(0, 0, SW(), SH(), rl.Color{20, 5, 5, 150})
429	
430	    cw := i32(500)
431	    ch := i32(140)
432	    cx := (SW() - cw) / 2
433	    cy := (SH() - ch) / 2
434	    rl.DrawRectangle(cx, cy, cw, ch, rl.Color{40, 20, 20, 240})
435	    rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(rl.Rectangle{f32(cx), f32(cy), f32(cw), f32(ch)},
436	                             2, rl.Color{220, 60, 60, 200})
437	
438	    an := strings.clone_to_cstring(character_name(a), context.temp_allocator)
439	    bn := strings.clone_to_cstring(character_name(b), context.temp_allocator)
440	    rl.DrawText(an, cx+20, cy+16, UI_FSIZE, char_palette_color(a.sprite))
441	    rl.DrawText(" vs ", cx+20+rl.MeasureText(an,UI_FSIZE), cy+16, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
442	    rl.DrawText(bn, cx+20+rl.MeasureText(an,UI_FSIZE)+rl.MeasureText(" vs ",UI_FSIZE),
443	                cy+16, UI_FSIZE, char_palette_color(b.sprite))
444	    rl.DrawText("Rivals are confronting each other!", cx+20, cy+44, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
445	
446	    bar_w := i32(f32(cw-40) * (ce.timer/15.0))
447	    rl.DrawRectangle(cx+20, cy+64, cw-40, 4, COL_MUTED)
448	    rl.DrawRectangle(cx+20, cy+64, bar_w, 4, rl.Color{220,60,60,255})
449	
450	    lit_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(cx+40), f32(cy+80), 160, 40}
451	    int_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(cx+300), f32(cy+80), 160, 40}
452	    hov_l := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), lit_rect)
453	    hov_i := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), int_rect)
454	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(lit_rect, hov_l ? rl.Color{160,40,40,255} : rl.Color{100,30,30,255})
455	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(int_rect, hov_i ? COL_ACCENT : COL_SELECT)
456	    rl.DrawText("Let it happen", cx+50, cy+92, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)
457	    rl.DrawText("Intervene", cx+318, cy+92, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)
458	
459	    if hov_l && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) { ce.state = .None; return 1 }
460	    if hov_i && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) { ce.state = .None; return 2 }
461	    if ce.timer <= 0                           { ce.state = .None; return 1 }  // auto: let it happen
462	    return 0
463	}
464	
465	confrontation_resolve :: proc(result: int, char_a, char_b: u32,
466	                               sim: ^SimState, res: ^Residents,
467	                               engine: ^EpilogEngine, tick: u64) {
468	    a := residents_find(res, char_a)
469	    b := residents_find(res, char_b)
470	    if a == nil || b == nil do return
471	
472	    ia := int(char_a - 1)
473	    ib := int(char_b - 1)
474	
475	    if result == 1 {
476	        // Let it happen — one dominates
477	        winner_is_a := sim.rng.state & 1 == 0
478	        dom_id  := winner_is_a ? char_a : char_b
479	        sub_idx := winner_is_a ? ib : ia
480	
481	        buf: [256]u8
482	        atom := character_atom(dom_id, buf[:])
483	        s := fmt.bprintf(buf[32:], "dominant_rival(%s).", atom)
484	        buf[32+len(s)] = 0
485	        epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(buf[32:])))
486	
487	        if sub_idx >= 0 && sub_idx < MAX_RESIDENTS {
488	            sim.social[sub_idx].mood = .Sad
489	        }
490	        dom_char := residents_find(res, dom_id)
491	        if dom_char != nil {
492	            msg: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
493	            ms := fmt.bprintf(msg[:], "%s emerged dominant from the confrontation",
494	                character_name(dom_char))
495	            event_log_push(&sim.log, tick, string(msg[:len(ms)]))
496	        }
497	    } else {
498	        // Intervene — both reset to content, cooldown already set
499	        if ia >= 0 && ia < MAX_RESIDENTS { sim.social[ia].mood = .Content }
500	        if ib >= 0 && ib < MAX_RESIDENTS { sim.social[ib].mood = .Content }
501	
502	        msg: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
503	        ms := fmt.bprintf(msg[:], "Confrontation between %s and %s was defused",
504	            character_name(a), character_name(b))
505	        event_log_push(&sim.log, tick, string(msg[:len(ms)]))
506	    }
507	}
508	
509	// ============================================================
510	// 3.4  Friendship Decay
511	// ============================================================
512	// Track last-interaction tick per pair. Decay friend→acquaintance
513	// if no interaction in 30 ticks, acquaintance→stranger in 50.
514	
515	FriendshipDecay :: struct {
516	    last_interact : [MAX_RESIDENTS][MAX_RESIDENTS]u64,
517	}
518	
519	friendship_decay_record :: proc(fd: ^FriendshipDecay, id_a, id_b: u32, tick: u64) {
520	    ia := int(id_a - 1)
521	    ib := int(id_b - 1)
522	    if ia >= MAX_RESIDENTS || ib >= MAX_RESIDENTS do return
523	    fd.last_interact[ia][ib] = tick
524	    fd.last_interact[ib][ia] = tick
525	}
526	
527	friendship_decay_tick :: proc(fd: ^FriendshipDecay, res: ^Residents,
528	                               engine: ^EpilogEngine, sim: ^SimState, tick: u64) {
529	    buf_a, buf_b, src: [256]u8
530	
531	    for i in 0..<res.count {
532	        for j in i+1..<res.count {
533	            a := &res.chars[i]
534	            b := &res.chars[j]
535	            ia := int(a.id - 1)
536	            ib := int(b.id - 1)
537	            if ia >= MAX_RESIDENTS || ib >= MAX_RESIDENTS do continue
538	
539	            last := fd.last_interact[ia][ib]
540	            if last == 0 do continue
541	
542	            idle := tick - last
543	
544	            // Query current relationship
545	            atom_a := character_atom(a.id, buf_a[:])
546	            atom_b := character_atom(b.id, buf_b[:])
547	
548	            // Check friend decay (30 ticks idle)
549	            if idle == 30 {
550	                gs := fmt.bprintf(src[:], "friend(%s, %s)", atom_a, atom_b)
551	                src[len(gs)] = 0
552	                if _epilog_bool(engine, string(src[:len(gs)])) {
553	                    // Downgrade to acquaintance
554	                    s := fmt.bprintf(src[:], "acquaintance(%s, %s).", atom_a, atom_b)
555	                    src[len(s)] = 0
556	                    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(src[:])))
557	                    s = fmt.bprintf(src[:], "acquaintance(%s, %s).", atom_b, atom_a)
558	                    src[len(s)] = 0
559	                    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(src[:])))
560	
561	                    msg: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
562	                    ms := fmt.bprintf(msg[:], "%s and %s drifted apart (friend -> acquaintance)",
563	                        character_name(a), character_name(b))
564	                    event_log_push(&sim.log, tick, string(msg[:len(ms)]))
565	                }
566	            }
567	
568	            // Check acquaintance decay (50 ticks idle)
569	            if idle == 50 {
570	                gs := fmt.bprintf(src[:], "acquaintance(%s, %s)", atom_a, atom_b)
571	                src[len(gs)] = 0
572	                if _epilog_bool(engine, string(src[:len(gs)])) {
573	                    s := fmt.bprintf(src[:], "stranger(%s, %s).", atom_a, atom_b)
574	                    src[len(s)] = 0
575	                    epilog_load_string(engine, cstring(raw_data(src[:])))
576	
577	                    msg: [EVENT_MSG_LEN]u8
578	                    ms := fmt.bprintf(msg[:], "%s and %s became strangers (acquaintance -> stranger)",
579	                        character_name(a), character_name(b))
580	                    event_log_push(&sim.log, tick, string(msg[:len(ms)]))
581	                }
582	            }
583	        }
584	    }
585	}
586	
587	// ============================================================
588	// 3.5  Character Backstory Panel
589	// ============================================================
590	
591	BackstoryPanel :: struct {
592	    open        : bool,
593	    char_id     : u32,
594	    rel_results : [MAX_REL_RESULTS]RelResult,
595	    rel_count   : int,
596	    scroll      : i32,
597	}
598	
599	backstory_open :: proc(bp: ^BackstoryPanel, id: u32,
600	                        res: ^Residents, engine: ^EpilogEngine) {
601	    bp.open    = true
602	    bp.char_id = id
603	    bp.scroll  = 0
604	    // Reuse _query_relationships from residents.odin via ResidentListScreen helper
605	    tmp: ResidentListScreen
606	    tmp.selected_id = id
607	    _query_relationships(&tmp, id, res, engine)
608	    bp.rel_count = tmp.rel_count
609	    for i in 0..<tmp.rel_count {
610	        bp.rel_results[i] = tmp.rel_results[i]
611	    }
612	}
613	
614	backstory_draw :: proc(bp: ^BackstoryPanel, res: ^Residents,
615	                        sim: ^SimState, log: ^EventLog) -> bool {
616	    if !bp.open do return false
617	
618	    c := residents_find(res, bp.char_id)
619	    if c == nil { bp.open = false; return false }
620	
621	    // Close on ESC or click outside
622	    if rl.IsKeyPressed(.ESCAPE) {
623	        bp.open = false
624	        return false
625	    }
626	
627	    // Panel dimensions
628	    pw := i32(340)
629	    ph := i32(500)
630	    px := (SW() - pw) / 2
631	    py := (SH() - ph) / 2
632	
633	    // Darken background
634	    rl.DrawRectangle(0, 0, SW(), SH(), rl.Color{0, 0, 0, 140})
635	
636	    // Click outside closes
637	    panel_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(px), f32(py), f32(pw), f32(ph)}
638	    if rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) &&
639	       !rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), panel_rect) {
640	        bp.open = false
641	        return false
642	    }
643	
644	    rl.DrawRectangle(px, py, pw, ph, rl.Color{20, 20, 35, 248})
645	    rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(panel_rect, 2, char_palette_color(c.sprite))
646	
647	    y  := py + 12
648	    lx := px + 14
649	
650	    // Name + personality + age
651	    name_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(character_name(c), context.temp_allocator)
652	    rl.DrawText(name_cstr, lx, y, UI_HSIZE, char_palette_color(c.sprite))
653	    y += UI_HSIZE + 4
654	
655	    pers_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(personality_label(c.personality), context.temp_allocator)
656	    age_buf: [32]u8
657	    age_s := fmt.bprintf(age_buf[:], " · %d", c.age)
658	    age_buf[len(age_s)] = 0
659	    rl.DrawText(pers_cstr, lx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
660	    rl.DrawText(cstring(raw_data(age_buf[:])), lx + rl.MeasureText(pers_cstr, UI_SMALL), y, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
661	    y += UI_SMALL + 4
662	
663	    // Divider
664	    rl.DrawLine(lx, y, px + pw - lx, y, COL_MUTED); y += 10
665	
666	    // Relationships
667	    rl.DrawText("RELATIONSHIPS", lx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_ACCENT); y += UI_SMALL + 4
668	    if bp.rel_count == 0 {
669	        rl.DrawText("none", lx + 8, y, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED); y += UI_SMALL + 4
670	    } else {
671	        for ri in 0..<bp.rel_count {
672	            r := &bp.rel_results[ri]
673	            row_buf: [64]u8
674	            nr := fmt.bprintf(row_buf[:], "  %s  —  %s", r.other_name, r.tag)
675	            row_buf[len(nr)] = 0
676	            rl.DrawText(cstring(raw_data(row_buf[:])), lx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_TEXT)
677	            y += UI_SMALL + 2
678	        }
679	    }
680	    y += 6
681	
682	    // Divider
683	    rl.DrawLine(lx, y, px + pw - lx, y, COL_MUTED); y += 10
684	
685	    // Mood + interaction count
686	    ia := int(c.id - 1)
687	    rl.DrawText("MOOD", lx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_ACCENT); y += UI_SMALL + 2
688	    if ia >= 0 && ia < MAX_RESIDENTS {
689	        mood_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(mood_label(sim.social[ia].mood), context.temp_allocator)
690	        rl.DrawText(mood_cstr, lx + 8, y, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
691	    }
692	    y += UI_FSIZE + 6
693	
694	    // Divider
695	    rl.DrawLine(lx, y, px + pw - lx, y, COL_MUTED); y += 10
696	
697	    // Last 5 events mentioning this character
698	    rl.DrawText("RECENT EVENTS", lx, y, UI_SMALL, COL_ACCENT); y += UI_SMALL + 4
699	    name_str := character_name(c)
700	    shown := 0
701	    for ei in 0..<log.count {
702	        if shown >= 5 do break
703	        e := event_log_get(log, ei)
704	        if e == nil do continue
705	        msg := event_entry_str(e)
706	        if strings.contains(msg, name_str) {
707	            trunc := msg
708	            if len(trunc) > 38 { trunc = trunc[:38] }
709	            msg_cstr := strings.clone_to_cstring(trunc, context.temp_allocator)
710	            rl.DrawText(msg_cstr, lx + 4, y, UI_SMALL - 2, COL_TEXT)
711	            y += UI_SMALL
712	            shown += 1
713	        }
714	    }
715	    if shown == 0 {
716	        rl.DrawText("none yet", lx + 4, y, UI_SMALL, COL_MUTED)
717	        y += UI_SMALL
718	    }
719	
720	    // Close hint
721	    rl.DrawText("[ESC] to close", lx, py + ph - 20, UI_SMALL - 2, COL_MUTED)
722	
723	    return true
724	}

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. This is a substantial implementation — HTTP server, WebSocket, snapshot system, headless mode, dashboard HTML, config file, CLI flags. Let me plan the files to create:

1. `src/api_snapshot.odin` — `SimSnapshot` struct + `snapshot_build` proc
2. `src/api_http.odin` — raw TCP socket HTTP/WebSocket server
3. `src/api_routes.odin` — route handlers for all REST endpoints + WS message handling
4. `src/api_server.odin` — server lifecycle, thread, broadcast loop
5. `src/headless.odin` — headless tick loop + CLI/config parsing
6. `src/dashboard.html` — self-contained dashboard
7. Modify `src/main.odin` — parse flags, launch API thread, headless branch

Let me start writing all files:

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * world.odin — World seed system and character placement / wandering (M3).
3	 */
4	
5	package dmls
6	
7	import "core:fmt"
8	
9	// ---- World -----------------------------------------------------------------
10	
11	World :: struct {
12	    seed        : u64,
13	    grid        : Grid,
14	    rng         : u64,              // LCG state for placement/wandering
15	    tick        : u64,
16	    // Per-character position cache (mirrors grid occupant)
17	    positions   : [MAX_RESIDENTS][2]i32,    // [id-1] = {col, row}
18	}
19	
20	world_init :: proc(w: ^World, seed: u64) {
21	    w.seed = seed
22	    w.rng  = seed
23	    w.tick = 0
24	    grid_generate(&w.grid, seed)
25	    landmarks_place(&w.grid, seed)
26	}
27	
28	// Spawn a newly created character onto the grid.
29	world_spawn_character :: proc(w: ^World, res: ^Residents, id: u32) -> bool {
30	    c := residents_find(res, id)
31	    if c == nil do return false
32	
33	    col, row, ok := grid_find_spawn(&w.grid, &w.rng)
34	    if !ok do return false
35	
36	    if !grid_place_character(&w.grid, id, col, row) do return false
37	
38	    c.position = {col, row}
39	    idx := id - 1
40	    if int(idx) < MAX_RESIDENTS {
41	        w.positions[idx] = {col, row}
42	    }
43	    return true
44	}
45	
46	// One wander step per character — moves to a random adjacent walkable cell.
47	world_tick_wander :: proc(w: ^World, res: ^Residents) {
48	    dx := [4]i32{0, 0, 1, -1}
49	    dy := [4]i32{1, -1, 0, 0}
50	
51	    for i in 0..<res.count {
52	        c   := &res.chars[i]
53	        col := c.position[0]
54	        row := c.position[1]
55	
56	        // Pick a random direction
57	        w.rng = w.rng * 6364136223846793005 + 1442695040888963407
58	        dir  := int((w.rng >> 33) % 4)
59	
60	        nc := col + dx[dir]

1	/*
2	 * screen_cfg.odin — Runtime screen resolution configuration.
3	 *
4	 * Three supported resolutions: 640×320, 1920×720, 1920×1080.
5	 * The player picks one on the title screen before generating the world.
6	 *
7	 * SCREEN_W / SCREEN_H compile-time constants remain as the maximum dimensions
8	 * (1920×1080) used only for RenderTexture allocation and rl.InitWindow.
9	 * All rendering code reads SW() / SH() for the active dimensions.
10	 *
11	 * Derived layout values (stats panel split, sim panel dimensions) are also
12	 * exposed as procs so they react to resolution changes automatically.
13	 */
14	
15	package dmls
16	
17	import rl "vendor:raylib"
18	
19	// ---- Resolution enum -------------------------------------------------------
20	
21	ScreenSize :: enum {
22	    S_640x320,
23	    S_1920x720,
24	    S_1920x1080,
25	}
26	
27	SCREEN_SIZE_LABELS := [ScreenSize]cstring{
28	    .S_640x320   = "640 × 320  (Compact)",
29	    .S_1920x720  = "1920 × 720  (Wide)",
30	    .S_1920x1080 = "1920 × 1080  (Full HD)",
31	}
32	
33	SCREEN_SIZE_W := [ScreenSize]i32{
34	    .S_640x320   = 640,
35	    .S_1920x720  = 1920,
36	    .S_1920x1080 = 1920,
37	}
38	
39	SCREEN_SIZE_H := [ScreenSize]i32{
40	    .S_640x320   = 320,
41	    .S_1920x720  = 720,
42	    .S_1920x1080 = 1080,
43	}
44	
45	// ---- Active screen state (global) -----------------------------------------
46	
47	ScreenCfg :: struct {
48	    w    : i32,
49	    h    : i32,
50	    size : ScreenSize,
51	}
52	
53	g_screen : ScreenCfg
54	
55	screen_cfg_set :: proc(sz: ScreenSize) {
56	    g_screen.size = sz
57	    g_screen.w    = SCREEN_SIZE_W[sz]
58	    g_screen.h    = SCREEN_SIZE_H[sz]
59	    rl.SetWindowSize(g_screen.w, g_screen.h)
60	}
61	
62	// Convenience accessors used everywhere in rendering code.
63	SW :: #force_inline proc() -> i32 { return g_screen.w }
64	SH :: #force_inline proc() -> i32 { return g_screen.h }
65	
66	// Derived layout values — 80 / 20 split: left 80% = grid sim, right 20% = sidebar.
67	// sim_w / sim_h = the grid viewport dimensions.
68	// stat_w / stat_h = the sidebar dimensions.
69	// stats_x = x pixel where the sidebar begins (= sim_w).
70	sim_w   :: #force_inline proc() -> i32 { return SW() * 80 / 100 }
71	sim_h   :: #force_inline proc() -> i32 { return SH() }
72	stats_x :: #force_inline proc() -> i32 { return sim_w() }
73	stat_w  :: #force_inline proc() -> i32 { return SW() - sim_w() }
74	stat_h  :: #force_inline proc() -> i32 { return SH() }
75	
76	// Dynamic cell pixel size: fit as many cells as possible into the sim viewport.
77	// Result is at least 1.
78	render_cell_px :: #force_inline proc() -> i32 {
79	    px := sim_w() / GRID_W
80	    if px < 1 { px = 1 }
81	    return px
82	}
83	
84	// ---- Character colour palette (8 distinct colours, indexed by sprite) ------
85	//
86	// Each of the 8 sprite slots has a fixed canonical colour.
87	// Starter characters are assigned slots 0–3 matching their canonical look.
88	//   0 = [NAME] — warm amber / gold
89	//   1 = Uriel     — soft sky blue
90	//   2 = Anna Croft — vivid coral red
91	//   3 = Saloni    — peach / warm orange
92	//   4–7           — available for player-created characters
93	
94	CHAR_PALETTE := [SPRITE_COUNT]rl.Color{
95	    rl.Color{230, 180,  60, 255},  // 0 [NAME]  — amber gold
96	    rl.Color{ 80, 180, 230, 255},  // 1 Uriel       — sky blue
97	    rl.Color{220,  70,  70, 255},  // 2 Anna Croft  — coral red
98	    rl.Color{240, 150,  80, 255},  // 3 Saloni      — warm peach
99	    rl.Color{100, 210, 140, 255},  // 4              — mint green
100	    rl.Color{170,  90, 220, 255},  // 5              — violet
101	    rl.Color{220, 210,  80, 255},  // 6              — yellow
102	    rl.Color{180, 180, 200, 255},  // 7              — silver
103	}
104	
105	char_palette_color :: proc(sprite: u32) -> rl.Color {
106	    idx := int(sprite) % SPRITE_COUNT
107	    return CHAR_PALETTE[idx]
108	}
109	
110	// ---- Resolution picker UI --------------------------------------------------
111	// Drawn on the title screen before island naming.
112	// Returns true when the player has confirmed a choice.
113	
114	ResolutionPicker :: struct {
115	    chosen   : ScreenSize,
116	    confirmed: bool,
117	}
118	
119	resolution_picker_init :: proc(p: ^ResolutionPicker) {
120	    p.chosen    = .S_1920x1080
121	    p.confirmed = false
122	}
123	
124	// Draw and handle input. Returns true once confirmed.
125	resolution_picker_update :: proc(p: ^ResolutionPicker) -> bool {
126	    rl.ClearBackground(COL_BG)
127	
128	    cy := SH() / 2 - 120
129	    rl.DrawText("Choose resolution", UI_LPAD, cy - 40, UI_HSIZE, COL_TEXT)
130	
131	    for sz in ScreenSize {
132	        label := SCREEN_SIZE_LABELS[sz]
133	        sel   := p.chosen == sz
134	        rect  := rl.Rectangle{f32(UI_LPAD), f32(cy), 400, 44}
135	        bg    := sel ? COL_SELECT : COL_INPUT
136	        hov   := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), rect)
137	        if hov { bg = sel ? COL_ACCENT : COL_HOVER }
138	        rl.DrawRectangleRec(rect, bg)
139	        rl.DrawRectangleLinesEx(rect, 1, sel ? COL_ACCENT : COL_MUTED)
140	        rl.DrawText(label, UI_LPAD + 14, cy + 12, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
141	        if hov && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT) {
142	            p.chosen = sz
143	        }
144	        cy += 54
145	    }
146	
147	    cy += 10
148	    ok_rect := rl.Rectangle{f32(UI_LPAD), f32(cy), 200, 44}
149	    hov_ok  := rl.CheckCollisionPointRec(rl.GetMousePosition(), ok_rect)
150	    rl.DrawRectangleRec(ok_rect, hov_ok ? COL_ACCENT : COL_SELECT)
151	    rl.DrawText("Confirm ->", UI_LPAD + 16, cy + 12, UI_FSIZE, COL_TEXT)
152	
153	    if (hov_ok && rl.IsMouseButtonPressed(.LEFT)) || rl.IsKeyPressed(.ENTER) {
154	        screen_cfg_set(p.chosen)
155	        p.confirmed = true
156	        return true
157	    }
158	    return false
159	}
160

content.text: Now fix the stack overflow: `SimSnapshot` at 425 KB is too large for the stack. Allocate it on the heap in `shared_read_snapshot` and all callers. The cleanest fix is to make the shared state hold a pointer and have read return a heap copy:

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * api_snapshot.odin — Shared simulation snapshot written by the sim thread,
3	 *                     read by the API thread under mutex.
4	 *
5	 * The snapshot is a plain Odin struct using only value types (fixed arrays,
6	 * integer counts) so it can be copied under the mutex without allocation.
7	 */

19	MAX_SNAP_EVENTS    :: 50
20	MAX_SNAP_NAME      :: 40
21

23	    id               : u32,

file.content:
/*
 * api_snapshot.odin — Shared simulation snapshot written by the sim thread,
 *                     read by the API thread under mutex.
 *
 * The snapshot is a plain Odin struct using only value types (fixed arrays,
 * integer counts) so it can be copied under the mutex without allocation.
 */

package dmls

import "core:sync"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:strings"

// ---- Snapshot structs (value-type safe to copy) ----------------------------

MAX_SNAP_RESIDENTS :: MAX_RESIDENTS
MAX_SNAP_RELS      :: MAX_RESIDENTS * MAX_RESIDENTS
MAX_SNAP_EVENTS    :: 50
MAX_SNAP_NAME      :: 40

ResidentSnap :: struct {
    id               : u32,
    name             : [MAX_SNAP_NAME]u8,
    mood             : [12]u8,
    personality      : [32]u8,
    gender           : [12]u8,
    dating_pref      : [12]u8,
    position         : [2]i32,
    sprite           : u32,

content.text: Rather than adding `defer free` to 13 call sites, a cleaner approach is to use a temp-allocator heap allocation so it gets cleaned up automatically with `free_all(context.temp_allocator)` which is already called in each request:

content.text:
Several issues. Let me fix them all:

**1. `_rel_snap_cb` is `"c"` ABI — no implicit context.** Fix by adding `context = runtime.default_context()`.

**2. `EventSnap` passed by value to `_ws_emit_event` — slice of fixed array field not addressable.** Fix by passing pointer.

**3. `base64.encode` wrong arg order.**

**4. `World` stack overflow in headless — heap-allocate.**

**5. `grid_remove_character` signature mismatch.**

    acc := cast(^_RelSnapAccum)ud
    if acc.count >= len(acc.out) do return .EPILOG_STOP

    other_atom: cstring
    tag_atom  : cstring

    for i in 0..<epilog_soln_var_count(soln) {
        vname := epilog_soln_var_name(soln, i)
        val   := epilog_soln_var_atom(soln, i)
        if vname == nil || val == nil do continue
        switch string(vname) {
        case "Other": other_atom = val
        case "Tag":   tag_atom   = val
        }
    }
    if other_atom == nil do return .EPILOG_NEXT

    other_id := _atom_to_id(string(other_atom))
    other    := residents_find(acc.res, other_id)
    if other == nil do return .EPILOG_NEXT

    rs := &acc.out[acc.count]

    my_c := residents_find(acc.res, acc.my_id)
    if my_c != nil { _snap_str(rs.from[:], character_name(my_c)) }
    _snap_str(rs.to[:], character_name(other))

    tag_s := "acquaintance"
    if tag_atom != nil { tag_s = string(tag_atom) }
    _snap_str(rs.kind[:], tag_s)

    acc.count += 1
    return .EPILOG_NEXT
}

The server is responding but routing isn't matching. The issue is the path parser — `parts[1]` for `GET / HTTP/1.1` will be `/`, but let me check the `_parse_request` path handling. The issue: `GET /api/status HTTP/1.1` — `parts[1]` = `/api/status` which should match. But the `Content-Length` isn't being parsed, so the body read might be blocking. Actually looking at the connection loop, we read until `\r\n\r\n` is found, but then pass the body as the remaining bytes. The HTTP body read works for this. The issue is likely that the `switch` in `api_dispatch` uses `strings.has_prefix` but the path `/api/status` case uses `req.path == "/api/status"` which should match.

Let me check if it's actually serving on the right port — the "not found" JSON is our own response, so routing IS happening but not matching. The issue is `path` parsing when the request line has `HTTP/1.1` at the end — `parts[1]` should be just the path. Let me check with a simpler curl-like test:

content.text:
The routing looks correct. The problem must be in `_parse_request` — `req.path` is being cloned to `context.temp_allocator`, but `api_dispatch` calls `free_all(context.temp_allocator)` at the top via `defer`. Wait — the `defer` runs at the *end* of the function, so the path is still valid during routing. Let me check what `req.path` actually contains by looking at the parse more carefully:

The issue is `strings.split_lines` — it splits on `\n` but our raw HTTP uses `\r\n`. The `parts[0]` line would be `GET /api/status HTTP/1.1\r` with the trailing `\r`. That means `parts[1]` in the fields split would be `/api/status` (fields splits on whitespace, which includes `\r`). Actually `strings.fields` splits on any whitespace so that's fine.

Wait — `_parse_request` uses `strings.split_lines` which splits on `\n`, giving lines with trailing `\r`. Then `lines[0]` = `"GET /api/status HTTP/1.1\r"` and `strings.fields(lines[0])` = `["GET", "/api/status", "HTTP/1.1"]` — that's correct since `\r` is part of `HTTP/1.1\r` but `parts[1]` = `/api/status`. So the path should be right.

Actually the real issue might be `context.temp_allocator` in the connection thread — each thread gets its own context but the temp allocator may not be initialized. The `strings.clone` call in `_parse_request` uses `context.temp_allocator`, which in a bare spawned thread is the default (arena allocator). But `strings.split_lines` also allocates. The path is being cloned correctly.

Let me look at whether the `http_send` status code is wrong — the response header says `HTTP/1.1 404 OK` (the "OK" text is hardcoded regardless of status). That's cosmetic. The real issue is the routes aren't matching. Let me add a debug print to confirm what path is being received:

Actually — I just noticed: `http_send` always writes `HTTP/1.1 %d OK` — the status code number IS 404 here. The JSON `{"error":"not found"}` is our fallthrough. So `/api/status` genuinely hits the `case:` default. The path must not be `"/api/status"`.

The `strings.split_lines` call splits on `\n` but the input is `\r\n` — so `lines[0]` = `"GET /api/status HTTP/1.1\r"`. Then `strings.fields` = `["GET", "/api/status", "HTTP/1.1\r"]`. `parts[1]` = `"/api/status"` — correct.

But wait: `strings.clone(full_path, context.temp_allocator)` — and `full_path` is a slice of `headers_str` which is a slice of `string(raw)` where `raw` is a stack array. After `_parse_request` returns, `raw` is deallocated! The `req.path` is a dangling reference. That's the bug.

The fix: parse the path before cloning, and the clone is fine since it allocates on `temp_allocator`. But `headers_str` is `s[:split]` where `s = string(raw)` — `raw` is a `[8192]u8` local in `_conn_thread`. That's still alive when `ctx.handler(req, ctx.sock)` is called since `_conn_thread` hasn't returned yet. So the slice is valid.

Let me check the actual parse more carefully — specifically whether `split_lines` returns substrings of the original or new allocations:

`strings.split_lines` in Odin typically returns slices into the original string (no allocation for the substrings themselves). So `lines[0]` is a substring of `headers_str`. `strings.fields` similarly returns subslices. Then `full_path = parts[1]` is a subslice. `strings.clone(full_path, ...)` copies it. So `req.path` is properly allocated on `temp_allocator`.

The real problem: `strings.split_lines` takes a `string` but we're passing the `context.temp_allocator` — wait, the signature is `strings.split_lines(s: string, allocator := context.allocator) -> []string`. It allocates the `[]string` slice on the allocator, but the string contents are subslices. So `lines` itself is allocated. And `strings.fields(lines[0], context.temp_allocator)` similarly allocates `parts`. These are all fine.

Let me just check it's not a path trimming issue — add a `\r` trim:

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user made two primary requests this session:
   1. **"implement API.MD"** — implement the full web API and dashboard specification from D:\DMLS\API.MD, including HTTP REST endpoints, WebSocket event stream, headless mode, shared snapshot system, and self-contained web dashboard.
   2. **"setup a minimal html+ajax/alpine frontend"** followed by **"also use material-ui skills /material-3"** — redesign the dashboard using Alpine.js for reactivity and Material Design 3 for styling.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Odin language** — `"c"` ABI callbacks need `context = runtime.default_context()`, `~` is XOR, pointer deref is `^`, auto-deref through `.`, no runtime string `+` concat for non-constants (use `fmt.tprintf`), `#load` embeds files at compile time
   - **Large stack frames** — SimSnapshot (~425KB) and World (~787KB) must be heap-allocated (`new(T)`) to avoid stack overflow; warnings are emitted but don't block build
   - **Odin stdlib changes** — `core:runtime` deprecated → `base:runtime`; `os.read_entire_file` now requires explicit allocator → use `os.read_entire_file_from_path(path, allocator)`; `base64.encode` signature: `encode(data, ENC_TABLE, allocator)`; sha1 at `core:crypto/legacy/sha1`
   - **Thread model** — main thread owns Raylib + Epilog; API thread reads only from `g_shared` snapshot under `sync.Mutex`; broadcast loop thread at 20Hz
   - **Shared state** — `SimShared` struct with `sync.Mutex`, snapshot, control requests (speed/quit/save), prompt state, pending events, resident add/remove requests
   - **WebSocket RFC 6455** — SHA1 handshake, text frames (opcode 0x81), unmasked server→client, masked client→server
   - **Material Design 3** — dark scheme with teal island seed, `--md-sys-color-*` tokens, `--md-sys-shape-corner-*`, MD3 motion easings, Roboto Flex + Material Symbols Outlined
   - **Alpine.js** — CDN-loaded, `x-data="dmls()"` pattern for reactive state
   - **Headless mode** — `--headless` CLI flag, pure tick loop, API is sole interface, `dmls.cfg` config file

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **d:\DMLS\src\api_snapshot.odin** (NEW)
     - Core shared state between sim thread and API thread
     - `SimSnapshot` struct (~425KB fixed arrays for residents/events/rels)
     - `SimShared` struct with mutex, snapshot, control requests, prompt state
     - `g_shared : SimShared` global
     - `snapshot_fill` (renamed from `snapshot_build`) takes `^SimSnapshot` as first arg to avoid stack overflow:
       ```odin
       snapshot_fill :: proc(snap: ^SimSnapshot, world: ^World, res: ^Residents, sim: ^SimState,
                              iname: ^IslandName, weather: ^WeatherState,
                              speed_idx: int, is_headless: bool, engine: ^EpilogEngine) {
           snap.tick = world.tick
           // ... fills all fields including querying Epilog for relationships
       }
       ```
     - `shared_read_snapshot` returns `^SimSnapshot` allocated on `context.temp_allocator`:
       ```odin
       shared_read_snapshot :: proc() -> ^SimSnapshot {
           s := new(SimSnapshot, context.temp_allocator)
           sync.lock(&g_shared.mu)
           s^ = g_shared.snap
           sync.unlock(&g_shared.mu)
           return s
       }
       ```
     - `shared_push_snapshot` takes `^SimSnapshot`
     - `_rel_snap_cb` is `"c"` ABI callback — has `context = runtime.default_context()` at top
     - Imports: `"core:sync"`, `"core:fmt"`, `"core:strings"`, `"base:runtime"`
     - Control procs: `shared_poll_controls`, `shared_set_speed_req`, `shared_set_quit`, `shared_set_save`
     - Prompt procs: `shared_set_prompt`, `shared_clear_prompt`, `shared_resolve_prompt`, `shared_poll_prompt_choice`
     - Resident req procs: `shared_request_add_resident`, `shared_request_remove_resident`, `shared_poll_res_req`
     - Query channel: `shared_eq_req : EpilogQueryReq` global, `shared_set_query`, `shared_put_query_result`, `shared_get_query_result`

   - **d:\DMLS\src\api_http.odin** (NEW)
     - Minimal TCP socket HTTP/1.1 + WebSocket server
     - Imports: `"core:net"`, `"core:thread"`, `"core:sync"`, `"core:fmt"`, `"core:strings"`, `"core:strconv"`, `"core:encoding/base64"`, `sha1 "core:crypto/legacy/sha1"`, `"core:bytes"`
     - `WsHub` struct with `sync.Mutex`, `[MAX_WS_CONNS]WsConn`; global `g_ws_hub`
     - `ws_broadcast(msg: string)` — copies conn list under lock, sends outside lock
     - `ws_make_frame(payload: string) -> []u8` — RFC 6455 text frame, unmasked
     - `ws_handshake` — SHA1(key + WS_MAGIC) → base64: `base64.encode(digest[:], base64.ENC_TABLE, context.temp_allocator)`
     - `ws_read_frame` — reads masked client frames, returns payload string
     - `_parse_request` — parses HTTP/1.1 request line, headers, body; detects WS upgrade
     - `http_server_run(port, handler)` — accepts TCP connections, spawns thread per connection
     - `_conn_thread` — reads up to 8KB, parses, calls handler

   - **d:\DMLS\src\api_routes.odin** (NEW)
     - All REST and WebSocket message handlers
     - Imports: `"core:net"`, `"core:fmt"`, `"core:strings"`, `"core:strconv"`, `"core:sync"`
     - JSON helper procs: `_js_str`, `_js_int`, `_js_u64`, `_js_bool`, `_json_escape`
     - Minimal JSON extractors: `_json_get_str`, `_json_get_int`
     - Route procs: `route_status`, `route_residents`, `route_resident_by_name`, `route_relationships`, `route_rivals`, `route_events`, `route_control_speed`, `route_control_pause`, `route_control_save`, `route_control_quit`, `route_add_resident_json`, `route_remove_resident`, `route_query`
     - `api_dispatch` — main HTTP dispatcher, handles WS upgrade at `/ws`, REST routes by method+path
     - `DASHBOARD_HTML :: #load("dashboard.html", string)` — embeds dashboard at compile time
     - `ws_handle_message` — handles set_speed, pause, unpause, resolve_prompt, query, add_resident
     - Non-constant string concat uses `fmt.tprintf`, not `+`
     - `_build_snapshot_json` — builds full WS snapshot message

   - **d:\DMLS\src\api_server.odin** (NEW)
     - API thread lifecycle and broadcast loop
     - `ApiServer` struct with port, running, tick_chan (`chan_bool` spin flag)
     - `api_push_tick(snap: SimSnapshot)` — signals broadcast loop (snap unused, reads g_shared directly)
     - `_api_broadcast_loop` — 20Hz timer, emits tick/positions/events/snapshot/prompts
     - `_ws_emit_tick(snap: ^SimSnapshot)`, `_ws_emit_positions(snap: ^SimSnapshot)` — take pointer
     - `_ws_emit_event(e: ^EventSnap)` — takes pointer (fixed arrays not addressable by value)
     - `api_thread_start(port: int)` — spawns HTTP listener thread + broadcast loop thread

   - **d:\DMLS\src\headless.odin** (NEW)
     - Headless simulation loop + CLI/config parsing
     - `DMLSConfig` struct: port, headless, speed, save, seed, load
     - `parse_config()` — reads `dmls.cfg` then applies CLI flags
     - `_read_cfg_file` uses `os.read_entire_file_from_path(path, context.temp_allocator)` (new API)
     - `headless_run(cfg: ^DMLSConfig)` — main headless loop
     - `world := new(World); defer free(world)` — heap-allocated to avoid 787KB stack frame
     - All `&world` → `world` since it's already `^World`; exception: `&world.grid` for grid procs
     - `grid_remove_character(&world.grid, c.position[0], c.position[1])` — correct signature
     - Uses `snapshot_fill(sp, world, ...)` pattern with temp-allocator

   - **d:\DMLS\src\dashboard.html** (NEW, rewritten with MD3 + Alpine.js)
     - Material Design 3 dark scheme (island teal seed)
     - CSS custom properties: `--md-sys-color-primary: #6dd4c0`, full dark palette
     - Shape tokens: `--md-sys-shape-corner-*` (extra-small 4px through full 9999px)
     - Motion tokens: `--md-motion-emphasized`, `--md-motion-emphasized-decelerate`, `--md-motion-standard`
     - Alpine.js from CDN: `x-data="dmls()"`, reactive state object
     - Components: top app bar with info chips + speed segmented buttons + connection badge, canvas map, residents panel with expandable rel chips, events panel with colour-coded names, query console, MD3 dialog for prompts
     - Material Symbols Outlined + Roboto Flex fonts
     - REST fallback polling every 5s when WS disconnected
     - Auto-reconnect WS every 3s
     - Canvas map: draws character dots with mood rings, hover shows relationship arrows + name label

   - **d:\DMLS\src\main.odin** (MODIFIED)
     - Added `parse_config()` call at top of `main()`
     - `g_shared` initialization before API thread start
     - `api_thread_start(cfg.port)` call
     - Headless branch: `if cfg.headless { headless_run(&cfg); return }`
     - API control polling in `_update_simulation`:
       ```odin
       speed_req, quit, save := shared_poll_controls()
       if quit { rl.CloseWindow(); return }
       if save { _do_save(app) }
       if speed_req >= 0 { app.sim_speed = speed_req }
       ```
     - Snapshot push per tick:
       ```odin
       snap := new(SimSnapshot, context.temp_allocator)
       snapshot_fill(snap, &app.world, &app.residents, &app.sim, &app.island_name,
                     &app.weather, app.sim_speed, false, app.engine)
       shared_push_snapshot(snap)
       api_push_tick(snap^)
       ```
     - Confession/confrontation prompts now also call `shared_set_prompt` and `shared_poll_prompt_choice` for WS resolution
     - Imports: `"core:fmt"`, `"core:strings"` (sync removed — was unused)

   - **d:\DMLS\build.bat** (MODIFIED)
     - Fixed raylib.dll discovery: uses `where odin` to find odin binary, derives path from it
     - No longer requires `%ODIN_ROOT%` environment variable

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **`core:crypto/sha1` path doesn't exist**: Fixed → `sha1 "core:crypto/legacy/sha1"`
   - **`sync` unused in api_http.odin**: Removed then re-added when WsHub needed it
   - **`sync` undeclared in api_routes.odin**: Added `import "core:sync"` (had `"core:thread"` instead)
   - **Runtime string `+` concat**: Odin only allows `+` on constant strings. Fixed two places with `fmt.tprintf(...)`
   - **SimSnapshot 425KB stack overflow**: Changed `shared_read_snapshot` to return `^SimSnapshot` via temp_allocator; renamed `snapshot_build` → `snapshot_fill(snap: ^SimSnapshot, ...)` to fill caller-provided struct; all callers use `new(SimSnapshot, context.temp_allocator)` then `snapshot_fill`
   - **`_rel_snap_cb` "c" ABI missing context**: Added `context = runtime.default_context()` as first line
   - **`import "core:runtime"` deprecated**: Changed to `import "base:runtime"`
   - **`_ws_emit_event` non-addressable fixed arrays**: Changed from `EventSnap` by-value to `^EventSnap`; updated call site to `&evts[i]`
   - **`base64.encode` wrong arg order**: Fixed to `base64.encode(digest[:], base64.ENC_TABLE, context.temp_allocator)`
   - **World 787KB stack overflow**: `world := new(World); defer free(world)`
   - **`&world` becomes `^^World`**: Replace_all `&world` → `world` throughout headless.odin (world already `^World`)
   - **replace_all also removed `&` from `world.grid`**: Manually restored `grid_remove_character(&world.grid, c.position[0], c.position[1])`
   - **`grid_remove_character` wrong signature**: Takes `(g: ^Grid, col, row: i32)` not `(id: u32)`. Fixed to use `c.position[0], c.position[1]`
   - **`os.read_entire_file` new API**: Now requires explicit allocator. Fixed → `os.read_entire_file_from_path(path, context.temp_allocator)`, check `err != nil`
   - **`strings.clone` non-addressable**: `strings.split_lines_iterator` needs `&text` where `text` is a variable. Fixed: `text := string(data); for line in strings.split_lines_iterator(&text)`

5. Problem Solving:
   - Build now succeeds: `[odin] Built build\dmls.exe`
   - Process confirmed alive after launch (PID visible via `Get-Process`)
   - HTTP server responding on port 7771 — raw TCP test confirms server routes `GET /api/status` to 404 handler
   - **Ongoing issue**: `GET /api/status` returns `{"error":"not found"}` — routing bug in `api_dispatch`. The `switch { case req.path == "/api/status": }` inside `case .GET:` looks syntactically correct but something is causing the match to fall through to the default 404. This was the last active problem before the summary request.

6. All user messages:
   - "implement API.MD"
   - "setup a minimal html+ajax/alpine frontend"
   - "also use material-ui skills /material-3"
   - "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools." (current summary request)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Fix routing bug**: `GET /api/status` returns 404 despite path matching logic looking correct — needs investigation of `_parse_request` path parsing or the switch structure
   - **Verify dashboard loads**: Once routing is fixed, verify the dashboard HTML serves correctly at `GET /`
   - **Verify WebSocket**: Test WS connection and broadcast loop
   - **Test headless mode**: `dmls.exe --headless` should run without Raylib window
   - **Wire Epilog query forwarding**: `route_query` currently returns empty results with a note — needs sim-thread query channel to be polled each tick and result written back

8. Current Work:
   The last active work was diagnosing a routing bug in `api_dispatch`. The raw TCP test showed:
   ```
   HTTP/1.1 404 OK
   Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
   Content-Length: 21
   {"error":"not found"}
   ```
   for `GET /api/status HTTP/1.1`. The routing switch in `api_routes.odin` (around line 582-612) uses:
   ```odin
   switch req.method {
   case .GET:
       switch {
       case req.path == "/":
           resp = http_ok(DASHBOARD_HTML, "text/html")
       case req.path == "/api/status":
           resp = http_ok(route_status())
       ...
       case:
           resp = http_err(404, `{"error":"not found"}`)
       }
   ```
   The server is alive and responding but path matching is failing — suggesting either `req.path` has extra whitespace/characters, or the `_parse_request` path parsing has a subtle bug.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Investigate the `_parse_request` path parsing bug. The path from `GET /api/status HTTP/1.1` is split by `strings.fields` which returns `["GET", "/api/status", "HTTP/1.1"]` — `parts[1]` should be `/api/status`. Check if `strings.clone(..., context.temp_allocator)` in `_parse_request` is causing the string to not match due to temp allocator being freed between the parse and the dispatch. The `_conn_thread` calls `free_all(context.temp_allocator)` at start with `defer free_all(context.temp_allocator)` — but `_parse_request` also uses `context.temp_allocator` for `strings.clone(full_path)`. The `defer` in `_conn_thread` runs after the handler returns, so the path string should still be valid when `api_dispatch` is called. The actual fix may be to inspect what `req.path` contains at dispatch time by adding a temporary debug print.
