content.text:
Good — the trim was already correct from `strings.fields` splitting on `\r`. But the real candidate is `_parse_request` receiving the line with `\r` still embedded. Let me look at exactly what `strings.split_lines` does — it splits on `\n`, leaving `\r` at the end of each line. Then `strings.fields(lines[0])` splits on any whitespace, which should handle `\r` in `HTTP/1.1\r` but `parts[1]` = `/api/status` should be clean.

The trim I just added is redundant but harmless. Let me look at `api_routes.odin` to see the actual `api_dispatch` code:

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * api_routes.odin — HTTP route handlers and WebSocket message processing.
3	 *
4	 * All handlers read from g_shared (snapshot) under mutex. They never touch
5	 * SimState, Residents, or the Epilog engine directly.
6	 */
7	
8	package dmls
9	
10	import "core:net"
11	import "core:fmt"
12	import "core:strings"
13	import "core:strconv"
14	import "core:sync"
15	
16	// ---- JSON helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
17	
18	_js_str :: proc(b: ^strings.Builder, key, val: string) {
19	    fmt.sbprintf(b, `"%s":"%s"`, key, val)
20	}
21	
22	_js_int :: proc(b: ^strings.Builder, key: string, val: int) {
23	    fmt.sbprintf(b, `"%s":%d`, key, val)
24	}
25	
26	_js_u64 :: proc(b: ^strings.Builder, key: string, val: u64) {
27	    fmt.sbprintf(b, `"%s":%d`, key, val)
28	}
29	
30	_js_bool :: proc(b: ^strings.Builder, key: string, val: bool) {
31	    v := val ? "true" : "false"
32	    fmt.sbprintf(b, `"%s":%s`, key, v)
33	}
34	
35	_snap_cstr :: proc(buf: []u8) -> string {
36	    return strings.truncate_to_byte(string(buf), 0)
37	}
38	
39	// ---- /api/status -----------------------------------------------------------
40	
41	route_status :: proc() -> string {
42	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
43	    b    := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
44	
45	    strings.write_string(&b, "{")
46	    _js_u64(&b, "tick", snap.tick); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
47	    _js_str(&b, "island_name", _snap_cstr(snap.island_name[:]) ); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
48	    fmt.sbprintf(&b, `"seed":"%016X"`, snap.seed); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
49	    _js_int(&b, "speed", int(snap.speed)); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
50	    _js_str(&b, "weather", _snap_cstr(snap.weather[:])); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
51	    _js_str(&b, "time_of_day", _snap_cstr(snap.time_of_day[:])); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
52	    _js_bool(&b, "is_headless", snap.is_headless); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
53	    _js_int(&b, "residents", snap.resident_count); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
54	    _js_int(&b, "crushes", snap.crush_count); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
55	    _js_int(&b, "rivals", snap.rival_count)
56	    strings.write_string(&b, "}")
57	
58	    return strings.to_string(b)
59	}
60	
61	// ---- /api/residents --------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	_resident_json :: proc(rs: ^ResidentSnap, b: ^strings.Builder) {
64	    strings.write_string(b, "{")
65	    _js_int(b, "id", int(rs.id)); strings.write_string(b, ",")
66	    _js_str(b, "name", _snap_cstr(rs.name[:])); strings.write_string(b, ",")
67	    _js_str(b, "mood", _snap_cstr(rs.mood[:])); strings.write_string(b, ",")
68	    _js_str(b, "personality", _snap_cstr(rs.personality[:])); strings.write_string(b, ",")
69	    _js_str(b, "gender", _snap_cstr(rs.gender[:])); strings.write_string(b, ",")
70	    fmt.sbprintf(b, `"position":[%d,%d]`, rs.position[0], rs.position[1])
71	    strings.write_string(b, `,"relationships":[`)
72	    for ri in 0..<rs.rel_count {
73	        if ri > 0 { strings.write_string(b, ",") }
74	        r := &rs.rels[ri]
75	        fmt.sbprintf(b, `{"to":"%s","kind":"%s"}`,
76	            _snap_cstr(r.to[:]), _snap_cstr(r.kind[:]))
77	    }
78	    strings.write_string(b, `],"interaction_counts":{`)
79	    for ri in 0..<rs.rel_count {
80	        if ri > 0 { strings.write_string(b, ",") }
81	        r := &rs.rels[ri]
82	        fmt.sbprintf(b, `"%s":%d`, _snap_cstr(r.to[:]), rs.interact_count)
83	    }
84	    strings.write_string(b, "}}")
85	}
86	
87	route_residents :: proc() -> string {
88	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
89	    b    := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
90	
91	    strings.write_string(&b, `{"residents":[`)
92	    for i in 0..<snap.resident_count {
93	        if i > 0 { strings.write_string(&b, ",") }
94	        _resident_json(&snap.residents[i], &b)
95	    }
96	    strings.write_string(&b, "]}")
97	    return strings.to_string(b)
98	}
99	
100	route_resident_by_name :: proc(name: string) -> (string, int) {
101	    snap   := shared_read_snapshot()
102	    name_l := strings.to_lower(name, context.temp_allocator)
103	
104	    for i in 0..<snap.resident_count {
105	        rs := &snap.residents[i]
106	        rn := strings.to_lower(_snap_cstr(rs.name[:]), context.temp_allocator)
107	        if rn == name_l {
108	            b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
109	            _resident_json(rs, &b)
110	            return strings.to_string(b), 200
111	        }
112	    }
113	    b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
114	    fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"error":"resident not found","name":"%s"}`, name)
115	    return strings.to_string(b), 404
116	}
117	
118	// ---- /api/relationships ----------------------------------------------------
119	
120	route_relationships :: proc() -> string {
121	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
122	    b    := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
123	    first := true
124	
125	    strings.write_string(&b, `{"relationships":[`)
126	    for i in 0..<snap.resident_count {
127	        rs := &snap.residents[i]
128	        for ri in 0..<rs.rel_count {
129	            r := &rs.rels[ri]
130	            if !first { strings.write_string(&b, ",") }
131	            first = false
132	            fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"from":"%s","to":"%s","kind":"%s"}`,
133	                _snap_cstr(rs.name[:]), _snap_cstr(r.to[:]), _snap_cstr(r.kind[:]))
134	        }
135	    }
136	    strings.write_string(&b, "]}")
137	    return strings.to_string(b)
138	}
139	
140	// ---- /api/rivals -----------------------------------------------------------
141	
142	route_rivals :: proc() -> string {
143	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
144	    b    := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
145	
146	    // Find rival pairs from relationship data
147	    strings.write_string(&b, `{"rivals":[`)
148	    first := true
149	    for i in 0..<snap.resident_count {
150	        rs := &snap.residents[i]
151	        for ri in 0..<rs.rel_count {
152	            r := &rs.rels[ri]
153	            if _snap_cstr(r.kind[:]) == "rivals" {
154	                if !first { strings.write_string(&b, ",") }
155	                first = false
156	                fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"a":"%s","b":"%s","duration_ticks":0}`,
157	                    _snap_cstr(rs.name[:]), _snap_cstr(r.to[:]))
158	            }
159	        }
160	    }
161	    strings.write_string(&b, "]}")
162	    return strings.to_string(b)
163	}
164	
165	// ---- /api/events -----------------------------------------------------------
166	
167	route_events :: proc(query: string) -> string {
168	    snap  := shared_read_snapshot()
169	    b     := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
170	    limit := 20
171	    kind_filter := ""
172	
173	    // Parse ?limit=N&kind=K
174	    for part in strings.split(query, "&", context.temp_allocator) {
175	        kv := strings.split(part, "=", context.temp_allocator)
176	        if len(kv) != 2 do continue
177	        switch kv[0] {
178	        case "limit":
179	            if n, ok := strconv.parse_int(kv[1]); ok { limit = n }
180	        case "kind":
181	            kind_filter = kv[1]
182	        }
183	    }
184	
185	    strings.write_string(&b, `{"events":[`)
186	    count := 0
187	    first := true
188	    for i in 0..<snap.event_count {
189	        if count >= limit do break
190	        e := &snap.events[i]
191	        kind := _snap_cstr(e.kind[:])
192	        if kind_filter != "" && kind != kind_filter do continue
193	        desc := _snap_cstr(e.description[:])
194	        if !first { strings.write_string(&b, ",") }
195	        first = false
196	        fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"tick":%d,"kind":"%s","description":"%s","characters":[]}`,
197	            e.tick, kind, _json_escape(desc, context.temp_allocator))
198	        count += 1
199	    }
200	    strings.write_string(&b, "]}")
201	    return strings.to_string(b)
202	}
203	
204	_json_escape :: proc(s: string, alloc := context.allocator) -> string {
205	    b := strings.builder_make(alloc)
206	    for c in s {
207	        switch c {
208	        case '"':  strings.write_string(&b, `\"`)
209	        case '\\': strings.write_string(&b, `\\`)
210	        case '\n': strings.write_string(&b, `\n`)
211	        case '\r': strings.write_string(&b, `\r`)
212	        case:      strings.write_rune(&b, c)
213	        }
214	    }
215	    return strings.to_string(b)
216	}
217	
218	// ---- /api/control/speed ---------------------------------------------------
219	
220	_speed_index_from_val :: proc(v: int) -> (int, bool) {
221	    switch v {
222	    case 0:  return 0, true
223	    case 1:  return 1, true
224	    case 4:  return 2, true
225	    case 16: return 3, true
226	    }
227	    return 0, false
228	}
229	
230	route_control_speed :: proc(body: string) -> (string, int) {
231	    // Parse {"speed": N}
232	    speed_val, ok := _json_get_int(body, "speed")
233	    if !ok {
234	        return `{"error":"missing speed field"}`, 400
235	    }
236	
237	    prev_snap := shared_read_snapshot()
238	    prev := int(prev_snap.speed)
239	
240	    idx, valid := _speed_index_from_val(speed_val)
241	    if !valid {
242	        b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
243	        fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"error":"invalid speed: %d"}`, speed_val)
244	        return strings.to_string(b), 400
245	    }
246	
247	    shared_set_speed_req(idx)
248	
249	    b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
250	    fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"speed":%d,"previous":%d}`, speed_val, prev)
251	    return strings.to_string(b), 200
252	}
253	
254	route_control_pause :: proc() -> string {
255	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
256	    paused := snap.speed == 0
257	    if paused {
258	        shared_set_speed_req(1)
259	    } else {
260	        shared_set_speed_req(0)
261	    }
262	    b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
263	    fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"paused":%s,"tick":%d}`, !paused ? "true" : "false", snap.tick)
264	    return strings.to_string(b)
265	}
266	
267	route_control_save :: proc() -> string {
268	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
269	    shared_set_save()
270	    iname := _snap_cstr(snap.island_name[:])
271	    path := "dmls_save.bin"
272	    if len(iname) > 0 {
273	        path_buf: [64]u8
274	        p := fmt.bprintf(path_buf[:], "%s.dmls", iname)
275	        path = strings.clone(string(path_buf[:len(p)]), context.temp_allocator)
276	    }
277	    b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
278	    fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"saved":true,"path":"%s","tick":%d}`, path, snap.tick)
279	    return strings.to_string(b)
280	}
281	
282	route_control_quit :: proc() -> string {
283	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
284	    shared_set_quit()
285	    b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
286	    fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"quitting":true,"final_tick":%d}`, snap.tick)
287	    return strings.to_string(b)
288	}
289	
290	// ---- /api/query (Epilog read-only proxy) ----------------------------------
291	// The query must be forwarded to the sim thread, which owns the engine.
292	// We use a simple request/response slot in g_shared.
293	
294	EpilogQueryReq :: struct {
295	    goal   : [256]u8,
296	    goal_len : int,
297	    pending  : bool,
298	    done     : bool,
299	    result   : [4096]u8,
300	    result_len : int,
301	}
302	
303	// We expose a single-slot query channel; concurrent queries are serialised.
304	g_eq_mu  : ^_EqState
305	_EqState :: struct {
306	    mu      : _sync_mu,
307	    req     : EpilogQueryReq,
308	}
309	
310	// minimal mutex alias to avoid import cycle
311	_sync_mu :: struct { _: [8]u8 } // placeholder — we reuse sync.Mutex via g_shared
312	
313	// Because we can't block the API thread indefinitely, we use a polling approach:
314	// API thread writes goal into g_shared, sim thread picks it up each tick.
315	
316	shared_eq_req   : EpilogQueryReq
317	shared_eq_mu    : _DummyMu  // we re-use the snapshot mutex
318	
319	_DummyMu :: struct{}
320	
321	// Actually we'll piggyback on the snapshot mutex for the query channel.
322	
323	shared_set_query :: proc(goal: string) {
324	    sync.lock(&g_shared.mu)
325	    n := min(len(goal), 255)
326	    copy(shared_eq_req.goal[:], goal[:n])
327	    shared_eq_req.goal[n]  = 0
328	    shared_eq_req.goal_len = n
329	    shared_eq_req.pending  = true
330	    shared_eq_req.done     = false
331	    sync.unlock(&g_shared.mu)
332	}
333	
334	shared_poll_query :: proc() -> (goal: string, pending: bool) {
335	    sync.lock(&g_shared.mu)
336	    defer sync.unlock(&g_shared.mu)
337	    if !shared_eq_req.pending do return "", false
338	    shared_eq_req.pending = false
339	    g := strings.truncate_to_byte(string(shared_eq_req.goal[:]), 0)
340	    return strings.clone(g, context.temp_allocator), true
341	}
342	
343	shared_put_query_result :: proc(result: string) {
344	    sync.lock(&g_shared.mu)
345	    n := min(len(result), 4095)
346	    copy(shared_eq_req.result[:], result[:n])
347	    shared_eq_req.result[n]    = 0
348	    shared_eq_req.result_len   = n
349	    shared_eq_req.done         = true
350	    sync.unlock(&g_shared.mu)
351	}
352	
353	shared_get_query_result :: proc() -> (result: string, done: bool) {
354	    sync.lock(&g_shared.mu)
355	    defer sync.unlock(&g_shared.mu)
356	    if !shared_eq_req.done do return "", false
357	    shared_eq_req.done = false
358	    r := strings.truncate_to_byte(string(shared_eq_req.result[:]), 0)
359	    return strings.clone(r, context.temp_allocator), true
360	}
361	
362	// Forbidden patterns for read-only query gate
363	_query_forbidden :: proc(goal: string) -> bool {
364	    gl := strings.to_lower(goal, context.temp_allocator)
365	    return strings.contains(gl, "assert") || strings.contains(gl, "retract")
366	}
367	
368	route_query :: proc(body: string) -> (string, int) {
369	    goal, ok := _json_get_str(body, "goal")
370	    if !ok {
371	        return `{"error":"missing goal field"}`, 400
372	    }
373	    if _query_forbidden(goal) {
374	        return `{"error":"assert and retract are not permitted via the query API"}`, 403
375	    }
376	    return fmt.tprintf(`{"goal":"%s","results":[],"count":0,"note":"query forwarding not yet wired in this build"}`,
377	        _json_escape(goal, context.temp_allocator)), 200
378	}
379	
380	// ---- /api/residents POST (add) / DELETE ------------------------------------
381	
382	route_add_resident_json :: proc(body: string) -> (string, int) {
383	    // Parse name — minimal JSON key extraction
384	    name, ok := _json_get_str(body, "name")
385	    if !ok || len(name) == 0 {
386	        return `{"error":"name required"}`, 400
387	    }
388	    // Signal sim thread to add a resident.  For now we write to a small shared
389	    // channel; the sim thread processes it next tick.
390	    shared_request_add_resident(name, body)
391	    return fmt.tprintf(`{"spawned":true,"name":"%s"}`, _json_escape(name, context.temp_allocator)), 202
392	}
393	
394	route_remove_resident :: proc(name: string) -> (string, int) {
395	    if len(name) == 0 {
396	        return `{"error":"name required"}`, 400
397	    }
398	    shared_request_remove_resident(name)
399	    b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
400	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
401	    fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"removed":"%s","at_tick":%d}`, name, snap.tick)
402	    return strings.to_string(b), 200
403	}
404	
405	// Resident add/remove request channel
406	ResidentReq :: struct {
407	    kind : u8,    // 0=add 1=remove
408	    name : [MAX_SNAP_NAME]u8,
409	    body : [512]u8,
410	}
411	
412	g_res_req : ResidentReq
413	g_res_req_pending : bool
414	g_res_req_mu      : bool  // spin via shared mu
415	
416	shared_request_add_resident :: proc(name, body: string) {
417	    sync.lock(&g_shared.mu)
418	    g_res_req.kind = 0
419	    _snap_str(g_res_req.name[:], name)
420	    n := min(len(body), 511)
421	    copy(g_res_req.body[:], body[:n])
422	    g_res_req.body[n] = 0
423	    g_res_req_pending = true
424	    sync.unlock(&g_shared.mu)
425	}
426	
427	shared_request_remove_resident :: proc(name: string) {
428	    sync.lock(&g_shared.mu)
429	    g_res_req.kind = 1
430	    _snap_str(g_res_req.name[:], name)
431	    g_res_req_pending = true
432	    sync.unlock(&g_shared.mu)
433	}
434	
435	shared_poll_res_req :: proc() -> (kind: u8, name: string, body: string, pending: bool) {
436	    sync.lock(&g_shared.mu)
437	    defer sync.unlock(&g_shared.mu)
438	    if !g_res_req_pending do return 0, "", "", false
439	    g_res_req_pending = false
440	    name = strings.clone(strings.truncate_to_byte(string(g_res_req.name[:]), 0), context.temp_allocator)
441	    body = strings.clone(strings.truncate_to_byte(string(g_res_req.body[:]), 0), context.temp_allocator)
442	    return g_res_req.kind, name, body, true
443	}
444	
445	// ---- WebSocket message handler (called per connected client frame) ----------
446	
447	ws_handle_message :: proc(msg: string, sock: net.TCP_Socket) {
448	    // Expect JSON with "type" field
449	    typ, ok := _json_get_str(msg, "type")
450	    if !ok do return
451	
452	    switch typ {
453	    case "set_speed":
454	        speed_val, sok := _json_get_int(msg, "speed")
455	        if !sok {
456	            ws_send_sock(sock, `{"type":"error","message":"missing speed"}`)
457	            return
458	        }
459	        idx, valid := _speed_index_from_val(speed_val)
460	        if !valid {
461	            b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
462	            fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"type":"error","message":"unknown speed value: %d","original_command":%s}`,
463	                speed_val, msg)
464	            ws_send_sock(sock, strings.to_string(b))
465	            return
466	        }
467	        shared_set_speed_req(idx)
468	
469	    case "pause":
470	        shared_set_speed_req(0)
471	
472	    case "unpause":
473	        shared_set_speed_req(1)
474	
475	    case "resolve_prompt":
476	        pid, _ := _json_get_str(msg, "prompt_id")
477	        choice_str, _ := _json_get_str(msg, "choice")
478	        choice := 0
479	        if choice_str == "accept" { choice = 1 }
480	        else if choice_str == "reject" { choice = 2 }
481	        if !shared_resolve_prompt(pid, choice) {
482	            ws_send_sock(sock, `{"type":"error","message":"no matching prompt"}`)
483	        }
484	
485	    case "query":
486	        goal, gok := _json_get_str(msg, "goal")
487	        if !gok {
488	            ws_send_sock(sock, `{"type":"error","message":"missing goal"}`)
489	            return
490	        }
491	        if _query_forbidden(goal) {
492	            ws_send_sock(sock, `{"type":"error","message":"assert/retract forbidden"}`)
493	            return
494	        }
495	        // Return empty result immediately (same limitation as REST /api/query)
496	        b := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
497	        fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"type":"query_result","goal":"%s","results":[],"count":0}`,
498	            _json_escape(goal, context.temp_allocator))
499	        ws_send_sock(sock, strings.to_string(b))
500	
501	    case "add_resident":
502	        name, _ := _json_get_str(msg, "name")
503	        if len(name) > 0 {
504	            shared_request_add_resident(name, msg)
505	        }
506	    }
507	}
508	
509	// Send a text frame to a single WS connection
510	ws_send_sock :: proc(sock: net.TCP_Socket, msg: string) {
511	    frame := ws_make_frame(msg)
512	    defer delete(frame)
513	    net.send_tcp(sock, frame)
514	}
515	
516	// ---- Minimal JSON value extractors -----------------------------------------
517	
518	_json_get_str :: proc(json: string, key: string) -> (string, bool) {
519	    pat := strings.concatenate({`"`, key, `"`}, context.temp_allocator)
520	    i := strings.index(json, pat)
521	    if i < 0 do return "", false
522	    rest := json[i + len(pat):]
523	    ci := strings.index(rest, ":")
524	    if ci < 0 do return "", false
525	    rest = strings.trim_space(rest[ci+1:])
526	    if len(rest) == 0 || rest[0] != '"' do return "", false
527	    rest = rest[1:]
528	    end := strings.index(rest, `"`)
529	    if end < 0 do return "", false
530	    return rest[:end], true
531	}
532	
533	_json_get_int :: proc(json: string, key: string) -> (int, bool) {
534	    pat := strings.concatenate({`"`, key, `"`}, context.temp_allocator)
535	    i := strings.index(json, pat)
536	    if i < 0 do return 0, false
537	    rest := json[i + len(pat):]
538	    ci := strings.index(rest, ":")
539	    if ci < 0 do return 0, false
540	    rest = strings.trim_space(rest[ci+1:])
541	    // Read digits
542	    end := 0
543	    for end < len(rest) && (rest[end] >= '0' && rest[end] <= '9') { end += 1 }
544	    if end == 0 do return 0, false
545	    v, ok := strconv.parse_int(rest[:end])
546	    return v, ok
547	}
548	
549	// ---- Main HTTP dispatcher --------------------------------------------------
550	
551	DASHBOARD_HTML :: #load("dashboard.html", string)
552	
553	api_dispatch :: proc(req: HttpRequest, sock: net.TCP_Socket) {
554	    free_all(context.temp_allocator)
555	    defer free_all(context.temp_allocator)
556	
557	    // WebSocket upgrade
558	    if req.is_ws_upgrade && req.path == "/ws" {
559	        if !ws_handshake(sock, req.ws_key) do return
560	        ws_hub_add(sock)
561	
562	        // Send initial snapshot
563	        snap_json := _build_snapshot_json()
564	        ws_send_sock(sock, snap_json)
565	
566	        // Read loop
567	        for {
568	            msg, closed := ws_read_frame(sock)
569	            if closed { break }
570	            if len(msg) > 0 {
571	                ws_handle_message(msg, sock)
572	            }
573	        }
574	        ws_hub_remove(sock)
575	        return
576	    }
577	
578	    // REST routes
579	    resp := HttpResponse{}
580	    switch req.method {
581	    case .GET:
582	        switch {
583	        case req.path == "/":
584	            resp = http_ok(DASHBOARD_HTML, "text/html")
585	
586	        case req.path == "/api/status":
587	            resp = http_ok(route_status())
588	
589	        case req.path == "/api/residents":
590	            resp = http_ok(route_residents())
591	
592	        case strings.has_prefix(req.path, "/api/residents/"):
593	            name := req.path[len("/api/residents/"):]
594	            body, code := route_resident_by_name(name)
595	            resp = HttpResponse{status = code, content = body, ctype = "application/json"}
596	
597	        case req.path == "/api/relationships":
598	            resp = http_ok(route_relationships())
599	
600	        case req.path == "/api/rivals":
601	            resp = http_ok(route_rivals())
602	
603	        case req.path == "/api/events":
604	            resp = http_ok(route_events(req.query))
605	
606	        case req.path == "/api/query":
607	            body, code := route_query(req.body)
608	            resp = HttpResponse{status = code, content = body, ctype = "application/json"}
609	
610	        case:
611	            resp = http_err(404, `{"error":"not found"}`)
612	        }
613	
614	    case .POST:
615	        switch req.path {
616	        case "/api/control/speed":
617	            body, code := route_control_speed(req.body)
618	            resp = HttpResponse{status = code, content = body, ctype = "application/json"}
619	        case "/api/control/pause":
620	            resp = http_ok(route_control_pause())
621	        case "/api/control/save":
622	            resp = http_ok(route_control_save())
623	        case "/api/control/quit":
624	            resp = http_ok(route_control_quit())
625	        case "/api/residents":
626	            body, code := route_add_resident_json(req.body)
627	            resp = HttpResponse{status = code, content = body, ctype = "application/json"}
628	        case "/api/query":
629	            body, code := route_query(req.body)
630	            resp = HttpResponse{status = code, content = body, ctype = "application/json"}
631	        case:
632	            resp = http_err(404, `{"error":"not found"}`)
633	        }
634	
635	    case .DELETE:
636	        if strings.has_prefix(req.path, "/api/residents/") {
637	            name := req.path[len("/api/residents/"):]
638	            body, code := route_remove_resident(name)
639	            resp = HttpResponse{status = code, content = body, ctype = "application/json"}
640	        } else {
641	            resp = http_err(404, `{"error":"not found"}`)
642	        }
643	
644	    case .UNKNOWN:
645	        resp = http_err(405, `{"error":"method not allowed"}`)
646	    }
647	
648	    http_send(sock, resp)
649	}
650	
651	// Build a WS "snapshot" message
652	_build_snapshot_json :: proc() -> string {
653	    snap := shared_read_snapshot()
654	    b    := strings.builder_make(context.temp_allocator)
655	
656	    strings.write_string(&b, `{"type":"snapshot",`)
657	    _js_u64(&b, "tick", snap.tick); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
658	    _js_str(&b, "island_name", _snap_cstr(snap.island_name[:])); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
659	    fmt.sbprintf(&b, `"seed":"%016X"`, snap.seed); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
660	    _js_int(&b, "crushes", snap.crush_count); strings.write_string(&b, ",")
661	    _js_int(&b, "rivals", snap.rival_count); strings.write_string(&b, `,"residents":[`)
662	    for i in 0..<snap.resident_count {
663	        if i > 0 { strings.write_string(&b, ",") }
664	        _resident_json(&snap.residents[i], &b)
665	    }
666	    strings.write_string(&b, `],"events":[`)
667	    for i in 0..<min(snap.event_count, 20) {
668	        if i > 0 { strings.write_string(&b, ",") }
669	        e := &snap.events[i]
670	        fmt.sbprintf(&b, `{"tick":%d,"kind":"%s","description":"%s"}`,
671	            e.tick, _snap_cstr(e.kind[:]),
672	            _json_escape(_snap_cstr(e.description[:]), context.temp_allocator))
673	    }
674	    strings.write_string(&b, "]}")
675	    return strings.to_string(b)
676	}
677

content.text:
The routing code looks correct. Notice `api_dispatch` calls `free_all(context.temp_allocator)` on the very first line — before the routing switch. This means any string data that was allocated on the temp allocator during `_parse_request` is now freed, including `req.path`! The `req.path` slice now points to freed memory, which may appear to contain garbage and fail the `==` comparison.

The fix: don't free the temp allocator at the top of `api_dispatch`. The defer at the end is sufficient — free only at the end, not at the start. The initial `free_all` was meant to flush any leftover temp data from prior work, but the path was just parsed and lives on `context.temp_allocator`.

file.content:
@echo off
setlocal enabledelayedexpansion

:: ============================================================================
:: DMLS build script (Windows)
::
:: Usage:
::   build.bat            -- build engine DLL + Odin binary (release)
::   build.bat debug      -- build engine DLL + Odin binary (debug)
::   build.bat engine     -- build Epilog DLL only
::   build.bat run        -- build then run
::   build.bat clean      -- remove build artifacts
:: ============================================================================

set MODE=release
set ACTION=all

for %%a in (%*) do (
    if /i "%%a"=="debug"  set MODE=debug
    if /i "%%a"=="engine" set ACTION=engine
    if /i "%%a"=="run"    set ACTION=run
    if /i "%%a"=="clean"  set ACTION=clean
)

:: ---- Clean -----------------------------------------------------------------

if /i "!ACTION!"=="clean" (
    echo [clean] Removing build artifacts...
    if exist build rmdir /s /q build
    if exist engine\epilog\epilog.dll del /q engine\epilog\epilog.dll
    echo [clean] Done.
    goto :eof
)

:: ---- Build Epilog DLL ------------------------------------------------------

if not exist build mkdir build

set EPILOG_DIR=engine\epilog
set EPILOG_DLL=engine\epilog\epilog.dll

if not exist "!EPILOG_DLL!" (
    set ACTION_ENGINE=1
) else (
    set ACTION_ENGINE=0
)

if /i "!ACTION!"=="engine" set ACTION_ENGINE=1
if /i "!ACTION!"=="all"    set ACTION_ENGINE=1
if /i "!ACTION!"=="run"    set ACTION_ENGINE=1

if "!ACTION_ENGINE!"=="1" (
    echo [engine] Building Epilog DLL...
    set CXX=clang++
    set CXXFLAGS=-std=c++23 -O2 -DNDEBUG -I!EPILOG_DIR!\include -target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

    if "!MODE!"=="debug" (
        set CXXFLAGS=-std=c++23 -g -O0 -DDEBUG -I!EPILOG_DIR!\include -target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
    )

    set EPILOG_SRCS=
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\internals\*.cpp) do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\parser\*.cpp)    do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"
    for %%f in (!EPILOG_DIR!\engine\*.cpp)    do set EPILOG_SRCS=!EPILOG_SRCS! "%%f"

    :: -Wl,/DEF generates epilog.lib (import library) alongside the DLL
    !CXX! !CXXFLAGS! -shared -Wl,/DEF:!EPILOG_DIR!\epilog.def !EPILOG_SRCS! -o "!EPILOG_DLL!"
    if !errorlevel! neq 0 (
        echo [engine] ERROR: Epilog DLL build failed.
        exit /b 1
    )
    echo [engine] Built !EPILOG_DLL! + epilog.lib
)

if /i "!ACTION!"=="engine" goto :eof

:: ---- Build Odin binary -----------------------------------------------------

echo [odin] Building DMLS...

set ODIN_FLAGS=-collection:dmls=src
set ODIN_OUT=build\dmls.exe

if "!MODE!"=="debug" (
    set ODIN_FLAGS=!ODIN_FLAGS! -debug
)

:: Copy DLLs next to the binary so Windows finds them at runtime
copy /y "!EPILOG_DLL!" build\ >nul

:: Locate raylib.dll from the odin compiler's own directory
for /f "tokens=*" %%i in ('where odin 2^>nul') do set ODIN_BIN=%%i
for %%i in ("!ODIN_BIN!") do set ODIN_DIR=%%~dpi
set RAYLIB_SRC=!ODIN_DIR!vendor\raylib\windows\raylib.dll
if exist "!RAYLIB_SRC!" (
    copy /y "!RAYLIB_SRC!" build\ >nul
) else (
    echo [warn] raylib.dll not found at !RAYLIB_SRC! -- ensure it is in build\
)

odin build src -out:!ODIN_OUT! !ODIN_FLAGS! -define:RAYLIB_SHARED=true
if !errorlevel! neq 0 (
    echo [odin] ERROR: Odin build failed.
    exit /b 1
)

echo [odin] Built !ODIN_OUT!

:: ---- Run -------------------------------------------------------------------

if /i "!ACTION!"=="run" (
    echo [run] Starting DMLS...
    !ODIN_OUT!
)

endlocal

content.text:
TCP connects (port 7771 is listening) but the server doesn't send a response. This suggests the `_conn_thread` is blocked somewhere — most likely in the `net.recv_tcp` read loop waiting for more data. The issue: the read loop in `_conn_thread` breaks when it finds `\r\n\r\n` but our HTTP request from PowerShell included that terminator. However, the loop continues reading more data even after finding the double CRLF if more bytes come in.

Let me look at the read loop logic more carefully:

Looking at `_conn_thread` in api_http.odin (lines 311-321):
```odin
for {
    n, err := net.recv_tcp(ctx.sock, buf[total:])
    if err != nil || n == 0 do break
    total += n
    if bytes.contains(buf[:total], []u8{'\r','\n','\r','\n'}) do break
    if total >= len(buf) do break
}
```

This looks correct — it breaks when `\r\n\r\n` is found. So the read loop should exit fine.

Wait — the CPU being 0.0625 is suspicious. The previous run (the user ran `build run`) launched the game with a Raylib window. But after the window is closed or if it exits, the process is gone. Let me check: maybe the game is running fine but the API thread is blocked waiting in `net.recv_tcp` for `\r\n\r\n` because the test from before (the background nc command) sent data but the connection hung.

Actually the real issue might be simpler: `_conn_thread` is the handler, but let me check if the issue is the `\r\n\r\n` detection. The PowerShell test sent `\r\n\r\n` as part of the HTTP request — `bytes.contains` should find it.

Let me trace what happens: TCP connects → `net.accept_tcp` → new `ConnCtx` allocated → `thread.create_and_start_with_data` → `_conn_thread` runs → `net.recv_tcp` blocks.

The problem might be that `net.recv_tcp` on Windows with a blocking socket doesn't return until data arrives, but the socket write from PowerShell may have been buffered. Or more likely: the game process is NOT the one listening — maybe the previous nc background task is intercepting connections.

Let me kill the background nc and check the ports:

content.text:
The server IS listening (`0.0.0.0:7771` in Listen state) and has many connections in `Established` and `CloseWait` state. The established connections that never get a response are leaking — those are from our test attempts. The `CloseWait` ones sent the request but the server never closed its side.

The bug is somewhere in `_conn_thread` — it's reading the request but never sending a response. Let me check: after the read loop, `_parse_request` is called, then `ctx.handler(req, ctx.sock)`. If `_parse_request` returns `ok=false`, we return early without sending anything. Our fix of removing `free_all` at the start of `api_dispatch` is correct. But wait — is `_parse_request` failing?

The `_parse_request` requires finding `\r\n\r\n` in the body. Let me double-check the PowerShell test sent proper CRLF. In the PowerShell snippet I constructed, I used `` `r`n `` (backtick-r, backtick-n). But the connection timed out on `Read` — which means the socket was established but no data was received back. If `_parse_request` returned `ok=false`, `_conn_thread` would just `return` without sending anything, leaving the socket in `CloseWait` from the server's `defer net.close`.

Actually if the server closes but the client is still waiting to read, the client gets EOF — not a timeout. A read timeout means the server is still holding the connection open and not closing it either. 

Looking at the leak of `Established` connections: those are connections where both sides are still open — the client is waiting for a response and the server is blocked inside the handler. This confirms `_conn_thread` is hanging inside `ctx.handler(req, ctx.sock)` — i.e., inside `api_dispatch`.

What in `api_dispatch` could block? The `ws_read_frame` loop for WebSocket, but that's only triggered if `req.is_ws_upgrade` is true. For a plain HTTP GET, it should route, call `route_status()`, and call `http_send`. 

The culprit might be `shared_read_snapshot()` — it locks `g_shared.mu`. If the main game thread is holding that lock for a long time, the API thread blocks. Let me check `snapshot_fill` in the main loop — it calls `epilog_query` while holding... wait, no, `snapshot

/*
 * main.odin — DMLS entry point (M0–M8, complete).
 *
 * Screen state machine:
 *   .Resolution   — resolution picker (first screen)
 *   .Title        — animated title card + New/Load buttons
 *   .World_Setup  — hex seed entry, generate island
 *   .Residents    — resident list + "Add resident"
 *   .Creation     — character creation form
 *   .Rel_Seeding  — relationship tags for new character
 *   .Simulation   — 80/20 split: grid left, sidebar right
 *
 * Layout in Simulation:
 *   Left  sim_tex  (80% of SW): grid, animated char dots, hover arrows
 *   Right stat_tex (20% of SW): island stats, residents, events, navigate
 *   Dividing line drawn at stats_x().
 *
 * Save / Load (M7): F5 = save, F9 = load; auto-save every 60 ticks.
 * Cutscene overlay (M8): triggered by new social events from SimState.
 *
 * Navigation:
 *   ESC on any screen = quit.
 *   Back buttons on World_Setup, Residents, Rel_Seeding, Simulation.
 */

package dmls

import rl  "vendor:raylib"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:strings"

SAVE_PATH :: "dmls_save.bin"

// ---- Screen enum -----------------------------------------------------------

AppScreen :: enum {
    Resolution,
    Title,
    World_Setup,
    Residents,
    Creation,
    Rel_Seeding,
    Simulation,
}

// ---- App state -------------------------------------------------------------

App :: struct {
    screen        : AppScreen,

    engine        : ^EpilogEngine,

    world         : World,
    residents     : Residents,
    sim           : SimState,

    island_name   : IslandName,

    creation      : CreationScreen,
    rel_seeding   : RelSeeding,
    resident_list : ResidentListScreen,
    title         : TitleScreen,
    res_picker    : ResolutionPicker,
    cutscene      : CutsceneState,

    pending_id    : u32,

    cam_col       : i32,
    cam_row       : i32,
    hovered_id    : u32,

    seed_field    : InputField,
    seed_err      : bool,

    sim_timer     : f32,
    stat_timer    : f32,
    anim_time     : f32,

    sim_speed     : int,    // 0=paused 1=1x 2=4x 3=16x

main :: proc() {
    // Parse config + CLI flags first — headless mode exits here
    cfg := parse_config()

    // Init shared control slot
    g_shared.snap = SimSnapshot{}
    g_shared.speed_req = -1

    // Start API thread (always, even in headed mode)
    api_thread_start(cfg.port)

    if cfg.headless {
        headless_run(&cfg)
        return
    }

    rl.SetConfigFlags({.WINDOW_RESIZABLE})
    g_screen.w = SCREEN_W
    g_screen.h = SCREEN_H
    rl.InitWindow(SCREEN_W, SCREEN_H, "DMLS — Deb's Minimal Life Simulator")
    rl.SetTargetFPS(60)
    defer rl.CloseWindow()

    app := new(App)
    defer free(app)

    app.engine = epilog_create()
    defer epilog_destroy(app.engine)

The only thing between `api_thread_start` and `rl.InitWindow` is the headless check — and since `cfg.headless` is false (no `--headless` flag), it should proceed to `rl.InitWindow`.

content.text:
DLLs are present. The process is sitting alive (not exiting) but not showing a window. Given the API thread starts and prints its message, the code IS running past `api_thread_start`. Let me add a debug print right before `rl.InitWindow` to confirm it's being reached — but actually the cleaner diagnosis is: the process is alive and WS is only 7MB, which means Raylib's OpenGL context never initialized (Raylib would allocate much more memory once a window and GL context open).

The most likely cause on Windows: `rl.InitWindow` is being called but the window appears and immediately gets hidden, or there's a display driver issue when launched from a subprocess. But when `build run` was used earlier, it worked. The difference is `build run` runs `dmls.exe` from the same console session.

The actual fix: launch it in a new console window that stays visible:

content.text:
Still 7MB WS — no Raylib window. The process is alive but stuck. Let me look at what's between `api_thread_start` and `rl.InitWindow` more carefully. The API threads are blocking threads — `thread.create_and_start_with_data` spawns threads. But that shouldn't block main.

Actually wait — `http_server_run` calls `net.listen_tcp` then loops on `net.accept_tcp`. That's being run inside a spawned thread, so it shouldn't block main. But `_api_broadcast_loop` is also a thread. Neither should block.

Let me check `rl.InitWindow` itself — on Windows, Raylib requires being called from the main thread and needs a proper GUI context. When launched via `Start-Process`, it might silently fail. Let me look at what `parse_config` does — specifically `_read_cfg_file`:

Looking at headless.odin line 59: `defer free_all(context.temp_allocator)` inside `_read_cfg_file` — this frees memory mid-function, which is fine. But then line 57: `os.read_entire_file_from_path(path, context.temp_allocator)` — if the cfg file doesn't exist, it returns early.

Actually the real issue: `_apply_cli_flags` — with no CLI args it does nothing. `parse_config` returns correctly with `headless=false`.

Let me check if there's a `dmls.cfg` in the working directory that might set `headless=true`:

content.text: The code is correct. The problem must be in `api_thread_start` — specifically `thread.destroy(http_t)` called immediately after `thread.create_and_start_with_data`. In Odin, `thread.destroy` on a running thread may block waiting for it to finish, which would deadlock since `http_server_run` runs forever. Let me check:

content.content:
143	
144	api_thread_start :: proc(port: int) {
145	    g_api_server.port    = port
146	    g_api_server.running = true
147	
148	    // HTTP listener thread
149	    http_t := thread.create_and_start_with_data(nil, proc(data: rawptr) {
150	        http_server_run(g_api_server.port, api_dispatch)
151	    })
152	    thread.destroy(http_t)
153	
154	    // Broadcast loop thread
155	    bc_t := thread.create_and_start_with_data(nil, _api_broadcast_loop)
156	    thread.destroy(bc_t)
157	
158	    fmt.println("[api] Dashboard: http://localhost:", port)
159	}

content.text:
That's the deadlock. `thread.destroy` in Odin joins the thread (waits for it to finish). Since `http_server_run` loops forever on `net.accept_tcp`, `main()` blocks at `thread.destroy(http_t)` and never reaches `rl.InitWindow`.

The fix: use `thread.create_and_start_with_data` without calling `thread.destroy` — just let the threads run detached. In Odin, you can set the thread to auto-destroy itself:

content.text: Also fix the same pattern in `api_http.odin` for per-connection threads — those should also be detached, not destroyed (they exit naturally after handling the request, but `thread.destroy` would still join):

input.old_string:
ctx := new(ConnCtx)
        ctx.sock    = client
        ctx.handler = handler
        t := thread.create_and_start_with_data(ctx, _conn_thread)
        thread.destroy(t)
input.new_string:
ctx := new(ConnCtx)
        ctx.sock    = client
        ctx.handler = handler
        t := thread.create_and_start_with_data(ctx, _conn_thread)
        t.init_context = context
        thread.detach(t)

content.text: Odin's thread package doesn't have `detach`. The correct approach is to set `thread.Thread.self_cleanup = true` so it frees itself when done, and just not call `destroy` at all:

content.content: The file d:\DMLS\src\api_server.odin has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -    // HTTP listener thread (detached — runs for lifetime of process)
+    // HTTP listener thread — self_cleanup so we don't need to join/destroy

input.old_string:
ctx := new(ConnCtx)
        ctx.sock    = client
        ctx.handler = handler
        t := thread.create_and_start_with_data(ctx, _conn_thread)
        t.init_context = context
        thread.detach(t)
input.new_string:
ctx := new(ConnCtx)
        ctx.sock    = client
        ctx.handler = handler
